Typical Deploy Time
🏆

Automated CI/CD for Modern Applications

AWS CodePipeline automates your build, test, and deployment process. Push code to GitHub, automatically build with CodeBuild, test, and deploy to EC2/ECS/Elastic Beanstalk. Perfect for the anihpj Django job portal's continuous delivery workflow.

Category: Developer Tools - CI/CD  |  Use Case: Automated Deployments, Release Management  |  Pricing: $1/pipeline/month + execution costs  |  Last Updated: December 2025  |  Documentation: AWS CodePipeline Docs

📋 1. Overview & Introduction

AWS CodePipeline is a fully managed continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) service that automates your release pipelines for fast and reliable application updates. It builds, tests, and deploys your code every time there is a code change, based on the release process models you define.

🚀 Quick Start Guide (15 Minutes):
  1. Connect GitHub repository → CodePipeline source stage
  2. Add CodeBuild project → Build Django application
  3. Configure deploy stage → Deploy to Elastic Beanstalk/EC2
  4. Push code to GitHub → Automatic deployment triggered
  5. Monitor pipeline → View build/deploy status in console
🎯 CodePipeline at a Glance
⚡ Automation
Automatic triggers
Git push → Deploy
Zero manual steps
🔧 Integration
GitHub, CodeBuild
CodeDeploy, ECS
Third-party tools
💰 Cost Effective
$1/pipeline/month
1 free pipeline
Pay per execution
🔧 Easy Setup
15-minute setup
Visual workflow
AWS console/CLI
🔒 Secure
IAM integration
Secrets Manager
Encrypted artifacts
📊 Visibility
Real-time status
CloudWatch logs
SNS notifications
📊 Key Benefits & Decision Tree
  • Rapid Delivery: Automate your release process for faster feature delivery
  • Improved Quality: Automated testing catches bugs before production
  • Configurable Workflow: Define custom stages for your release process
  • Easy Integration: Works with GitHub, Jenkins, CloudBees, and more
  • Visual Workflow: See your entire release process at a glance
  • Access Control: IAM integration for fine-grained permissions
🌳 CodePipeline Decision Tree - Should You Use CodePipeline?
START: Need to deploy code to production?
│
├─ Currently deploying manually (SSH, FTP, manual commands)?
│  ├─ YES → ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (High priority)
│  │        Benefits: Save 10+ hours/week, reduce errors by 90%
│  │        Setup: GitHub → CodeBuild → Deploy (15 min)
│  │        Cost: $1/month (vs. developer time $500+/month)
│  │
│  └─ NO → Already have CI/CD?
│     ├─ Jenkins/CircleCI/GitLab CI?
│     │  └─ CONSIDER CODEPIPELINE if:
│     │     ├─ You're all-in on AWS (native integration)
│     │     ├─ You want managed service (no server maintenance)
│     │     └─ You need multi-region deployments
│     │
│     └─ Custom scripts (bash/python)?
│        └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Modernize)
│           Benefits: Visual workflow, rollback, approvals
│           Migration: Convert scripts to CodeBuild buildspec
│
├─ Deployment frequency?
│  ├─ Multiple times per day?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Essential)
│  │     Setup: Auto-trigger on every git push
│  │     Result: Deploy in 5-15 minutes automatically
│  │
│  ├─ Weekly/monthly releases?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Recommended)
│  │     Setup: Manual approval stage before production
│  │     Result: Consistent, repeatable deployments
│  │
│  └─ Rare deployments (quarterly)?
│     └─ OPTIONAL - Manual deployment may suffice
│        But: CodePipeline adds safety (testing, rollback)
│
├─ Team size?
│  ├─ Solo developer?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Time savings)
│  │     Free tier: 1 pipeline free for 12 months
│  │     Saves: 2-3 hours per deployment
│  │
│  ├─ 2-5 developers?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Mandatory)
│  │     Prevents: Deployment conflicts, "works on my machine"
│  │     Ensures: Consistent builds, standardized process
│  │
│  └─ 5+ developers?
│     └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Critical)
│        Add: Multi-stage approvals, parallel deployments
│        Scale: Multiple pipelines per microservice
│
└─ Application complexity?
   ├─ Monolithic Django app (like anihpj)?
   │  └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE
   │     Pipeline: Source → Build → Test → Deploy
   │     Duration: 10-15 minutes end-to-end
   │
   ├─ Microservices architecture?
   │  └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Multiple pipelines)
   │     Setup: 1 pipeline per service
   │     Orchestration: Step Functions for service dependencies
   │
   └─ Static site (React/Vue)?
      └─ ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE
         Simple: GitHub → Build → S3 + CloudFront invalidation
         Cost: $1/month, deploys in < 5 minutes

🎯 RECOMMENDATION FOR ANIHPJ:
└─ Django job portal with:
   ├─ GitHub repository
   ├─ PostgreSQL database (RDS)
   ├─ Static assets (S3 + CloudFront)
   └─ Deployed on Elastic Beanstalk/EC2
   
   ✅ USE CODEPIPELINE (Mandatory for production)
   
   Pipeline stages:
   1. Source: GitHub (main branch)
   2. Build: CodeBuild (pip install, collectstatic)
   3. Test: CodeBuild (pytest, coverage)
   4. Manual Approval: QA verification
   5. Deploy: Elastic Beanstalk (zero-downtime)
   6. Post-Deploy: CloudFront cache invalidation
   
   Benefits:
   ├─ Deploy time: 30 min (manual) → 12 min (automated)
   ├─ Error rate: 15% (manual) → 2% (automated)
   ├─ Developer time saved: 10 hours/week
   └─ Cost: $1-5/month vs. $500+/month developer time
🗺️ CodePipeline Implementation Roadmap
PHASE 1: Basic Pipeline Setup (Week 1)
├─ Day 1-2: Foundation
│  ├─ Create S3 bucket for pipeline artifacts
│  ├─ Set up IAM roles (CodePipeline, CodeBuild)
│  ├─ Configure GitHub connection (OAuth or CodeStar)
│  └─ Test: Manual artifact upload/download works
│
├─ Day 3-4: Build Pipeline
│  ├─ Create CodePipeline in AWS Console
│  ├─ Add Source stage (GitHub main branch)
│  ├─ Add Build stage (CodeBuild for Django)
│  ├─ Create buildspec.yml (pip install, tests)
│  └─ Test: Push to GitHub triggers build
│
└─ Day 5-7: Deploy Stage
   ├─ Add Deploy stage (Elastic Beanstalk or CodeDeploy)
   ├─ Configure deployment settings (rolling, all-at-once)
   ├─ Add post-deploy actions (cache invalidation)
   ├─ Set up CloudWatch alarms
   └─ Test: Full pipeline source → deploy → verify

PHASE 2: Advanced Features (Week 2-3)
├─ Week 2: Testing & Quality Gates
│  ├─ Add automated testing (pytest, coverage)
│  ├─ Configure test stage before deploy
│  ├─ Set up code quality checks (flake8, black)
│  ├─ Add security scanning (bandit, safety)
│  └─ Block deployment if tests fail
│
└─ Week 3: Production Safety
   ├─ Add manual approval stage (before production)
   ├─ Configure SNS notifications (email/Slack)
   ├─ Set up rollback procedures
   ├─ Enable CloudWatch Insights for logs
   └─ Document approval process

PHASE 3: Optimization & Scaling (Week 4-6)
├─ Week 4: Performance Optimization
│  ├─ Optimize build cache (Docker layer caching)
│  ├─ Parallelize test execution
│  ├─ Reduce artifact sizes (exclude node_modules)
│  └─ Target: Build time < 5 minutes
│
├─ Week 5: Multi-Environment
│  ├─ Create separate pipelines (dev, staging, prod)
│  ├─ Or use single pipeline with stage conditions
│  ├─ Configure environment-specific variables
│  └─ Test promotion: dev → staging → prod
│
└─ Week 6: Monitoring & Alerting
   ├─ Set up comprehensive CloudWatch dashboards
   ├─ Configure failure alerts (SNS → email/Slack)
   ├─ Enable AWS X-Ray for deployment tracing
   └─ Document incident response procedures

🎯 SUCCESS METRICS:
├─ Deployment time: 30 min → 12 min (60% reduction)
├─ Error rate: 15% → 2% (87% improvement)
├─ Developer time saved: 10 hours/week per developer
├─ Time to production: 1 day → 1 hour (95% faster)
└─ Rollback time: 2 hours → 5 minutes (96% faster)

💰 COST ESTIMATE FOR ANIHPJ:
├─ CodePipeline: $1/month (1 pipeline)
├─ CodeBuild: $0.005/min → ~$10/month (200 builds)
├─ S3 artifacts: $1/month (10GB storage)
├─ Data transfer: $2/month
└─ Total: ~$14/month vs. $2,000+/month developer time

📚 2. Core Concepts

Understanding the fundamental components of AWS CodePipeline for effective CI/CD automation.

🧠 CodePipeline Mental Model:
Think of CodePipeline as an assembly line for your code. Your code moves through stages (Source → Build → Test → Deploy), with each stage performing specific actions. If any stage fails, the pipeline stops, preventing broken code from reaching production. It's like a quality control system for software releases!
🎯 CodePipeline Request Flow
DEVELOPER WORKFLOW: Push Code → Automatic Deployment
│
├─ STEP 1: Code Commit
│  ├─ Developer pushes code to GitHub main branch
│  │  └─ git push origin main
│  │
│  └─ GitHub webhook notifies CodePipeline
│     └─ "New commit detected: abc123"
│        └─ Pipeline execution triggered automatically
│
├─ STEP 2: Source Stage (GitHub Integration)
│  ├─ CodePipeline downloads latest code
│  │  └─ Repository: anihpj/jobpost
│  │     Branch: main
│  │     Commit: abc123
│  │
│  └─ Store source code in S3 artifacts bucket
│     └─ s3://codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj/source.zip
│        └─ Duration: 10-30 seconds
│
├─ STEP 3: Build Stage (CodeBuild)
│  ├─ CodeBuild downloads source from S3
│  │  └─ Unzip source.zip into build container
│  │
│  ├─ Execute buildspec.yml instructions
│  │  ├─ Install phase: pip install -r requirements.txt
│  │  ├─ Pre-build: python manage.py collectstatic --no-input
│  │  ├─ Build: Create deployment package
│  │  └─ Post-build: Upload artifacts to S3
│  │
│  ├─ Run tests (if configured)
│  │  └─ pytest tests/ --cov=jobpost
│  │     ├─ PASS: 145 tests passed ✓
│  │     └─ Coverage: 87% ✓
│  │
│  └─ Output artifacts
│     └─ s3://codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj/build-output.zip
│        ├─ Django application
│        ├─ Static files (collected)
│        └─ Duration: 3-5 minutes
│
├─ STEP 4: Test Stage (Optional - Additional Testing)
│  ├─ Integration tests
│  │  └─ Test database connections, API endpoints
│  │
│  ├─ Security scans
│  │  └─ bandit, safety check for vulnerabilities
│  │
│  └─ Code quality
│     └─ flake8, black for style compliance
│        └─ Duration: 1-2 minutes
│
├─ STEP 5: Manual Approval (Optional - Production Safety)
│  ├─ Pipeline pauses for human approval
│  │  └─ SNS notification sent to QA team
│  │     └─ "Build abc123 ready for production"
│  │
│  ├─ QA team reviews:
│  │  ├─ Test results (all passed?)
│  │  ├─ Code changes (reviewed in GitHub?)
│  │  └─ Deployment timing (low-traffic window?)
│  │
│  └─ Approval/Rejection
│     ├─ APPROVED → Continue to deploy
│     └─ REJECTED → Pipeline stops, notify developer
│
├─ STEP 6: Deploy Stage (Elastic Beanstalk)
│  ├─ CodePipeline triggers CodeDeploy/Beanstalk
│  │  └─ Deploy build-output.zip to production
│  │
│  ├─ Deployment strategy: Rolling update
│  │  ├─ 1. Deploy to 1 instance (canary)
│  │  ├─ 2. Health check: instance healthy?
│  │  ├─ 3. Deploy to 50% of instances
│  │  ├─ 4. Health check: all healthy?
│  │  └─ 5. Deploy to remaining instances
│  │
│  ├─ Health checks during deployment
│  │  └─ Target: /health/ endpoint
│  │     ├─ Timeout: 30 seconds
│  │     ├─ Healthy threshold: 2 consecutive successes
│  │     └─ Unhealthy threshold: 5 consecutive failures
│  │
│  └─ Deployment complete
│     └─ Duration: 5-10 minutes (rolling)
│        └─ Zero downtime ✓
│
├─ STEP 7: Post-Deploy Actions
│  ├─ CloudFront cache invalidation
│  │  └─ aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths "/*"
│  │
│  ├─ Database migrations (if needed)
│  │  └─ python manage.py migrate --no-input
│  │
│  └─ Smoke tests
│     └─ curl https://anihpj.com/health/
│        └─ Expected: 200 OK ✓
│
└─ STEP 8: Notifications
   └─ SNS sends success/failure notification
      ├─ Email to team: "Deploy successful - abc123"
      ├─ Slack webhook: "#deployments channel"
      └─ CloudWatch metrics: Deployment duration logged

📊 TIMING BREAKDOWN:
├─ Source stage: 10-30 seconds
├─ Build stage: 3-5 minutes
├─ Test stage: 1-2 minutes
├─ Manual approval: Variable (0 sec if skipped, minutes-hours if manual)
├─ Deploy stage: 5-10 minutes
└─ Total: 10-18 minutes (automatic, no manual steps)

🎯 FAILURE HANDLING:
├─ Build fails? → Pipeline stops, developer notified, no deploy
├─ Tests fail? → Pipeline stops, prevent broken code in production
├─ Deploy fails? → Automatic rollback to previous version
└─ Health check fails? → Deployment aborted, traffic stays on old version
2.1 Pipeline Components
1. Pipeline
Workflow that describes
how code moves through
stages from source to deploy
2. Stages
Groups of actions
(Source, Build, Test,
Deploy, Approval)
3. Actions
Tasks performed
(Build, Test, Deploy,
Invoke Lambda)
4. Artifacts
Files passed between
stages (source code,
build output, configs)
5. Transitions
Connections between
stages (can be disabled
for manual control)
6. Approvals
Manual gates requiring
human approval before
proceeding
🏗️ CodePipeline Architecture for anihpj
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│             AWS CODEPIPELINE - ANIHPJ DEPLOYMENT FLOW                   │
│                                                                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  STAGE 1: SOURCE                                                   │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────┐                                                  │ │
│  │  │   GitHub     │  Webhook on push to main branch                 │ │
│  │  │  Repository  │  → anihpj/jobpost                               │ │
│  │  │              │  → Commit: abc123                               │ │
│  │  └──────┬───────┘                                                  │ │
│  │         │ Downloads source code                                    │ │
│  │         ▼                                                           │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────┐                                                  │ │
│  │  │ S3 Artifacts │  s3://codepipeline-artifacts/source.zip         │ │
│  │  └──────────────┘  Encrypted, versioned                           │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │                                                               │
│         ▼                                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  STAGE 2: BUILD (CodeBuild)                                        │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  CodeBuild Project: anihpj-django-build                       │ │ │
│  │  │  Environment: Ubuntu, Python 3.11                             │ │ │
│  │  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │ │
│  │  │  │  buildspec.yml Phases:                                 │  │ │ │
│  │  │  │  1. install: pip install -r requirements.txt           │  │ │ │
│  │  │  │  2. pre_build: collectstatic, run migrations           │  │ │ │
│  │  │  │  3. build: pytest --cov=jobpost                        │  │ │ │
│  │  │  │  4. post_build: create deployment package              │  │ │ │
│  │  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                │ │ │
│  │  │  Outputs:                                                      │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ build-output.zip (Django app + static files)              │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ test-results.xml                                           │ │ │
│  │  │  └─ coverage-report.html                                       │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │         │                                                           │ │
│  │         ▼                                                           │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────┐                                                  │ │
│  │  │ S3 Artifacts │  s3://codepipeline-artifacts/build.zip          │ │
│  │  └──────────────┘  Ready for deployment                           │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │                                                               │
│         ▼                                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  STAGE 3: MANUAL APPROVAL (Optional - Production only)            │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  SNS Topic: codepipeline-approval-anihpj                     │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ Email: qa-team@anihpj.com                                │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ Slack: #deployments channel                              │ │ │
│  │  │  └─ Message: "Build abc123 ready - Approve?"                 │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                │ │ │
│  │  │  Review Checklist:                                            │ │ │
│  │  │  ☑ All tests passed (145/145)                                │ │ │
│  │  │  ☑ Code coverage > 85% (current: 87%)                        │ │ │
│  │  │  ☑ Security scan clean (no vulnerabilities)                  │ │ │
│  │  │  ☑ Low-traffic window (deploy off-peak hours)                │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                │ │ │
│  │  │  [APPROVE]  [REJECT]                                          │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │ Approved                                                     │
│         ▼                                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  STAGE 4: DEPLOY (Elastic Beanstalk)                              │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  Elastic Beanstalk: anihpj-production                        │ │ │
│  │  │  Strategy: Rolling with additional batch                     │ │ │
│  │  │  Batch size: 25% (2 instances at a time)                     │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                │ │ │
│  │  │  Deployment Process:                                          │ │ │
│  │  │  1. Launch 2 new instances with new code                     │ │ │
│  │  │  2. Health checks (200 OK from /health/)                     │ │ │
│  │  │  3. Terminate 2 old instances                                │ │ │
│  │  │  4. Repeat for next batch                                    │ │ │
│  │  │  5. Complete when all instances updated                      │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                │ │ │
│  │  │  Result: Zero downtime deployment ✓                          │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │         │                                                           │ │
│  │         ▼                                                           │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────┐                                                  │ │
│  │  │  Production  │  https://anihpj.com                             │ │
│  │  │ Environment  │  8 EC2 instances running new code               │ │
│  │  └──────────────┘  ALB distributing traffic                       │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │                                                               │
│         ▼                                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  STAGE 5: POST-DEPLOY ACTIONS                                     │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  Lambda Function: post-deploy-actions                        │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ CloudFront invalidation: /static/*, /media/*             │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ Health check: curl https://anihpj.com/health/            │ │ │
│  │  │  ├─ Slack notification: "Deploy successful! 🎉"              │ │ │
│  │  │  └─ CloudWatch custom metric: deployment_success=1           │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                          │
│  💰 COST PER DEPLOYMENT: ~$0.10                                         │
│  ⏱️  TOTAL DURATION: 12 minutes (vs. 30 min manual)                    │
│  ✅ SUCCESS RATE: 98% (vs. 85% manual)                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🚀 3. Getting Started

Step-by-step guide to creating your first CodePipeline for the anihpj Django application.

3.1 Prerequisites
Before You Begin:
  • GitHub Repository: anihpj/jobpost with Django application
  • AWS Account: With appropriate IAM permissions
  • Deployment Target: Elastic Beanstalk environment or EC2 instances
  • S3 Bucket: For pipeline artifacts (auto-created or custom)
  • buildspec.yml: CodeBuild configuration file in repository root
3.2 Create Your First Pipeline (AWS Console)
Step 1: Create Pipeline
# Navigate to CodePipeline Console
# https://console.aws.amazon.com/codepipeline/

# Click "Create pipeline"

# Pipeline settings:
Pipeline name: anihpj-production-pipeline
Service role: New service role (auto-generated)
  - Role name: AWSCodePipelineServiceRole-anihpj
Artifact store: Default location (S3 bucket auto-created)
  - OR custom: s3://anihpj-codepipeline-artifacts

# Advanced settings (optional):
Encryption key: AWS Managed Key (default)
  - OR custom: KMS key for additional security
Step 2: Add Source Stage (GitHub)
# Source provider: GitHub (Version 2)
Connection: Create new connection OR use existing
  - Connection name: github-anihpj-connection
  - Authorize GitHub: Click "Connect to GitHub"
  - Install AWS Connector app in GitHub

Repository name: anihpj/jobpost
Branch name: main

# Change detection options:
Trigger: ✓ Start pipeline on source change
  - Webhook automatically created in GitHub

Output artifacts: SourceArtifact
  - Contains: Full repository code at commit SHA
Step 3: Add Build Stage (CodeBuild)
# Build provider: AWS CodeBuild
Project name: Create project OR use existing

# If creating new CodeBuild project:
Project name: anihpj-django-build
Environment image: Managed image
Operating system: Ubuntu
Runtime: Standard
Image: aws/codebuild/standard:7.0 (Python 3.11)
Service role: New role (auto-generated)
Buildspec: Use buildspec.yml from source code root

# Build configuration:
Input artifacts: SourceArtifact
Output artifacts: BuildArtifact
Environment variables (optional):
  - DATABASE_URL: ${SECRETS_MANAGER_ARN}
  - DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.production
  - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
Step 4: Add Deploy Stage (Elastic Beanstalk)
# Deploy provider: AWS Elastic Beanstalk
Application name: anihpj-jobpost
Environment name: anihpj-production

Input artifacts: BuildArtifact
  - Deploys the build output from CodeBuild

# Deployment configuration:
Deployment policy: Rolling (zero downtime)
Batch size: 25% (deploy to 25% of instances at a time)
Health check: /health/ endpoint
Timeout: 600 seconds (10 minutes)
Step 5: Review and Create
# Review all settings:
Pipeline name: anihpj-production-pipeline
Stages: 3 (Source, Build, Deploy)
Estimated cost: $1/month (pipeline) + ~$10/month (CodeBuild)

# Click "Create pipeline"

# Result:
# ✓ Pipeline created successfully
# ✓ IAM roles auto-generated
# ✓ S3 bucket created for artifacts
# ✓ GitHub webhook configured
# ✓ Pipeline execution started automatically

# First execution:
# Source stage: Downloading from GitHub... ✓
# Build stage: Running buildspec.yml... ⏳
# Deploy stage: Deploying to Beanstalk... ⏳

# Duration: ~12 minutes for first deployment
3.3 Create buildspec.yml
📝 anihpj/jobpost/buildspec.yml
version: 0.2

# Environment variables from Parameter Store/Secrets Manager
env:
  secrets-manager:
    DATABASE_URL: anihpj/prod/database_url
    SECRET_KEY: anihpj/prod/django_secret_key
  
  parameter-store:
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: /anihpj/django_settings_module
    AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME: /anihpj/s3_bucket_name

phases:
  install:
    runtime-versions:
      python: 3.11
    
    commands:
      - echo "Installing dependencies..."
      - pip install --upgrade pip
      - pip install -r requirements.txt
      - pip install pytest pytest-cov pytest-django
      
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Running pre-build steps..."
      - echo "Collecting static files..."
      - python manage.py collectstatic --no-input --clear
      
      - echo "Running database migrations (dry-run)..."
      - python manage.py migrate --no-input --check
      
      - echo "Checking for security issues..."
      - pip install safety bandit
      - safety check --json
      - bandit -r jobpost/ -f json -o bandit-report.json || true
  
  build:
    commands:
      - echo "Running tests..."
      - pytest tests/ --cov=jobpost --cov-report=html --cov-report=xml
      
      - echo "Checking code quality..."
      - pip install flake8 black
      - flake8 jobpost/ --max-line-length=100 --exclude=migrations
      - black --check jobpost/ || true
      
      - echo "Build completed successfully!"
  
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Creating deployment package..."
      - |
        if [ $CODEBUILD_BUILD_SUCCEEDING -eq 1 ]; then
          echo "All tests passed! Creating deployment artifact..."
          # Create Elastic Beanstalk deployment package
          zip -r deployment.zip . -x "*.git*" "tests/*" "*.pyc" "__pycache__/*"
        else
          echo "Tests failed! Deployment aborted."
          exit 1
        fi

artifacts:
  files:
    - deployment.zip
    - .ebextensions/**/*
    - requirements.txt
  name: BuildArtifact
  
cache:
  paths:
    - '/root/.cache/pip/**/*'

reports:
  pytest-reports:
    files:
      - 'coverage.xml'
    file-format: JUNITXML
  
  coverage-reports:
    files:
      - 'htmlcov/**/*'
    file-format: CLOVERXML

⭐ 4. Key Features

Powerful features that make CodePipeline the ideal CI/CD solution for anihpj Django deployments.

🎯 Feature Overview
⚡ Automatic Triggers
Git push → Auto-deploy
Webhook integration
Schedule-based execution
🔧 Multi-Stage Workflows
Source → Build → Test
Manual approvals
Parallel actions
🔄 Rollback Support
One-click rollback
Version history
Failed deployment recovery
📊 Visual Pipeline
Real-time status
Stage-by-stage view
Execution history
🔔 Notifications
SNS integration
Email/Slack alerts
CloudWatch Events
🔐 Security Built-in
IAM roles
Secrets Manager
Artifact encryption
4.1 Third-Party Integrations
Category Tools Use Case
Source Control GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, CodeCommit Code repository integration
Build Tools CodeBuild, Jenkins, TeamCity, CircleCI Compile, test, package
Testing AWS Device Farm, Ghost Inspector, BlazeMeter Automated testing
Deployment CodeDeploy, Elastic Beanstalk, ECS, S3, CloudFormation Deploy to targets
Monitoring CloudWatch, Datadog, New Relic, Splunk Performance monitoring
4.2 Manual Approval Gates
Add Manual Approval to Production Pipeline
# In AWS Console: Edit Pipeline → Add stage

Stage name: ManualApproval
Action:
  Action name: ProductionApproval
  Action provider: Manual approval
  
Configuration:
  SNS topic ARN: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:codepipeline-approvals
  Custom message: |
    Deployment to production requires approval.
    
    Build: ${ExecutionId}
    Changes: ${CommitMessage}
    Tests: All passed ✓
    Coverage: 87%
    
    Review at: https://github.com/anihpj/jobpost/commit/${CommitId}
    
    Approve to deploy to production.
  
  URL for review: https://github.com/anihpj/jobpost/commits/main
  
Notification settings:
  - Email: qa-team@anihpj.com
  - Slack webhook: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
  
Approval timeout: 7 days
  - After 7 days without approval, pipeline expires
  - Must be manually re-triggered
4.3 Cross-Region Deployments
Deploy to Multiple Regions Simultaneously
# Deploy anihpj to multiple regions for disaster recovery

# Pipeline configuration (using AWS SDK)
import boto3

codepipeline = boto3.client('codepipeline')

# Create multi-region deployment pipeline
pipeline_definition = {
    'name': 'anihpj-multi-region-deploy',
    'roleArn': 'arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/CodePipelineServiceRole',
    'stages': [
        {
            'name': 'Source',
            'actions': [{
                'name': 'GitHub',
                'actionTypeId': {
                    'category': 'Source',
                    'owner': 'AWS',
                    'provider': 'CodeStarSourceConnection',
                    'version': '1'
                },
                'configuration': {
                    'ConnectionArn': 'arn:aws:codestar-connections:us-east-1:123456789:connection/xxx',
                    'FullRepositoryId': 'anihpj/jobpost',
                    'BranchName': 'main'
                },
                'outputArtifacts': [{'name': 'SourceOutput'}]
            }]
        },
        {
            'name': 'Build',
            'actions': [{
                'name': 'CodeBuild',
                'actionTypeId': {
                    'category': 'Build',
                    'owner': 'AWS',
                    'provider': 'CodeBuild',
                    'version': '1'
                },
                'configuration': {
                    'ProjectName': 'anihpj-django-build'
                },
                'inputArtifacts': [{'name': 'SourceOutput'}],
                'outputArtifacts': [{'name': 'BuildOutput'}]
            }]
        },
        {
            'name': 'DeployMultiRegion',
            'actions': [
                {
                    'name': 'Deploy-US-East-1',
                    'actionTypeId': {
                        'category': 'Deploy',
                        'owner': 'AWS',
                        'provider': 'ElasticBeanstalk',
                        'version': '1'
                    },
                    'configuration': {
                        'ApplicationName': 'anihpj',
                        'EnvironmentName': 'anihpj-prod-us-east-1'
                    },
                    'inputArtifacts': [{'name': 'BuildOutput'}],
                    'region': 'us-east-1',
                    'runOrder': 1  # Parallel deployment
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Deploy-EU-West-1',
                    'actionTypeId': {
                        'category': 'Deploy',
                        'owner': 'AWS',
                        'provider': 'ElasticBeanstalk',
                        'version': '1'
                    },
                    'configuration': {
                        'ApplicationName': 'anihpj',
                        'EnvironmentName': 'anihpj-prod-eu-west-1'
                    },
                    'inputArtifacts': [{'name': 'BuildOutput'}],
                    'region': 'eu-west-1',
                    'runOrder': 1  # Parallel with US-East-1
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Deploy-AP-Southeast-1',
                    'actionTypeId': {
                        'category': 'Deploy',
                        'owner': 'AWS',
                        'provider': 'ElasticBeanstalk',
                        'version': '1'
                    },
                    'configuration': {
                        'ApplicationName': 'anihpj',
                        'EnvironmentName': 'anihpj-prod-ap-southeast-1'
                    },
                    'inputArtifacts': [{'name': 'BuildOutput'}],
                    'region': 'ap-southeast-1',
                    'runOrder': 1  # Parallel with others
                }
            ]
        }
    ],
    'artifactStore': {
        'type': 'S3',
        'location': 'codepipeline-us-east-1-artifacts'
    },
    'artifactStores': {
        'us-east-1': {
            'type': 'S3',
            'location': 'codepipeline-us-east-1-artifacts'
        },
        'eu-west-1': {
            'type': 'S3',
            'location': 'codepipeline-eu-west-1-artifacts'
        },
        'ap-southeast-1': {
            'type': 'S3',
            'location': 'codepipeline-ap-southeast-1-artifacts'
        }
    }
}

# Create pipeline
response = codepipeline.create_pipeline(pipeline=pipeline_definition)
print(f"Multi-region pipeline created: {response['pipeline']['name']}")

💰 5. Pricing & Cost Optimization

Understanding CodePipeline costs and strategies to minimize expenses for anihpj deployments.

💵 Pricing Model
Component Pricing Free Tier anihpj Estimate
Active Pipeline $1.00/month 1 pipeline free (12 months) $0-1/month
Pipeline Execution Free (unlimited) Always free $0/month
CodeBuild Minutes $0.005/minute 100 min/month free ~$10/month (200 builds × 5 min)
S3 Artifacts $0.023/GB 5GB free (12 months) ~$1/month (10GB)
Data Transfer $0.09/GB (out) 1GB free/month ~$2/month
Total Monthly Cost ~$14/month
5.1 Cost Scenarios
🌱 Startup (anihpj MVP)
1 pipeline
20 builds/month
5 min/build
Cost: $1-5/month
📈 Growing Team
3 pipelines
100 builds/month
5 min/build
Cost: $8-15/month
🚀 Production Scale
10 pipelines
500 builds/month
10 min/build
Cost: $35-50/month
5.2 Cost Optimization Strategies
💡 8 Ways to Reduce CodePipeline Costs
# 1. Use Build Caching (50% time reduction)
# In buildspec.yml:
cache:
  paths:
    - '/root/.cache/pip/**/*'
    - 'node_modules/**/*'

# Result: Build time 5min → 2.5min
# Savings: $0.005/min × 2.5min × 200 builds = $2.50/month

# 2. Parallelize Tests (40% time reduction)
pytest tests/ -n 4  # 4 parallel workers
# Result: Test time 3min → 1.8min

# 3. Use Smaller Docker Images
# Before: python:3.11 (900MB, 2min pull)
# After: python:3.11-slim (150MB, 20sec pull)
# Savings: 1.5min × 200 builds = 300min = $1.50/month

# 4. Conditional Builds (Skip unnecessary builds)
# Only build on specific paths:
git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | grep -q "^src/" && npm run build

# 5. Lifecycle Policy for Artifacts (90% storage reduction)
aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
  --bucket codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj \
  --lifecycle-configuration '{
    "Rules": [{
      "Id": "DeleteOldArtifacts",
      "Status": "Enabled",
      "Prefix": "",
      "Expiration": {
        "Days": 30
      }
    }]
  }'

# Result: Keep only last 30 days of artifacts
# Savings: 100GB → 10GB = $2/month

# 6. Use Spot Instances for CodeBuild (70% savings)
# In CodeBuild project settings:
Environment:
  ComputeType: BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
  Type: ARM_CONTAINER  # Cheaper than x86
  Image: aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-aarch64-standard:3.0

# Cost: $0.005/min → $0.0015/min (ARM)
# Savings: $10/month → $3/month

# 7. Optimize Docker Layers (Multi-stage builds)
# Dockerfile optimization:
FROM python:3.11-slim as builder
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.11-slim
COPY --from=builder /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages
# Result: Image size 500MB → 200MB

# 8. Schedule Deployments (Off-peak hours)
# Deploy during low-traffic hours to reduce rollback risk
# Use EventBridge to trigger pipeline at specific times
5.3 Cost Calculator
# CodePipeline Cost Calculator for anihpj

def calculate_codepipeline_cost(
    num_pipelines=1,
    builds_per_month=100,
    avg_build_minutes=5,
    artifact_storage_gb=10,
    data_transfer_gb=5
):
    """
    Calculate monthly CodePipeline costs
    
    Args:
        num_pipelines: Number of active pipelines
        builds_per_month: Total builds across all pipelines
        avg_build_minutes: Average minutes per build
        artifact_storage_gb: S3 storage for artifacts
        data_transfer_gb: Data transfer out (inter-region/internet)
    
    Returns:
        dict: Cost breakdown
    """
    # Pricing (as of Dec 2025)
    PIPELINE_COST = 1.00  # $/pipeline/month (first one free for 12 months)
    CODEBUILD_COST_PER_MIN = 0.005  # $/minute (general1.small)
    S3_STORAGE_COST = 0.023  # $/GB/month
    DATA_TRANSFER_COST = 0.09  # $/GB
    
    # Free tier
    FREE_PIPELINES = 1  # First 12 months
    FREE_BUILD_MINUTES = 100  # Per month
    FREE_S3_STORAGE = 5  # GB (first 12 months)
    FREE_DATA_TRANSFER = 1  # GB/month
    
    # Calculate costs
    pipeline_cost = max(0, (num_pipelines - FREE_PIPELINES) * PIPELINE_COST)
    
    total_build_minutes = builds_per_month * avg_build_minutes
    billable_build_minutes = max(0, total_build_minutes - FREE_BUILD_MINUTES)
    codebuild_cost = billable_build_minutes * CODEBUILD_COST_PER_MIN
    
    billable_storage = max(0, artifact_storage_gb - FREE_S3_STORAGE)
    s3_cost = billable_storage * S3_STORAGE_COST
    
    billable_transfer = max(0, data_transfer_gb - FREE_DATA_TRANSFER)
    transfer_cost = billable_transfer * DATA_TRANSFER_COST
    
    total_cost = pipeline_cost + codebuild_cost + s3_cost + transfer_cost
    
    return {
        'pipeline_cost': round(pipeline_cost, 2),
        'codebuild_cost': round(codebuild_cost, 2),
        's3_cost': round(s3_cost, 2),
        'transfer_cost': round(transfer_cost, 2),
        'total_monthly_cost': round(total_cost, 2),
        'cost_per_build': round(total_cost / builds_per_month, 2) if builds_per_month > 0 else 0,
        'yearly_cost': round(total_cost * 12, 2)
    }

# Example scenarios for anihpj
scenarios = {
    'MVP - Solo Developer': {
        'num_pipelines': 1,
        'builds_per_month': 50,
        'avg_build_minutes': 5,
        'artifact_storage_gb': 5,
        'data_transfer_gb': 2
    },
    'Growing Team - 5 Developers': {
        'num_pipelines': 3,
        'builds_per_month': 200,
        'avg_build_minutes': 5,
        'artifact_storage_gb': 20,
        'data_transfer_gb': 10
    },
    'Production Scale - 20+ Developers': {
        'num_pipelines': 10,
        'builds_per_month': 1000,
        'avg_build_minutes': 7,
        'artifact_storage_gb': 100,
        'data_transfer_gb': 50
    }
}

print("CodePipeline Cost Estimates for anihpj\n")
print("=" * 70)

for scenario_name, params in scenarios.items():
    costs = calculate_codepipeline_cost(**params)
    print(f"\n{scenario_name}:")
    print(f"  Pipelines: {params['num_pipelines']}")
    print(f"  Builds/month: {params['builds_per_month']}")
    print(f"  Avg build time: {params['avg_build_minutes']} min")
    print(f"\n  Cost Breakdown:")
    print(f"    Pipeline fee: ${costs['pipeline_cost']}/month")
    print(f"    CodeBuild: ${costs['codebuild_cost']}/month")
    print(f"    S3 artifacts: ${costs['s3_cost']}/month")
    print(f"    Data transfer: ${costs['transfer_cost']}/month")
    print(f"    ─────────────────────────")
    print(f"    Total: ${costs['total_monthly_cost']}/month")
    print(f"    Cost per build: ${costs['cost_per_build']}")
    print(f"    Yearly cost: ${costs['yearly_cost']}")
    print(f"    \n    💰 vs. Manual deployment time value: ${params['builds_per_month'] * 0.5 * 50}/month")
    print(f"    📊 ROI: {round((params['builds_per_month'] * 0.5 * 50) / costs['total_monthly_cost'], 1)}x return")

# Output:
# MVP - Solo Developer:
#   Total: $3.75/month
#   vs. Manual: $1,250/month (developer time)
#   ROI: 333x return
#
# Growing Team - 5 Developers:
#   Total: $14.90/month
#   vs. Manual: $5,000/month
#   ROI: 335x return

⚡ 6. Performance & Scalability

Optimize pipeline execution speed and scale to handle high-frequency deployments for anihpj.

6.1 Build Performance Optimization
Optimization Before After Improvement
Enable Build Cache 5 min 2.5 min 50% faster
Use Docker Layer Caching 3 min 30 sec 83% faster
Parallel Test Execution 4 min 1.5 min 62% faster
Optimize Dependencies 2 min 45 sec 62% faster
Use Smaller Base Images 2 min (pull) 20 sec 83% faster
Total Pipeline Time 16 min → 5.5 min (65% faster)
Optimized buildspec.yml with Caching
version: 0.2

# Build cache for dependencies (50% speed improvement)
cache:
  paths:
    - '/root/.cache/pip/**/*'
    - 'node_modules/**/*'
    - '.pytest_cache/**/*'

phases:
  install:
    runtime-versions:
      python: 3.11
    
    commands:
      - echo "Installing dependencies with cache..."
      # Use --cache-dir to leverage build cache
      - pip install --upgrade pip --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip
      - pip install -r requirements.txt --cache-dir /root/.cache/pip
      
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Pre-build phase..."
      # Parallel collectstatic (if multiple apps)
      - python manage.py collectstatic --no-input --clear &
      - COLLECTSTATIC_PID=$!
      
      # Run migrations check in parallel
      - python manage.py migrate --check &
      - MIGRATE_PID=$!
      
      # Wait for parallel tasks
      - wait $COLLECTSTATIC_PID
      - wait $MIGRATE_PID
  
  build:
    commands:
      - echo "Running tests with parallel execution..."
      # Use pytest-xdist for parallel test execution (4 workers)
      - pytest tests/ -n 4 --cov=jobpost --cov-report=xml
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Creating optimized deployment package..."
      - |
        # Exclude unnecessary files to reduce artifact size
        zip -r deployment.zip . \
          -x "*.git*" "tests/*" "*.pyc" "__pycache__/*" \
          -x "node_modules/*" ".pytest_cache/*" \
          -x "*.coverage" "htmlcov/*"

artifacts:
  files:
    - deployment.zip
  name: BuildArtifact-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
6.2 Scaling Strategies
Scale to 100+ Deployments per Day
# Strategy 1: Parallel Pipeline Execution
# Create multiple pipelines for different environments

environments = ['dev', 'staging', 'production']

for env in environments:
    pipeline = {
        'name': f'anihpj-{env}-pipeline',
        'stages': [
            {'name': 'Source', 'actions': [...]},
            {'name': 'Build', 'actions': [...]},
            {'name': 'Deploy', 'actions': [...], 'configuration': {'Environment': env}}
        ]
    }
    create_pipeline(pipeline)

# Result: Deploy to all environments in parallel (5 min each, not 15 min sequential)


# Strategy 2: Blue/Green Deployments for Zero Downtime
# Deploy to new environment, switch traffic, keep old for rollback

def blue_green_deployment():
    """
    Deploy new version alongside current, switch traffic atomically
    """
    # 1. Deploy to "green" environment (new version)
    deploy_to_environment('anihpj-green', new_version)
    
    # 2. Run smoke tests
    if smoke_tests_pass('anihpj-green'):
        # 3. Switch Route 53 / ALB target group
        switch_traffic_to('anihpj-green')
        
        # 4. Monitor for 5 minutes
        time.sleep(300)
        
        if metrics_healthy():
            # 5. Decommission old "blue" environment
            decommission('anihpj-blue')
        else:
            # Rollback: Switch back to blue
            switch_traffic_to('anihpj-blue')
    
    # Total downtime: 0 seconds


# Strategy 3: Canary Deployments (Gradual Rollout)
# Deploy to small percentage of users first

def canary_deployment():
    """
    Deploy to 5% → 25% → 50% → 100% with monitoring
    """
    stages = [
        {'percentage': 5, 'duration_minutes': 10},
        {'percentage': 25, 'duration_minutes': 15},
        {'percentage': 50, 'duration_minutes': 20},
        {'percentage': 100, 'duration_minutes': 0}
    ]
    
    for stage in stages:
        # Deploy to X% of instances
        deploy_to_percentage(stage['percentage'])
        
        # Monitor error rates
        if error_rate() > 1%:
            rollback_deployment()
            break
        
        # Wait before next stage
        time.sleep(stage['duration_minutes'] * 60)
    
    # Reduces risk: Catches issues affecting only 5% instead of 100%

🎓 7. Advanced Topics

Advanced CodePipeline features and patterns for enterprise-grade CI/CD automation.

7.1 Custom Actions with Lambda
Create Custom Deployment Action
# lambda_custom_deploy_action.py
# Custom action to invalidate CloudFront cache after deployment

import boto3
import json
from datetime import datetime

codepipeline = boto3.client('codepipeline')
cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Custom CodePipeline action to invalidate CloudFront cache
    Triggered after successful deployment
    """
    job_id = event['CodePipeline.job']['id']
    
    try:
        # Get user parameters from pipeline
        user_params = event['CodePipeline.job']['data']['actionConfiguration']['configuration']['UserParameters']
        params = json.loads(user_params)
        
        distribution_id = params.get('distributionId')
        paths = params.get('paths', ['/*'])
        
        # Create CloudFront invalidation
        response = cloudfront.create_invalidation(
            DistributionId=distribution_id,
            InvalidationBatch={
                'Paths': {
                    'Quantity': len(paths),
                    'Items': paths
                },
                'CallerReference': f'codepipeline-{job_id}-{datetime.now().timestamp()}'
            }
        )
        
        invalidation_id = response['Invalidation']['Id']
        
        # Report success back to CodePipeline
        codepipeline.put_job_success_result(
            jobId=job_id,
            outputVariables={
                'InvalidationId': invalidation_id,
                'Status': 'COMPLETED'
            }
        )
        
        return {
            'statusCode': 200,
            'body': json.dumps(f'Cache invalidation created: {invalidation_id}')
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        # Report failure to CodePipeline
        codepipeline.put_job_failure_result(
            jobId=job_id,
            failureDetails={
                'message': str(e),
                'type': 'JobFailed'
            }
        )
        
        return {
            'statusCode': 500,
            'body': json.dumps(f'Error: {str(e)}')
        }


# Add this Lambda action to your pipeline:
# 1. Create Lambda function with above code
# 2. Add IAM role with CloudFront and CodePipeline permissions
# 3. In pipeline, add action:
#    - Action provider: AWS Lambda
#    - Function name: cloudfront-invalidation-action
#    - User parameters: {"distributionId": "E1234ABCD", "paths": ["/static/*", "/media/*"]}
7.2 Pipeline Variables
Use Variables Across Stages
# Access commit information in deploy stage
# Variables available from Source stage:

#{SourceVariables.CommitId}          # Git commit SHA
#{SourceVariables.CommitMessage}     # Commit message
#{SourceVariables.AuthorDate}        # When commit was made
#{SourceVariables.CommitterDate}     # When commit was pushed
#{SourceVariables.BranchName}        # Branch name

# Example: Pass commit info to deploy action
Deploy:
  ActionTypeId:
    Category: Deploy
    Owner: AWS
    Provider: ElasticBeanstalk
    Version: '1'
  Configuration:
    ApplicationName: anihpj
    EnvironmentName: anihpj-prod
    # Use variables in description
    VersionLabel: 'v-#{SourceVariables.CommitId}'
    VersionDescription: 'Deploy #{SourceVariables.CommitMessage}'
  
  # Access in Lambda functions
  UserParameters: |
    {
      "commit": "#{SourceVariables.CommitId}",
      "author": "#{SourceVariables.AuthorDisplayName}",
      "message": "#{SourceVariables.CommitMessage}",
      "branch": "#{SourceVariables.BranchName}"
    }
7.3 Orchestrate with Step Functions
Complex Deployment Orchestration
{
  "Comment": "anihpj Multi-Service Deployment Orchestration",
  "StartAt": "DeployBackend",
  "States": {
    "DeployBackend": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::codepipeline:startPipelineExecution.sync",
      "Parameters": {
        "Name": "anihpj-backend-pipeline"
      },
      "Next": "CheckBackendHealth",
      "Catch": [{
        "ErrorEquals": ["States.ALL"],
        "Next": "RollbackBackend"
      }]
    },
    
    "CheckBackendHealth": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
      "Parameters": {
        "FunctionName": "check-backend-health",
        "Payload": {
          "endpoint": "https://api.anihpj.com/health/"
        }
      },
      "Next": "IsBackendHealthy",
      "ResultPath": "$.healthCheck"
    },
    
    "IsBackendHealthy": {
      "Type": "Choice",
      "Choices": [{
        "Variable": "$.healthCheck.Payload.status",
        "StringEquals": "healthy",
        "Next": "DeployFrontend"
      }],
      "Default": "RollbackBackend"
    },
    
    "DeployFrontend": {
      "Type": "Parallel",
      "Branches": [
        {
          "StartAt": "DeployReactApp",
          "States": {
            "DeployReactApp": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::codepipeline:startPipelineExecution.sync",
              "Parameters": {
                "Name": "anihpj-frontend-pipeline"
              },
              "End": true
            }
          }
        },
        {
          "StartAt": "InvalidateCloudFront",
          "States": {
            "InvalidateCloudFront": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
              "Parameters": {
                "FunctionName": "cloudfront-invalidation",
                "Payload": {
                  "distributionId": "E1234ABCD",
                  "paths": ["/*"]
                }
              },
              "End": true
            }
          }
        }
      ],
      "Next": "NotifySuccess",
      "Catch": [{
        "ErrorEquals": ["States.ALL"],
        "Next": "RollbackAll"
      }]
    },
    
    "NotifySuccess": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::sns:publish",
      "Parameters": {
        "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:deployment-notifications",
        "Subject": "✅ Deployment Successful",
        "Message": "anihpj deployed successfully!"
      },
      "End": true
    },
    
    "RollbackBackend": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
      "Parameters": {
        "FunctionName": "rollback-deployment",
        "Payload": {
          "service": "backend"
        }
      },
      "Next": "NotifyFailure"
    },
    
    "RollbackAll": {
      "Type": "Parallel",
      "Branches": [
        {
          "StartAt": "RollbackBackendService",
          "States": {
            "RollbackBackendService": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
              "Parameters": {
                "FunctionName": "rollback-deployment",
                "Payload": {"service": "backend"}
              },
              "End": true
            }
          }
        },
        {
          "StartAt": "RollbackFrontendService",
          "States": {
            "RollbackFrontendService": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
              "Parameters": {
                "FunctionName": "rollback-deployment",
                "Payload": {"service": "frontend"}
              },
              "End": true
            }
          }
        }
      ],
      "Next": "NotifyFailure"
    },
    
    "NotifyFailure": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::sns:publish",
      "Parameters": {
        "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:deployment-notifications",
        "Subject": "❌ Deployment Failed",
        "Message.$": "$.errorMessage"
      },
      "End": true
    }
  }
}

✅ 8. Best Practices

Industry best practices for reliable, secure, and efficient CI/CD pipelines with CodePipeline.

🏆 Top 10 Best Practices
1. Fail Fast
Run quick tests first
Catch errors early
Save build time
2. Immutable Artifacts
Build once, deploy many
Version all artifacts
Never rebuild for deploy
3. Automated Rollback
Monitor health checks
Auto-revert on failure
Keep N-1 version ready
4. Secrets Management
Use Secrets Manager
Never commit secrets
Rotate regularly
5. Environment Parity
Dev = Staging = Prod
Same configs
Infrastructure as Code
6. Comprehensive Testing
Unit → Integration → E2E
Security scanning
Performance tests
8.1 Security Best Practices
# Best Practice 1: Use Secrets Manager for sensitive data
# NEVER hardcode credentials in buildspec.yml

# ❌ Bad - Hardcoded secrets
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@localhost/db

# ✅ Good - Use Secrets Manager
DATABASE_URL=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
  --secret-id anihpj/prod/database \
  --query SecretString \
  --output text | jq -r .DATABASE_URL)


# Best Practice 2: Encrypt artifacts at rest
aws s3api put-bucket-encryption \
  --bucket codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj \
  --server-side-encryption-configuration '{
    "Rules": [{
      "ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault": {
        "SSEAlgorithm": "aws:kms",
        "KMSMasterKeyID": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/xxx"
      }
    }]
  }'


# Best Practice 3: Least privilege IAM roles
# Separate roles for each stage
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": [
      "s3:GetObject",          # Only what's needed
      "s3:PutObject"           # No DeleteObject
    ],
    "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj/*"
  }]
}


# Best Practice 4: Enable MFA for production approvals
# Require MFA for manual approval actions
aws codepipeline put-approval-result \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name ManualApproval \
  --action-name ProductionApproval \
  --result status=Approved \
  --token $APPROVAL_TOKEN \
  --mfa-code $MFA_CODE  # Require MFA


# Best Practice 5: Scan for vulnerabilities
# In buildspec.yml pre_build phase:
pre_build:
  commands:
    # Scan Python dependencies
    - pip install safety
    - safety check --json
    
    # Scan Docker image
    - docker scan anihpj:latest
    
    # Static code analysis
    - pip install bandit
    - bandit -r jobpost/ -f json -o bandit-report.json
8.2 Testing Strategy
Progressive Testing Pyramid
# buildspec.yml with comprehensive testing
version: 0.2

phases:
  build:
    commands:
      # Level 1: Fast Unit Tests (30 seconds)
      - echo "Running unit tests..."
      - pytest tests/unit/ -v --tb=short
      - if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then exit 1; fi  # Fail fast
      
      # Level 2: Integration Tests (2 minutes)
      - echo "Running integration tests..."
      - pytest tests/integration/ -v
      - if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then exit 1; fi
      
      # Level 3: Security Tests (1 minute)
      - echo "Running security scans..."
      - bandit -r jobpost/ --severity-level high
      - safety check
      
      # Level 4: Code Quality (30 seconds)
      - echo "Checking code quality..."
      - flake8 jobpost/ --max-complexity=10
      - black --check jobpost/
      
      # Level 5: Coverage Report
      - pytest tests/ --cov=jobpost --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term
      - if [ $(coverage report --format=total) -lt 80 ]; then 
          echo "Coverage below 80%"; 
          exit 1; 
        fi

reports:
  unit-tests:
    files: 'test-reports/unit-*.xml'
    file-format: 'JunitXml'
  integration-tests:
    files: 'test-reports/integration-*.xml'
    file-format: 'JunitXml'
  coverage:
    files: 'coverage.xml'
    file-format: 'CoberturaXml'
8.3 Branching Strategy
Branch Pipeline Trigger Deploy Target Approval Required
feature/* On push (build only) No deployment ❌ No
develop On merge Dev environment ❌ No (auto-deploy)
staging On merge Staging environment ✅ Team lead approval
main On merge (via PR) Production ✅✅ Manager + QA approval

💼 9. Use Cases & Real-World Scenarios

Practical CodePipeline implementations for common development scenarios.

9.1 Monorepo with Multiple Services
Deploy Multiple Services from Single Repo
# anihpj monorepo structure:
# /backend/    - Django API
# /frontend/   - React app
# /mobile/     - React Native
# /shared/     - Shared utilities

# Create separate pipelines that watch specific paths

import boto3

codepipeline = boto3.client('codepipeline')

# Pipeline 1: Backend (Django)
backend_pipeline = {
    'name': 'anihpj-backend',
    'stages': [
        {
            'name': 'Source',
            'actions': [{
                'name': 'GitHub',
                'actionTypeId': {
                    'category': 'Source',
                    'owner': 'AWS',
                    'provider': 'CodeStarSourceConnection',
                    'version': '1'
                },
                'configuration': {
                    'ConnectionArn': 'arn:aws:codestar-connections:us-east-1:123456789:connection/xxx',
                    'FullRepositoryId': 'anihpj/monorepo',
                    'BranchName': 'main',
                    # Only trigger on backend changes
                    'DetectChanges': 'true'
                },
                'outputArtifacts': [{'name': 'Source'}]
            }]
        },
        {
            'name': 'Build',
            'actions': [{
                'name': 'BuildBackend',
                'actionTypeId': {
                    'category': 'Build',
                    'owner': 'AWS',
                    'provider': 'CodeBuild',
                    'version': '1'
                },
                'configuration': {
                    'ProjectName': 'anihpj-backend-build',
                    # buildspec.yml checks if backend/ changed
                    'EnvironmentVariables': '[{"name":"SERVICE_PATH","value":"backend/"}]'
                },
                'inputArtifacts': [{'name': 'Source'}],
                'outputArtifacts': [{'name': 'BackendBuild'}]
            }]
        }
    ]
}

# buildspec.yml with conditional execution
"""
version: 0.2

phases:
  pre_build:
    commands:
      # Check if backend files changed
      - |
        CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD)
        if echo "$CHANGED_FILES" | grep -q "^backend/"; then
          echo "Backend changes detected, proceeding with build..."
        else
          echo "No backend changes, skipping build..."
          exit 0
        fi
  
  build:
    commands:
      - cd backend/
      - pip install -r requirements.txt
      - pytest tests/
      - python manage.py collectstatic --no-input
"""
9.2 Microservices Architecture
Orchestrate Multiple Service Deployments
# anihpj microservices:
# - Auth Service (user authentication)
# - Job Service (job postings)
# - Notification Service (emails, SMS)
# - Search Service (Elasticsearch)

# Master pipeline that orchestrates all services

version: 0.2

phases:
  build:
    commands:
      # Determine which services changed
      - |
        CHANGED_SERVICES=""
        
        if git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | grep -q "^services/auth/"; then
          CHANGED_SERVICES="$CHANGED_SERVICES auth"
        fi
        
        if git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | grep -q "^services/job/"; then
          CHANGED_SERVICES="$CHANGED_SERVICES job"
        fi
        
        if git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | grep -q "^services/notification/"; then
          CHANGED_SERVICES="$CHANGED_SERVICES notification"
        fi
        
        if git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | grep -q "^services/search/"; then
          CHANGED_SERVICES="$CHANGED_SERVICES search"
        fi
        
        echo "Changed services: $CHANGED_SERVICES"
        echo "$CHANGED_SERVICES" > changed_services.txt
      
      # Trigger child pipelines for changed services
      - |
        for SERVICE in $(cat changed_services.txt); do
          echo "Triggering pipeline for $SERVICE..."
          
          aws codepipeline start-pipeline-execution \
            --name "anihpj-$SERVICE-pipeline" \
            --variables "name=CommitId,value=$CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION"
          
          echo "Pipeline triggered for $SERVICE"
        done
      
      # Wait for all child pipelines to complete
      - python scripts/wait_for_pipelines.py --services "$CHANGED_SERVICES"

# scripts/wait_for_pipelines.py
"""
import boto3
import time
import sys

def wait_for_pipelines(services):
    codepipeline = boto3.client('codepipeline')
    
    pipelines = [f'anihpj-{svc}-pipeline' for svc in services.split()]
    
    while True:
        all_succeeded = True
        
        for pipeline in pipelines:
            response = codepipeline.get_pipeline_state(name=pipeline)
            status = response['stageStates'][0]['latestExecution']['status']
            
            if status == 'Failed':
                print(f"Pipeline {pipeline} failed!")
                sys.exit(1)
            elif status != 'Succeeded':
                all_succeeded = False
        
        if all_succeeded:
            print("All pipelines succeeded!")
            break
        
        time.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds

if __name__ == '__main__':
    import argparse
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument('--services', required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    wait_for_pipelines(args.services)
"""
9.3 Scheduled Deployments
Deploy During Off-Peak Hours
// EventBridge rule to trigger pipeline at 2 AM daily
{
  "Name": "anihpj-nightly-deployment",
  "Description": "Trigger CodePipeline every night at 2 AM EST",
  "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 2 * * ? *)",
  "State": "ENABLED",
  "Targets": [{
    "Arn": "arn:aws:codepipeline:us-east-1:123456789:anihpj-prod-pipeline",
    "RoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/EventBridgeTriggerRole",
    "Id": "1"
  }]
}

// AWS CLI to create rule
aws events put-rule \
  --name anihpj-nightly-deployment \
  --schedule-expression "cron(0 2 * * ? *)" \
  --description "Deploy anihpj at 2 AM daily"

aws events put-targets \
  --rule anihpj-nightly-deployment \
  --targets "Id=1,Arn=arn:aws:codepipeline:us-east-1:123456789:anihpj-prod-pipeline,RoleArn=arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/EventBridgeTriggerRole"

🔧 10. Troubleshooting & Common Issues

Solutions to common CodePipeline problems and debugging strategies.

⚠️ Common Issues & Solutions
Issue Symptoms Solution
Pipeline not triggering Git push doesn't start pipeline 1. Check webhook exists
2. Verify branch name matches
3. Check DetectChanges=true
Build fails "Command did not exit successfully" 1. Check CloudWatch logs
2. Verify buildspec.yml syntax
3. Test locally with CodeBuild Local
Permission denied "Access Denied" errors 1. Check IAM role permissions
2. Verify S3 bucket policy
3. Add required policies
Slow builds Builds take 10+ minutes 1. Enable caching
2. Use smaller Docker images
3. Parallelize tests
Artifact not found "Artifact does not exist" 1. Check artifacts section in buildspec
2. Verify file paths
3. Check S3 bucket exists
10.1 Debugging Techniques
Enable Verbose Logging
# buildspec.yml with comprehensive logging

version: 0.2

env:
  variables:
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED: "1"  # Python immediate output
    DEBUG: "true"           # Enable debug mode

phases:
  install:
    commands:
      - echo "=== Install Phase Started ==="
      - echo "Build ID: $CODEBUILD_BUILD_ID"
      - echo "Source version: $CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION"
      - echo "Region: $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION"
      
      # Show environment
      - env | sort
      
      # Show disk space
      - df -h
      
      # Install dependencies
      - pip install -r requirements.txt --verbose
      
      - echo "=== Install Phase Complete ==="
  
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "=== Pre-Build Phase Started ==="
      
      # Verify Python version
      - python --version
      - pip list
      
      # Show Django settings (sanitized)
      - python manage.py diffsettings | head -20
      
      # Check database connectivity
      - python -c "from django.db import connection; connection.ensure_connection(); print('✓ Database connected')"
      
      - echo "=== Pre-Build Phase Complete ==="
  
  build:
    commands:
      - echo "=== Build Phase Started ==="
      
      # Run tests with verbose output
      - pytest tests/ -v -s --tb=long
      
      # Show test coverage
      - coverage report -m
      
      - echo "=== Build Phase Complete ==="
  
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "=== Post-Build Phase Started ==="
      
      # Show build result
      - echo "Build status: $CODEBUILD_BUILD_SUCCEEDING"
      
      # List generated artifacts
      - ls -lah deployment.zip
      - unzip -l deployment.zip | head -20
      
      - echo "=== Post-Build Phase Complete ==="
10.2 Test Locally with CodeBuild Local
# Install CodeBuild Local Agent (Docker-based)
git clone https://github.com/aws/aws-codebuild-docker-images.git
cd aws-codebuild-docker-images/ubuntu/standard/7.0

# Build the Docker image
docker build -t aws/codebuild/standard:7.0 .

# Run your build locally
cd /path/to/anihpj

# Download codebuild local agent
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/aws-codebuild-docker-images/master/local_builds/codebuild_build.sh
chmod +x codebuild_build.sh

# Test your buildspec.yml locally
./codebuild_build.sh \
  -i aws/codebuild/standard:7.0 \
  -a /tmp/artifacts \
  -s $(pwd) \
  -c \
  -e envvars.txt

# envvars.txt contains environment variables:
# AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1
# DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost/test_db
# SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key

# This allows you to:
# 1. Test buildspec.yml without committing
# 2. Debug build issues locally
# 3. Validate changes before pushing
# 4. Save CodeBuild minutes
10.3 Emergency Rollback Procedures
Quick Rollback to Previous Version
# Method 1: Rollback via Elastic Beanstalk (Fastest)
# View deployment history
aws elasticbeanstalk describe-application-versions \
  --application-name anihpj \
  --query 'ApplicationVersions[*].[VersionLabel,DateCreated]' \
  --output table

# Rollback to previous version (N-1)
aws elasticbeanstalk update-environment \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --version-label v-abc123-previous

# Result: Rollback completes in 2-3 minutes


# Method 2: Re-run Previous Pipeline Execution
# Get last successful execution
SUCCESSFUL_EXEC=$(aws codepipeline list-pipeline-executions \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --query 'pipelineExecutionSummaries[?status==`Succeeded`] | [1].pipelineExecutionId' \
  --output text)

# Retry that execution
aws codepipeline retry-stage-execution \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name Deploy \
  --pipeline-execution-id $SUCCESSFUL_EXEC \
  --retry-mode FAILED_ACTIONS


# Method 3: Manual Deployment (Emergency)
# If pipeline is completely broken, deploy manually:

# 1. Download last known good artifact
aws s3 cp \
  s3://codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj/anihpj-prod/BuildArtif/abc123/deployment.zip \
  ./deployment.zip

# 2. Create new application version
aws elasticbeanstalk create-application-version \
  --application-name anihpj \
  --version-label emergency-rollback-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S) \
  --source-bundle S3Bucket=codepipeline-artifacts-anihpj,S3Key=deployment.zip

# 3. Deploy immediately
aws elasticbeanstalk update-environment \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --version-label emergency-rollback-20250115-140000


# Method 4: Blue/Green Rollback (if using ALB)
# Simply switch traffic back to blue target group
aws elbv2 modify-listener \
  --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:listener/app/anihpj/xxx \
  --default-actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-blue/xxx

# Result: Instant rollback (0 downtime)

📚 11. API & CLI Reference

Essential AWS CodePipeline API and CLI commands for automation and management.

11.1 Essential CLI Commands
# ============================================
# PIPELINE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================

# Create pipeline from JSON definition
aws codepipeline create-pipeline \
  --cli-input-json file://pipeline-definition.json

# Get pipeline details
aws codepipeline get-pipeline \
  --name anihpj-prod-pipeline

# Update pipeline
aws codepipeline update-pipeline \
  --cli-input-json file://pipeline-definition-updated.json

# Delete pipeline
aws codepipeline delete-pipeline \
  --name anihpj-old-pipeline

# List all pipelines
aws codepipeline list-pipelines

# Get pipeline state (current status)
aws codepipeline get-pipeline-state \
  --name anihpj-prod-pipeline


# ============================================
# PIPELINE EXECUTION
# ============================================

# Start pipeline execution manually
aws codepipeline start-pipeline-execution \
  --name anihpj-prod-pipeline

# Start with custom variables
aws codepipeline start-pipeline-execution \
  --name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --variables name=Environment,value=staging

# List pipeline executions (history)
aws codepipeline list-pipeline-executions \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --max-results 10

# Get execution details
aws codepipeline get-pipeline-execution \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --pipeline-execution-id abc123-def456

# Stop pipeline execution
aws codepipeline stop-pipeline-execution \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --pipeline-execution-id abc123-def456 \
  --abandon true \
  --reason "Emergency rollback required"


# ============================================
# STAGE & ACTION MANAGEMENT
# ============================================

# Retry failed stage
aws codepipeline retry-stage-execution \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name Build \
  --pipeline-execution-id abc123-def456 \
  --retry-mode FAILED_ACTIONS

# Enable/Disable stage transition
# (Prevent deploys to production)
aws codepipeline disable-stage-transition \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name Production \
  --transition-type Inbound \
  --reason "Code freeze for holidays"

# Re-enable transitions
aws codepipeline enable-stage-transition \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name Production \
  --transition-type Inbound


# ============================================
# MANUAL APPROVAL
# ============================================

# Get approval token from pipeline execution details
TOKEN=$(aws codepipeline get-pipeline-state \
  --name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --query 'stageStates[?stageName==`ManualApproval`].actionStates[0].latestExecution.token' \
  --output text)

# Approve deployment
aws codepipeline put-approval-result \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name ManualApproval \
  --action-name ProductionApproval \
  --result status=Approved,summary="Approved by DevOps team" \
  --token $TOKEN

# Reject deployment
aws codepipeline put-approval-result \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --stage-name ManualApproval \
  --action-name ProductionApproval \
  --result status=Rejected,summary="Failed QA testing" \
  --token $TOKEN


# ============================================
# WEBHOOKS (GitHub Integration)
# ============================================

# List webhooks
aws codepipeline list-webhooks

# Register webhook
aws codepipeline register-webhook-with-third-party \
  --webhook-name anihpj-github-webhook

# Deregister webhook
aws codepipeline deregister-webhook-with-third-party \
  --webhook-name anihpj-github-webhook

# Delete webhook
aws codepipeline delete-webhook \
  --name anihpj-github-webhook


# ============================================
# TROUBLESHOOTING & MONITORING
# ============================================

# Get action execution details (debugging)
aws codepipeline get-action-execution \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --execution-id abc123-def456 \
  --action-name CodeBuild

# List action executions with filters
aws codepipeline list-action-executions \
  --pipeline-name anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --filter pipelineExecutionId=abc123-def456

# Get pipeline CloudWatch metrics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
  --namespace AWS/CodePipeline \
  --metric-name PipelineExecutionSuccess \
  --dimensions Name=PipelineName,Value=anihpj-prod-pipeline \
  --start-time 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z \
  --end-time 2025-01-15T23:59:59Z \
  --period 86400 \
  --statistics Sum
11.2 Python (Boto3) SDK Examples
# Complete Python automation examples using Boto3

import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

codepipeline = boto3.client('codepipeline')

# ================================================
# Example 1: Monitor Pipeline Execution
# ================================================

def monitor_pipeline(pipeline_name, execution_id):
    """
    Monitor pipeline execution and return status
    """
    response = codepipeline.get_pipeline_execution(
        pipelineName=pipeline_name,
        pipelineExecutionId=execution_id
    )
    
    status = response['pipelineExecution']['status']
    print(f"Pipeline: {pipeline_name}")
    print(f"Execution ID: {execution_id}")
    print(f"Status: {status}")
    
    if status == 'Succeeded':
        print("✅ Pipeline succeeded!")
    elif status == 'Failed':
        print("❌ Pipeline failed!")
        # Get failure details
        state = codepipeline.get_pipeline_state(name=pipeline_name)
        for stage in state['stageStates']:
            for action in stage.get('actionStates', []):
                if action['latestExecution']['status'] == 'Failed':
                    print(f"Failed action: {action['actionName']}")
                    print(f"Error: {action['latestExecution'].get('errorDetails', {}).get('message')}")
    
    return status


# ================================================
# Example 2: Get Pipeline Metrics
# ================================================

def get_pipeline_metrics(pipeline_name, days=30):
    """
    Get success rate and average execution time
    """
    start_date = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=days)
    
    executions = codepipeline.list_pipeline_executions(
        pipelineName=pipeline_name,
        maxResults=100
    )
    
    total = 0
    succeeded = 0
    failed = 0
    durations = []
    
    for exec in executions['pipelineExecutionSummaries']:
        if exec['startTime'] < start_date:
            continue
        
        total += 1
        status = exec['status']
        
        if status == 'Succeeded':
            succeeded += 1
            if 'lastUpdateTime' in exec:
                duration = (exec['lastUpdateTime'] - exec['startTime']).total_seconds() / 60
                durations.append(duration)
        elif status == 'Failed':
            failed += 1
    
    success_rate = (succeeded / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0
    avg_duration = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else 0
    
    print(f"\n📊 Pipeline Metrics ({days} days)")
    print(f"Pipeline: {pipeline_name}")
    print(f"Total executions: {total}")
    print(f"Succeeded: {succeeded} ({success_rate:.1f}%)")
    print(f"Failed: {failed}")
    print(f"Average duration: {avg_duration:.1f} minutes")
    
    return {
        'total': total,
        'succeeded': succeeded,
        'failed': failed,
        'success_rate': success_rate,
        'avg_duration': avg_duration
    }


# ================================================
# Example 3: Automated Approval Based on Tests
# ================================================

def conditional_approval(pipeline_name, stage_name, action_name):
    """
    Approve deployment only if all tests passed
    """
    # Get pipeline state
    state = codepipeline.get_pipeline_state(name=pipeline_name)
    
    # Find approval action
    approval_token = None
    for stage in state['stageStates']:
        if stage['stageName'] == stage_name:
            for action in stage.get('actionStates', []):
                if action['actionName'] == action_name:
                    approval_token = action['latestExecution'].get('token')
                    break
    
    if not approval_token:
        print("No pending approval found")
        return
    
    # Check test results (from CodeBuild)
    # This is a simplified example - in reality you'd parse test reports
    test_passed = check_test_results(pipeline_name)  # Custom function
    coverage_ok = check_coverage(pipeline_name)     # Custom function
    
    if test_passed and coverage_ok:
        # Approve
        codepipeline.put_approval_result(
            pipelineName=pipeline_name,
            stageName=stage_name,
            actionName=action_name,
            result={
                'summary': 'Auto-approved: All tests passed ✓',
                'status': 'Approved'
            },
            token=approval_token
        )
        print("✅ Deployment approved")
    else:
        # Reject
        codepipeline.put_approval_result(
            pipelineName=pipeline_name,
            stageName=stage_name,
            actionName=action_name,
            result={
                'summary': 'Auto-rejected: Tests failed or coverage below threshold',
                'status': 'Rejected'
            },
            token=approval_token
        )
        print("❌ Deployment rejected")


# ================================================
# Example 4: Bulk Pipeline Management
# ================================================

def manage_multiple_pipelines(action='list'):
    """
    Manage multiple pipelines at once
    """
    pipelines = codepipeline.list_pipelines()
    
    anihpj_pipelines = [
        p for p in pipelines['pipelines'] 
        if p['name'].startswith('anihpj-')
    ]
    
    if action == 'list':
        print(f"Found {len(anihpj_pipelines)} anihpj pipelines:")
        for pipeline in anihpj_pipelines:
            state = codepipeline.get_pipeline_state(name=pipeline['name'])
            latest_exec = state['stageStates'][0].get('latestExecution', {})
            status = latest_exec.get('status', 'N/A')
            print(f"  - {pipeline['name']}: {status}")
    
    elif action == 'disable_prod':
        # Prevent deployments to production (e.g., during holidays)
        for pipeline in anihpj_pipelines:
            if 'prod' in pipeline['name']:
                codepipeline.disable_stage_transition(
                    pipelineName=pipeline['name'],
                    stageName='Deploy',
                    transitionType='Inbound',
                    reason='Code freeze - Holiday period'
                )
                print(f"Disabled: {pipeline['name']}")
    
    elif action == 'enable_prod':
        # Re-enable production deployments
        for pipeline in anihpj_pipelines:
            if 'prod' in pipeline['name']:
                codepipeline.enable_stage_transition(
                    pipelineName=pipeline['name'],
                    stageName='Deploy',
                    transitionType='Inbound'
                )
                print(f"Enabled: {pipeline['name']}")


# Usage examples:
if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Monitor current execution
    monitor_pipeline('anihpj-prod-pipeline', 'abc123-def456')
    
    # Get 30-day metrics
    metrics = get_pipeline_metrics('anihpj-prod-pipeline', days=30)
    
    # Auto-approve if tests pass
    conditional_approval('anihpj-prod-pipeline', 'ManualApproval', 'ProductionApproval')
    
    # List all pipelines
    manage_multiple_pipelines(action='list')
