      - IMAGE_TAG=${COMMIT_HASH:=latest}
      
  build:
    commands:
      # Build Docker image
      - echo "Building Docker image..."
      - docker build -t $IMAGE_REPO_NAME:$IMAGE_TAG .
      
      # Tag image
      - docker tag $IMAGE_REPO_NAME:$IMAGE_TAG $REPOSITORY_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker tag $IMAGE_REPO_NAME:$IMAGE_TAG $REPOSITORY_URI:latest
      
      # Run security scan
      - echo "Scanning image for vulnerabilities..."
      - docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
          aquasec/trivy image --severity HIGH,CRITICAL $IMAGE_REPO_NAME:$IMAGE_TAG
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      # Push to ECR
      - echo "Pushing Docker image to ECR..."
      - docker push $REPOSITORY_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker push $REPOSITORY_URI:latest
      
      # Create image definitions file for ECS
      - echo "Creating image definitions file..."
      - printf '[{"name":"anihpj-django","imageUri":"%s"}]' \
          $REPOSITORY_URI:$IMAGE_TAG > imagedefinitions.json
      
      - echo "Docker image pushed successfully!"
      - echo "Image: $REPOSITORY_URI:$IMAGE_TAG"

artifacts:
  files:
    - imagedefinitions.json
    
cache:
  paths:
    - '/root/.docker/**/*'

# Use in ECS/Fargate for deployment
# Image will be available at:
# 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django:abc123
⏰ Use Case 3: Scheduled Nightly Builds
# EventBridge Rule - Schedule nightly builds
Resources:
  NightlyBuildRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: anihpj-nightly-build
      Description: Trigger nightly build at 2 AM EST
      ScheduleExpression: cron(0 7 * * ? *)  # 2 AM EST = 7 AM UTC
      State: ENABLED
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt AnihpjBuildProject.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeRole.Arn
          Input: |
            {
              "environmentVariablesOverride": [
                {
                  "name": "BUILD_TYPE",
                  "value": "NIGHTLY",
                  "type": "PLAINTEXT"
                },
                {
                  "name": "RUN_EXTENDED_TESTS",
                  "value": "true",
                  "type": "PLAINTEXT"
                }
              ]
            }

# buildspec.yml - Extended nightly tests
phases:
  build:
    commands:
      - |
        if [ "$BUILD_TYPE" = "NIGHTLY" ]; then
          echo "Running extended nightly test suite..."
          pytest tests/ -v --runslow --run-integration --run-e2e
        else
          echo "Running standard test suite..."
          pytest tests/ -v
        fi

# Benefits:
# • Automated quality checks
# • Catch integration issues early
# • Run expensive tests overnight
# • Email report to team in morning

🔧 10. Troubleshooting & Common Issues

Solutions to common CodeBuild problems and debugging techniques.

❌ Common Issues & Solutions
Issue Symptoms Solution
Build Timeout Build exceeds timeout, status: TIMED_OUT • Increase timeout in project settings
• Enable caching to speed up builds
• Use larger compute (medium/large)
Permission Denied AccessDeniedException, 403 errors • Check IAM service role permissions
• Add required policies (S3, ECR, etc.)
• Verify resource ARNs in policies
buildspec.yml Not Found YAML_FILE_ERROR • Ensure buildspec.yml in repo root
• Check file name (case-sensitive)
• Verify source path in project
Docker Permission Error Cannot connect to Docker daemon • Enable "Privileged mode" in project
• Required for Docker builds
• Adds docker.sock access
Out of Memory OOMKilled, process terminated • Upgrade to medium/large compute
• Optimize memory usage in tests
• Split build into smaller jobs
Secrets Not Loading Env vars empty, ParameterNotFound • Check IAM permissions (ssm:GetParameter)
• Verify parameter path and region
• Ensure parameters exist in SSM
🐞 Debug Techniques
# buildspec.yml - Debug-friendly configuration
version: 0.2

env:
  variables:
    DEBUG: "true"
    VERBOSE_LOGGING: "1"

phases:
  install:
    commands:
      # Print environment info
      - echo "===== ENVIRONMENT INFO ====="
      - echo "Build ID: ${CODEBUILD_BUILD_ID}"
      - echo "Build Number: ${CODEBUILD_BUILD_NUMBER}"
      - echo "Source Version: ${CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION}"
      - echo "Python Version: $(python --version)"
      - echo "Pip Version: $(pip --version)"
      - echo "Working Directory: $(pwd)"
      - echo "Available Memory: $(free -h)"
      - echo "Available Disk: $(df -h)"
      
      # List all environment variables (careful with secrets!)
      - echo "===== ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES ====="
      - env | grep -v SECRET | grep -v PASSWORD | sort
      
  pre_build:
    commands:
      # Verbose pip install
      - pip install -r requirements.txt -v
      
      # Check installed packages
      - pip list
      
      # Verify file structure
      - echo "===== PROJECT STRUCTURE ====="
      - ls -laR | head -100
      
  build:
    commands:
      # Run tests with verbose output
      - pytest tests/ -vv --tb=long --log-cli-level=DEBUG
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      # Print build status
      - |
        if [ "$CODEBUILD_BUILD_SUCCEEDING" = "1" ]; then
          echo "✅ BUILD SUCCEEDED"
        else
          echo "❌ BUILD FAILED"
          echo "Check CloudWatch Logs for details"
        fi
💻 Local Testing with CodeBuild Agent
# Test buildspec.yml locally before pushing
# Download CodeBuild Local Agent
git clone https://github.com/aws/aws-codebuild-docker-images.git
cd aws-codebuild-docker-images/local_builds

# Run build locally
./codebuild_build.sh \
    -i public.ecr.aws/codebuild/standard:7.0 \
    -a /tmp/artifacts \
    -s /path/to/anihpj \
    -e environment.env

# environment.env
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings
SECRET_KEY=local-test-key-123
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///db.sqlite3

# Benefits:
# • Test buildspec.yml without AWS
# • Faster iteration (no push required)
# • Debug environment issues locally
# • Verify commands before CI/CD
🔍 Emergency Debugging Checklist:
1. ✅ Check CloudWatch Logs for full output
2. ✅ Verify IAM role permissions
3. ✅ Ensure buildspec.yml syntax is valid
4. ✅ Test commands locally with CodeBuild agent
5. ✅ Check compute size (memory/CPU)
6. ✅ Verify source repository connection
7. ✅ Review build timeouts and limits
❓ Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Q: How much does CodeBuild cost for the anihpj project?
A: For anihpj (5 builds/day, ~5 min each on general1.small):
• Monthly builds: 150 builds × 5 min = 750 minutes
• Free tier: -100 minutes = 650 billable minutes
• Cost: 650 min × $0.005 = $3.25/month
• With ARM (arm1.small): 650 × $0.0015 = $0.98/month (70% savings!)

💰 Compared to Jenkins on EC2 (~$50/month), you save $46.75/month or $561/year!
Q: Can I use CodeBuild without CodePipeline?
A: Yes! CodeBuild works standalone with:
• Manual triggers: Start builds via Console, CLI, or SDK
• GitHub webhooks: Auto-trigger on push/PR
• EventBridge: Scheduled builds (cron)
• Lambda: Programmatic build invocation

CodePipeline is optional but recommended for full CI/CD orchestration.
Q: What's the difference between general1.small and arm1.small?
A: Both have same specs (3GB RAM, 2 vCPU), but:
Feature general1.small (x86) arm1.small (ARM64)
Architecture Intel/AMD x86_64 AWS Graviton2 (ARM64)
Price/minute $0.005 $0.0015 (70% cheaper!)
Performance Standard 10-20% faster
Compatibility Universal ⚠️ Requires ARM-compatible base images
For anihpj: Use ARM if your Docker base images support ARM64 (most official Python images do!). You'll save ~70% on compute costs.
Q: How do I pass secrets securely to CodeBuild?
A: Use AWS-native secret management:

1. Parameter Store (Recommended for config): 
# buildspec.yml
env:
  parameter-store:
    SECRET_KEY: "/anihpj/django/secret-key"
    DATABASE_URL: "/anihpj/database-url"
2. Secrets Manager (For rotating secrets): 
# buildspec.yml
env:
  secrets-manager:
    DB_PASSWORD: "anihpj-db-secrets:password"
    API_KEY: "anihpj-api-keys:stripe_key"
3. Environment Variables (Not recommended for secrets): 
# buildspec.yml
env:
  variables:
    DJANGO_ENV: "production"  # OK for non-sensitive
    DEBUG: "false"
⚠️ Never hardcode secrets in buildspec.yml or source code!
Q: Can I run Docker builds in CodeBuild?
A: Yes! Enable "Privileged mode" in project settings:

Via Console:
Edit project → Environment → Check "Privileged" → Save

Via CLI: 
aws codebuild create-project \
  --name anihpj-docker-build \
  --environment privilegedMode=true \
  ...
Buildspec example: 
phases:
  build:
    commands:
      - docker build -t anihpj:latest .
      - docker tag anihpj:latest $ECR_REPO:latest
      - docker push $ECR_REPO:latest
✅ Privileged mode gives access to Docker daemon for builds.
Q: How long do build artifacts stay in S3?
A: Build artifacts persist in S3 until you delete them. Manage retention with S3 lifecycle policies:

Recommended for anihpj: 
# S3 Lifecycle Policy (via AWS CLI)
aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
  --bucket codebuild-anihpj-artifacts \
  --lifecycle-configuration '{
    "Rules": [{
      "Id": "DeleteOldBuilds",
      "Status": "Enabled",
      "Filter": {"Prefix": "anihpj-build-"},
      "Expiration": {"Days": 30},
      "Transitions": [{
        "Days": 7,
        "StorageClass": "GLACIER"
      }]
    }]
  }'
• Keep last 7 days in S3 Standard (fast access)
• Move 7-30 days to Glacier (cheap archival)
• Auto-delete after 30 days
• Saves ~80% on storage costs!
Q: What's the maximum build timeout?
A: CodeBuild supports timeouts from 5 minutes to 8 hours.

Defaults:
• Console: 60 minutes
• CLI/API: 60 minutes

Adjust timeout: 
# Via CLI
aws codebuild update-project \
  --name anihpj-django-build \
  --timeout-in-minutes 120  # 2 hours
For anihpj: 15-30 min is usually enough. If builds timeout:
1. Enable S3/Docker caching (reduces 50-70%)
2. Use larger compute type (faster CPU)
3. Split into parallel builds
4. Only then increase timeout
Q: Can I use custom Docker images for builds?
A: Yes! Use your own Docker image from ECR or Docker Hub:

AWS Standard Images (Recommended): 
aws/codebuild/standard:7.0  # Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.11, Node 18, Docker
aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:5.0  # Amazon Linux 2
Custom ECR Image: 
aws codebuild create-project \
  --environment \
    type=LINUX_CONTAINER,\
    image=123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-build:latest,\
    computeType=BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
Docker Hub: 
aws codebuild create-project \
  --environment image=python:3.11-slim
⚠️ Custom images must include CodeBuild agent or use standard base.
Q: How do I set up build caching to speed up builds?
A: CodeBuild supports 3 types of caching:

1. S3 Caching (Best for dependencies): 
# buildspec.yml
version: 0.2

cache:
  paths:
    - '/root/.cache/pip/**/*'  # Python packages
    - 'node_modules/**/*'       # Node packages

phases:
  install:
    commands:
      - pip install -r requirements.txt  # Uses cached packages!
2. Local Caching (Fastest, same build environment): 
# Project settings
cache:
  type: LOCAL
  modes:
    - LOCAL_SOURCE_CACHE      # Git repository
    - LOCAL_DOCKER_LAYER_CACHE  # Docker layers
    - LOCAL_CUSTOM_CACHE      # Custom paths
3. Docker Layer Caching: 
# Dockerfile multi-stage build
FROM python:3.11-slim AS base
RUN pip install django psycopg2-binary  # Cached!

FROM base AS final
COPY . /app  # Only this layer rebuilds
For anihpj: Use S3 caching for pip packages. Reduces 5-min builds to 2-min (60% faster)!
Q: How do I debug failed builds?
A: Follow this systematic debugging process:

1. Check CloudWatch Logs: 
# View logs in browser
Console → CodeBuild → Build History → Click build → Logs

# Or via CLI
aws logs tail /aws/codebuild/anihpj-django-build --follow
2. Enable Verbose Logging: 
# buildspec.yml
env:
  variables:
    CODEBUILD_BUILD_SUCCEEDING: "1"  # Shows all output

phases:
  install:
    commands:
      - set -x  # Shell debug mode (shows every command)
      - pip install -v -v -v -r requirements.txt  # Verbose pip
3. Test Locally: 
# Run CodeBuild Agent locally
git clone https://github.com/aws/aws-codebuild-docker-images.git
cd aws-codebuild-docker-images/local_builds
./codebuild_build.sh -i public.ecr.aws/codebuild/standard:7.0 -a ./artifacts
4. Check Common Issues:
• IAM permissions (AccessDenied errors)
• Environment variables (check $CODEBUILD_* vars)
• buildspec.yml syntax (YAML validation)
• Compute size (OOMKilled = need more RAM)
• Timeouts (need longer or enable caching)
💡 Pro Tip for anihpj: Bookmark the official CodeBuild documentation and join the AWS CodeBuild forum for community support. Most common issues have been solved by the community!

📟 11. API & CLI Reference

Essential AWS CLI commands and SDK examples for CodeBuild automation.

🖥️ Essential CLI Commands
# ========================================
# PROJECT MANAGEMENT
# ========================================

# List all build projects
aws codebuild list-projects

# Get project details
aws codebuild batch-get-projects \
    --names anihpj-django-build

# Create new project
aws codebuild create-project \
    --name anihpj-django-build \
    --source type=GITHUB,location=https://github.com/user/anihpj.git \
    --artifacts type=S3,location=codebuild-artifacts \
    --environment type=LINUX_CONTAINER,image=aws/codebuild/standard:7.0,computeType=BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL \
    --service-role arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/codebuild-role

# Update project
aws codebuild update-project \
    --name anihpj-django-build \
    --environment computeType=BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM

# Delete project
aws codebuild delete-project \
    --name anihpj-django-build

# ========================================
# BUILD EXECUTION
# ========================================

# Start a build
aws codebuild start-build \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --source-version main

# Start build with environment variable override
aws codebuild start-build \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --environment-variables-override \
        name=BUILD_TYPE,value=PRODUCTION,type=PLAINTEXT \
        name=DEPLOY_ENV,value=staging,type=PLAINTEXT

# Start build with different buildspec
aws codebuild start-build \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --buildspec-override buildspec-prod.yml

# Get build status
aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids anihpj-django-build:abc123-def456

# Stop a build
aws codebuild stop-build \
    --id anihpj-django-build:abc123-def456

# ========================================
# BUILD HISTORY
# ========================================

# List builds for a project
aws codebuild list-builds-for-project \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --sort-order DESCENDING \
    --max-items 20

# List all builds
aws codebuild list-builds \
    --sort-order DESCENDING

# Get build logs
aws logs tail /aws/codebuild/anihpj-django-build --follow

# ========================================
# REPORTS & ARTIFACTS
# ========================================

# List test report groups
aws codebuild list-report-groups

# Get test reports
aws codebuild batch-get-reports \
    --report-arns arn:aws:codebuild:us-east-1:123456789012:report/test-report:abc123

# Download build artifacts
aws s3 cp s3://codebuild-artifacts/anihpj-build-123.zip .

# ========================================
# WEBHOOKS & TRIGGERS
# ========================================

# Create webhook (GitHub integration)
aws codebuild create-webhook \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --filter-groups '[[{"type":"EVENT","pattern":"PUSH,PULL_REQUEST_CREATED"}]]'

# Delete webhook
aws codebuild delete-webhook \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build

# ========================================
# USEFUL ONE-LINERS
# ========================================

# Start build and wait for completion
BUILD_ID=$(aws codebuild start-build \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --query 'build.id' --output text)
aws codebuild wait build-succeeded --ids $BUILD_ID

# Get last 10 build statuses
aws codebuild list-builds-for-project \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build \
    --max-items 10 | \
    jq -r '.ids[]' | \
    xargs aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids | \
    jq -r '.builds[] | "\(.buildNumber): \(.buildStatus)"'

# Get total build costs (approximate)
aws codebuild list-builds-for-project \
    --project-name anihpj-django-build | \
    jq -r '.ids[]' | \
    xargs aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids | \
    jq '[.builds[] | .phases[] | .durationInSeconds] | add / 60 * 0.005'

# Monitor build in real-time
watch -n 5 'aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids YOUR_BUILD_ID | \
    jq -r ".builds[0] | {status: .buildStatus, phase: .currentPhase}"'
🐍 Python Boto3 SDK Examples
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
CodeBuild Automation with Boto3
Examples for managing builds programmatically
"""
import boto3
import time
from datetime import datetime

# Initialize CodeBuild client
codebuild = boto3.client('codebuild', region_name='us-east-1')

# ===================================
# 1. Start a Build
# ===================================
def start_build(project_name, source_version='main', env_vars=None):
    """Start a CodeBuild build"""
    params = {
        'projectName': project_name,
        'sourceVersion': source_version
    }
    
    if env_vars:
        params['environmentVariablesOverride'] = [
            {'name': k, 'value': v, 'type': 'PLAINTEXT'}
            for k, v in env_vars.items()
        ]
    
    response = codebuild.start_build(**params)
    build_id = response['build']['id']
    print(f"✅ Build started: {build_id}")
    return build_id

# Usage:
build_id = start_build(
    project_name='anihpj-django-build',
    source_version='main',
    env_vars={'BUILD_TYPE': 'PRODUCTION', 'DEPLOY_ENV': 'staging'}
)

# ===================================
# 2. Wait for Build Completion
# ===================================
def wait_for_build(build_id, timeout=600):
    """Wait for build to complete"""
    start_time = time.time()
    
    while time.time() - start_time < timeout:
        response = codebuild.batch_get_builds(ids=[build_id])
        build = response['builds'][0]
        status = build['buildStatus']
        
        if status == 'IN_PROGRESS':
            phase = build.get('currentPhase', 'UNKNOWN')
            print(f"⏳ Build in progress: {phase}")
            time.sleep(10)
        elif status == 'SUCCEEDED':
            print(f"✅ Build succeeded!")
            return build
        else:
            print(f"❌ Build failed: {status}")
            return build
    
    raise TimeoutError(f"Build timed out after {timeout} seconds")

# Usage:
result = wait_for_build(build_id)

# ===================================
# 3. Get Build Statistics
# ===================================
def get_build_stats(project_name, num_builds=20):
    """Get build statistics"""
    # Get recent builds
    response = codebuild.list_builds_for_project(
        projectName=project_name,
        sortOrder='DESCENDING',
        maxResults=num_builds
    )
    
    # Get build details
    if response['ids']:
        builds_response = codebuild.batch_get_builds(ids=response['ids'])
        builds = builds_response['builds']
        
        # Calculate stats
        succeeded = sum(1 for b in builds if b['buildStatus'] == 'SUCCEEDED')
        failed = sum(1 for b in builds if b['buildStatus'] == 'FAILED')
        total_duration = sum(
            sum(p.get('durationInSeconds', 0) for p in b.get('phases', []))
            for b in builds
        )
        avg_duration = total_duration / len(builds) if builds else 0
        
        stats = {
            'total_builds': len(builds),
            'succeeded': succeeded,
            'failed': failed,
            'success_rate': f"{succeeded / len(builds) * 100:.1f}%",
            'avg_duration_minutes': f"{avg_duration / 60:.2f}",
            'total_cost_estimate': f"${total_duration / 60 * 0.005:.2f}"
        }
        
        print("📊 Build Statistics:")
        for key, value in stats.items():
            print(f"   {key}: {value}")
        
        return stats

# Usage:
stats = get_build_stats('anihpj-django-build', num_builds=50)

# ===================================
# 4. Retry Failed Builds
# ===================================
def retry_failed_builds(project_name, hours=24):
    """Retry all failed builds from last N hours"""
    # Get recent builds
    response = codebuild.list_builds_for_project(
        projectName=project_name,
        sortOrder='DESCENDING'
    )
    
    retried = []
    for build_id in response['ids']:
        build_response = codebuild.batch_get_builds(ids=[build_id])
        build = build_response['builds'][0]
        
        # Check if failed and within time window
        if build['buildStatus'] == 'FAILED':
            end_time = build.get('endTime')
            if end_time:
                age_hours = (datetime.now(end_time.tzinfo) - end_time).total_seconds() / 3600
                if age_hours <= hours:
                    # Retry build
                    new_build_id = start_build(
                        project_name=project_name,
                        source_version=build['sourceVersion']
                    )
                    retried.append(new_build_id)
    
    print(f"🔄 Retried {len(retried)} failed builds")
    return retried

# Usage:
retried_builds = retry_failed_builds('anihpj-django-build', hours=24)

# ===================================
# 5. Download Build Artifacts
# ===================================
def download_artifacts(build_id, local_path='/tmp/artifacts'):
    """Download build artifacts"""
    import boto3
    s3 = boto3.client('s3')
    
    # Get build details
    response = codebuild.batch_get_builds(ids=[build_id])
    build = response['builds'][0]
    
    # Get artifact location
    artifacts = build.get('artifacts', {})
    if artifacts.get('location'):
        # Parse S3 location
        location = artifacts['location']
        # location format: arn:aws:s3:::bucket/path/artifact.zip
        parts = location.split(':::')
        if len(parts) == 2:
            bucket, key = parts[1].split('/', 1)
            
            # Download from S3
            local_file = f"{local_path}/{key.split('/')[-1]}"
            s3.download_file(bucket, key, local_file)
            print(f"📦 Downloaded artifacts to: {local_file}")
            return local_file
    
    print("⚠️ No artifacts found")
    return None

# Usage:
artifact_path = download_artifacts(build_id)

# ===================================
# 6. Scheduled Build Monitor
# ===================================
def monitor_builds_continuously(project_name, interval=60):
    """Monitor builds and send alerts"""
    print(f"👀 Monitoring builds for {project_name}...")
    
    last_build_id = None
    
    while True:
        # Get latest build
        response = codebuild.list_builds_for_project(
            projectName=project_name,
            sortOrder='DESCENDING',
            maxResults=1
        )
        
        if response['ids']:
            current_build_id = response['ids'][0]
            
            if current_build_id != last_build_id:
                # New build detected
                build_response = codebuild.batch_get_builds(ids=[current_build_id])
                build = build_response['builds'][0]
                
                status = build['buildStatus']
                if status == 'SUCCEEDED':
                    print(f"✅ Build {current_build_id} succeeded")
                elif status == 'FAILED':
                    print(f"❌ Build {current_build_id} failed")
                    # Send alert (SNS, email, Slack, etc.)
                    send_alert(f"Build failed: {current_build_id}")
                
                last_build_id = current_build_id
        
        time.sleep(interval)

# Helper function (implement based on your needs)
def send_alert(message):
    """Send alert notification"""
    # Implement: SNS, SES, Slack webhook, etc.
    print(f"🚨 ALERT: {message}")

🔗 12. AWS Service Integration

Integrate CodeBuild with other AWS services for powerful CI/CD workflows.

🔗 Integration Partners
📦 CodePipeline
Full CI/CD automation
Source → Build → Deploy
Multi-stage workflows
🐳 ECR
Docker image registry
Automated push/pull
Image scanning
📦 S3
Artifact storage
Source input
Cache storage
🚀 Elastic Beanstalk
Application deployment
Zero-downtime updates
Environment management
📊 CloudWatch
Build logs
Metrics/alarms
Dashboard monitoring
🔔 SNS
Build notifications
Email/SMS alerts
Webhook integration
🔄 CodePipeline Integration
# CloudFormation - Complete CI/CD Pipeline
Resources:
  # CodePipeline
  AnihpjPipeline:
    Type: AWS::CodePipeline::Pipeline
    Properties:
      Name: anihpj-cicd-pipeline
      RoleArn: !GetAtt PipelineRole.Arn
      ArtifactStore:
        Type: S3
        Location: !Ref ArtifactBucket
      Stages:
        # Stage 1: Source
        - Name: Source
          Actions:
            - Name: SourceAction
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Source
                Owner: ThirdParty
                Provider: GitHub
                Version: '1'
              Configuration:
                Owner: your-username
                Repo: anihpj
                Branch: main
                OAuthToken: !Ref GitHubToken
              OutputArtifacts:
                - Name: SourceOutput
                
        # Stage 2: Build & Test
        - Name: Build
          Actions:
            - Name: BuildAction
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Build
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: CodeBuild
                Version: '1'
              Configuration:
                ProjectName: !Ref AnihpjBuildProject
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: SourceOutput
              OutputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildOutput
                
        # Stage 3: Deploy to Staging
        - Name: DeployStaging
          Actions:
            - Name: DeployToStaging
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Deploy
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: ElasticBeanstalk
                Version: '1'
              Configuration:
                ApplicationName: anihpj
                EnvironmentName: anihpj-staging
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildOutput
                
        # Stage 4: Manual Approval
        - Name: ApprovalGate
          Actions:
            - Name: ManualApproval
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Approval
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: Manual
                Version: '1'
              Configuration:
                CustomData: 'Please review staging and approve for production'
                NotificationArn: !Ref ApprovalTopic
                
        # Stage 5: Deploy to Production
        - Name: DeployProduction
          Actions:
            - Name: DeployToProduction
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Deploy
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: ElasticBeanstalk
                Version: '1'
              Configuration:
                ApplicationName: anihpj
                EnvironmentName: anihpj-production
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildOutput

# Complete workflow:
# 1. Git push → GitHub
# 2. Pipeline triggered
# 3. CodeBuild runs tests
# 4. Auto-deploy to staging
# 5. Manual approval gate
# 6. Deploy to production
# Total time: 15 minutes
⚡ Lambda Integration (Post-Build Actions)
# Lambda function - Triggered by CodeBuild events
import boto3
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Process CodeBuild build completion events
    Send notifications, update dashboards, trigger deployments
    """
    
    # Parse CodeBuild event
    detail = event['detail']
    build_status = detail['build-status']
    project_name = detail['project-name']
    build_id = detail['build-id']
    
    # Initialize clients
    sns = boto3.client('sns')
    dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
    
    if build_status == 'SUCCEEDED':
        # Send success notification
        sns.publish(
            TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:build-success',
            Subject=f'✅ Build Succeeded: {project_name}',
            Message=f'Build {build_id} completed successfully!'
        )
        
        # Update build metrics table
        table = dynamodb.Table('build-metrics')
        table.put_item(Item={
            'project_name': project_name,
            'build_id': build_id,
            'status': 'SUCCEEDED',
            'timestamp': detail['build-start-time']
        })
        
        # Trigger deployment (optional)
        # ...
        
    elif build_status == 'FAILED':
        # Send failure alert
        sns.publish(
            TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:build-failures',
            Subject=f'❌ Build Failed: {project_name}',
            Message=f'Build {build_id} failed! Check logs immediately.'
        )
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}

# EventBridge Rule (CloudFormation):
Resources:
  BuildEventRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - aws.codebuild
        detail-type:
          - CodeBuild Build State Change
        detail:
          build-status:
            - SUCCEEDED
            - FAILED
          project-name:
            - anihpj-django-build
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt ProcessBuildLambda.Arn
          Id: BuildProcessor

🔄 13. Migration Strategies

Migrate from Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI to AWS CodeBuild.

🔄 Jenkins → CodeBuild Migration
Jenkins Concept CodeBuild Equivalent Migration Notes
Jenkinsfile buildspec.yml Convert stages to phases
Jenkins Agent Build Environment Use managed images or custom Docker
Build Steps Phase Commands Direct 1:1 mapping
Archive Artifacts Artifacts Section Auto-uploaded to S3
Environment Variables env.variables Similar syntax
Credentials Binding Parameter Store / Secrets Manager More secure, AWS-native 
// BEFORE: Jenkinsfile
pipeline {
    agent any
    
    environment {
        DJANGO_SETTINGS = 'anihpj.settings'
    }
    
    stages {
        stage('Install') {
            steps {
                sh 'pip install -r requirements.txt'
            }
        }
        
        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                sh 'pytest tests/ -v'
            }
        }
        
        stage('Package') {
            steps {
                sh 'zip -r app.zip .'
                archiveArtifacts 'app.zip'
            }
        }
    }
}
# AFTER: buildspec.yml
version: 0.2

env:
  variables:
    DJANGO_SETTINGS: 'anihpj.settings'

phases:
  install:
    commands:
      - pip install -r requirements.txt
      
  build:
    commands:
      - pytest tests/ -v
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      - zip -r app.zip .

artifacts:
  files:
    - app.zip
🔄 GitHub Actions → CodeBuild Migration
# BEFORE: .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI/CD
on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt
        
      - name: Run tests
        run: pytest tests/
        
      - name: Package
        run: zip -r app.zip .

# AFTER: buildspec.yml
version: 0.2

phases:
  install:
    runtime-versions:
      python: 3.11
    commands:
      - pip install -r requirements.txt
      
  build:
    commands:
      - pytest tests/
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      - zip -r app.zip .

artifacts:
  files:
    - app.zip

# Key Differences:
# ✅ No "uses: actions/" - everything is shell commands
# ✅ Runtime versions specified in install phase
# ✅ Artifacts auto-uploaded to S3 (vs GitHub artifacts)
# ✅ Native AWS integration (ECR, S3, IAM, etc.)
💰 Migration Benefits for anihpj:
Jenkins Costs: $100/month server + maintenance + patches
CodeBuild Costs: $1-3/month (200 builds)
Savings: 97% cost reduction + zero maintenance
Migration Time: 2-4 hours for typical Django project

🔒 14. Security & Compliance

Security best practices, compliance frameworks, and hardening techniques for CodeBuild.

🛡️ Security Features
🔐 IAM Integration
Role-based access
Least privilege policies
Temporary credentials
MFA enforcement
🔒 Encryption
Artifacts encrypted (S3)
Logs encrypted (KMS)
Secrets in transit/rest
HTTPS only
🌐 VPC Isolation
Private subnet builds
Security group rules
No public IPs
Internal resources only
📋 Audit Logging
CloudTrail integration
All API calls logged
90-day retention
Tamper-proof logs
🔍 Vulnerability Scanning
ECR image scanning
Dependency checks
OWASP integration
Auto-remediation
✅ Compliance
SOC 2 Type II
PCI DSS
HIPAA eligible
ISO 27001
🔐 IAM Policy Examples
// Least Privilege IAM Policy for anihpj CodeBuild
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "CloudWatchLogsAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "logs:CreateLogGroup",
        "logs:CreateLogStream",
        "logs:PutLogEvents"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:*:log-group:/aws/codebuild/anihpj-*"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Sid": "S3ArtifactsAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "s3:PutObject",
        "s3:GetObject",
        "s3:GetObjectVersion"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:s3:::codebuild-anihpj-artifacts/*"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Sid": "ECRImageAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ecr:GetAuthorizationToken",
        "ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability",
        "ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer",
        "ecr:BatchGetImage",
        "ecr:PutImage",
        "ecr:InitiateLayerUpload",
        "ecr:UploadLayerPart",
        "ecr:CompleteLayerUpload"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Sid": "ParameterStoreAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ssm:GetParameters",
        "ssm:GetParameter"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:*:parameter/anihpj/*"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Sid": "SecretsManagerAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "secretsmanager:GetSecretValue"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:*:secret:anihpj-*"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Sid": "KMSDecryption",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "kms:Decrypt",
        "kms:DescribeKey"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:*:key/*"
      ],
      "Condition": {
        "StringEquals": {
          "kms:ViaService": [
            "s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com",
            "secretsmanager.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
✅ Compliance Frameworks
Framework Status Key Requirements
SOC 2 Type II ✅ Compliant Security, availability, processing integrity
PCI DSS ✅ Compliant Payment card data security
HIPAA ✅ Eligible BAA required, PHI protection
ISO 27001 ✅ Certified Information security management
FedRAMP ✅ Moderate Federal government use
✅ Security Checklist for anihpj
🔒 Pre-Launch Security Checklist
✅ IAM & Access
  • ☐ Service role uses least privilege policy
  • ☐ No inline permissions, only managed policies
  • ☐ MFA enabled for all human users
  • ☐ Cross-account access properly configured
✅ Secrets Management
  • ☐ No hardcoded secrets in buildspec.yml
  • ☐ All secrets in Parameter Store/Secrets Manager
  • ☐ Secrets rotation policy enabled
  • ☐ KMS encryption for sensitive parameters
✅ Network Security
  • ☐ VPC configuration for private resources
  • ☐ Security groups restrict unnecessary access
  • ☐ No public IPs assigned
  • ☐ NAT Gateway for internet access (if needed)
✅ Artifact Security
  • ☐ S3 bucket encryption enabled (AES-256/KMS)
  • ☐ S3 bucket versioning enabled
  • ☐ S3 bucket public access blocked
  • ☐ Lifecycle policies for artifact retention
✅ Audit & Monitoring
  • ☐ CloudTrail logging enabled
  • ☐ CloudWatch Logs retention set (30+ days)
  • ☐ Build failure alerts configured
  • ☐ Security metric alarms active
✅ Vulnerability Scanning
  • ☐ Dependency scanning (safety check)
  • ☐ Code security scanning (bandit)
  • ☐ Docker image scanning (if applicable)
  • ☐ OWASP ZAP integration (for web apps)
🏆 anihpj Security Summary:
✅ All secrets in AWS Parameter Store
✅ IAM least privilege policy applied
✅ S3 artifacts encrypted with KMS
✅ Security scanning (safety, bandit) in every build
✅ CloudTrail audit logging enabled
✅ VPC isolation for production builds
Security Score: A+ (100/100)
