└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              🎯 DEPLOYMENT FLOW - DETAILED BREAKDOWN                               │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 1: SOURCE (GitHub)                          Step 4: DEPLOY (CodeDeploy - Lifecycle Hooks)
├─ Repository: anihpj/jobportal                  ├─ ❌ ApplicationStop (stop_server.sh)
├─ Branch: main                                  │   └─ sudo systemctl stop apache2
├─ Trigger: Push/PR merge                        │
└─ Webhook: Instant notification                 ├─ ⬇️  DownloadBundle (from S3)
                                                  │   └─ Download artifact to /tmp
Step 2: ORCHESTRATE (CodePipeline)               │
├─ Pipeline: anihpj-cicd-pipeline                ├─ 📥 BeforeInstall (before_install.sh)
├─ Stages:                                       │   ├─ sudo apt-get update
│   1. Source (GitHub)                           │   ├─ pip install -r requirements.txt
│   2. Build (CodeBuild)                         │   └─ Backup /opt/anihpj
│   3. Deploy (CodeDeploy)                       │
│   4. [Optional] Approval                       ├─ 📝 Install (CodeDeploy agent)
└─ Artifact Store: s3://anihpj-artifacts/        │   └─ Copy files from /tmp to /opt/anihpj
                                                  │
Step 3: BUILD (CodeBuild)                        ├─ 🔧 AfterInstall (after_install.sh)
├─ Image: python:3.11                            │   ├─ python manage.py collectstatic
├─ buildspec.yml phases:                         │   ├─ sudo chown www-data:www-data -R
│   • Pre-build:                                 │   └─ Set permissions
│     └─ pip install --upgrade pip               │
│   • Build:                                     ├─ 🚀 ApplicationStart (start_server.sh)
│     ├─ python manage.py test                   │   ├─ python manage.py migrate
│     ├─ python manage.py collectstatic          │   └─ sudo systemctl start apache2
│     └─ Create deployment package               │
│   • Post-build:                                └─ ✅ ValidateService (validate_service.sh)
│     └─ Upload to S3                                └─ curl http://localhost:8000/health
└─ Output: anihpj-build-v1.2.3.zip                   └─ Check HTTP 200 OK


Step 5: MONITOR & VALIDATE                       Step 6: ROLLBACK (If Needed)
├─ CloudWatch Alarms:                            ├─ Trigger: Health check fails > 2 min
│   • HTTP 5xx errors > 10                       ├─ Action: Automatic rollback
│   • CPU usage > 80%                            ├─ Process:
│   • Memory usage > 90%                         │   1. Stop current deployment
│   • Response time > 2s                         │   2. Revert to previous revision
└─ Health Check:                                 │   3. Restore old version
    └─ GET /health every 30s                     │   4. Notify team via SNS
                                                  └─ Time: ~2 minutes (Blue/Green)
                                                      └─ ~5 minutes (In-Place Rolling)


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                   📈 PIPELINE METRICS & STATS                                      │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Total Pipeline Time: 8-14 minutes (source to production)                                        │
│  Success Rate: 98.5% (automated tests catch issues early)                                        │
│  Rollback Time: 1-2 minutes (Blue/Green) | 5-10 minutes (Rolling)                                │
│  Deployments/Month: 20-25 (weekly releases + hotfixes)                                           │
│  Cost: $0 (CodeDeploy EC2 free) + $1 (CodeBuild) = $1/month                                      │
│  Developer Time Saved: 10 hours/month (vs manual deployment)                                     │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          🎨 DEPLOYMENT STRATEGIES COMPARISON (VISUAL)                              │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

BLUE/GREEN DEPLOYMENT (Zero Downtime):          IN-PLACE ROLLING DEPLOYMENT (Partial Availability):
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  BLUE (Current)     GREEN (New)     │         │  Rolling Update: OneAtATime         │
│  ┌──────────┐      ┌──────────┐    │         │  ┌──────────┐┌──────────┐┌──────────┐
│  │ v1.2.2   │      │ v1.2.3   │    │         │  │ v1.2.2   ││ v1.2.2   ││ v1.2.2   │
│  │ EC2-1    │      │ EC2-4    │    │         │  │ EC2-1    ││ EC2-2    ││ EC2-3    │
│  │ EC2-2    │      │ EC2-5    │    │         │  │ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active│
│  │ EC2-3    │      │ EC2-6    │    │         │  └──────────┘└──────────┘└──────────┘
│  │ ✅ Live  │      │ 🧪 Test  │    │         │       ↓           ↓           ↓      │
│  └──────────┘      └──────────┘    │         │  ┌──────────┐┌──────────┐┌──────────┐
│       ↓                  ↓          │         │  │ v1.2.3   ││ v1.2.2   ││ v1.2.2   │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │         │  │ EC2-1    ││ EC2-2    ││ EC2-3    │
│  │    ALB TRAFFIC SWITCH        │   │         │  │ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active│
│  │    100% Blue → 100% Green    │   │         │  └──────────┘└──────────┘└──────────┘
│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │         │       ↓           ↓           ↓      │
│  Keep Blue for quick rollback      │         │  Continue until all updated...       │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘         └─────────────────────────────────────┘
    ⏱️ Switch time: 30 seconds                      ⏱️ Total time: 15 minutes
    💰 Cost: 2x instances (temporary)               💰 Cost: Same instances
    🛡️  Risk: Lowest                                🛡️  Risk: Medium


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                  🔄 ANIHPJ RECOMMENDED STRATEGY                                    │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                                   │
│  🚀 PRODUCTION:  Blue/Green with ALB                                                              │
│     ├─ Zero downtime critical for job seekers                                                    │
│     ├─ Instant rollback if issues detected                                                       │
│     └─ Worth the temporary cost for production stability                                         │
│                                                                                                   │
│  🧪 STAGING:     Rolling (OneAtATime)                                                             │
│     ├─ Cost-effective for testing environment                                                    │
│     ├─ Acceptable brief downtime                                                                 │
│     └─ Mirrors production process for validation                                                 │
│                                                                                                   │
│  💻 DEVELOPMENT: In-Place (AllAtOnce)                                                             │
│     ├─ Fastest updates for rapid iteration                                                       │
│     ├─ Downtime not critical for dev environment                                                 │
│     └─ Maximum efficiency for developer testing                                                  │
│                                                                                                   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🎯 Key Takeaway: This complete CI/CD pipeline reduces anihpj deployment time from 45 minutes (manual) to 10 minutes (automated), eliminates human error, provides automatic rollback, and costs only $1/month for CodeBuild.
📊 CloudWatch Integration

Monitor deployments with CloudWatch metrics and logs

📧 SNS Notifications
# Create SNS topic for deployment notifications
aws sns create-topic --name anihpj-deployments

# Subscribe email
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-deployments \
    --protocol email \
    --notification-endpoint dev@anihpj.com
📦 S3 for Artifacts

Store deployment revisions in S3 with versioning enabled

🚚 13. Deployment Strategies & Migration

🔄 Complete CI/CD Pipeline
GitHub → CodePipeline → CodeBuild → CodeDeploy → EC2

1. Developer pushes to GitHub (main branch)
2. CodePipeline detects change (webhook)
3. CodeBuild runs tests, creates artifact
4. CodeDeploy deploys to anihpj-prod-dg
5. Rolling update across 3 EC2 instances
6. CloudWatch monitors, SNS notifies team
7. Automatic rollback if health checks fail
🔧 Migrating from Jenkins
  1. Convert Jenkins deployment scripts to CodeDeploy lifecycle hooks
  2. Create appspec.yml mapping Jenkins stages to CodeDeploy events
  3. Test in development environment
  4. Gradually migrate production workloads
  5. Decommission Jenkins server (save $30/month)
🦊 Migrating from GitLab CI

Similar to Jenkins: Extract deployment scripts, create appspec.yml, leverage CodePipeline for GitLab integration

🛡️ 14. Compliance & Governance

📜 Audit Trail & Compliance
  • ✅ All deployments logged in CloudTrail
  • ✅ Deployment history retained (who deployed what, when)
  • ✅ IAM policies enforce deployment approvals
  • ✅ CloudWatch Logs for detailed deployment activities
🔐 Security & Certifications
  • ✅ SOC 1, 2, 3 compliant
  • ✅ PCI DSS Level 1
  • ✅ HIPAA eligible
  • ✅ ISO 27001, 27017, 27018
  • ✅ FedRAMP authorized
⚙️ Deployment Governance
// IAM policy: Require approval for production deployments
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": "codedeploy:CreateDeployment",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:codedeploy:*:*:deploymentgroup:anihpj-jobportal/anihpj-prod-dg",
    "Condition": {
      "StringEquals": {
        "aws:RequestedRegion": "us-east-1"
      }
    }
  }]
}

AWS CodeCommit

Category: Developer Tools - Source Control  |  Use Case: Git Repository Hosting  |  Pricing: First 5 Users Free  |  Last Updated: December 2025  |  Documentation: AWS CodeCommit Docs

Fully managed, secure, and highly scalable Git-based source control service for hosting private repositories in the AWS cloud.

📦 AWS CodeCommit - Managed Git Repository Service

Git-Compatible Encrypted CI/CD Ready Unlimited Repos AWS Native
💰
FREE
First 5 Users
📦
Unlimited
Repositories
🔒
100%
Encrypted at Rest
⚡
99.99%
Availability SLA
🌎
Multi-Region
Global Availability
🔗
Native
AWS Integration
🎯
Perfect for anihpj Django Development
Securely host your job portal source code with native AWS integration, automatic encryption, and seamless CI/CD pipeline connection to CodeBuild and CodeDeploy
$0 Monthly Cost
5 mins Setup Time

📋 Overview & Introduction

AWS CodeCommit is a fully managed source control service that hosts secure Git-based repositories. It eliminates the need to operate your own source control system or worry about scaling its infrastructure. You can use CodeCommit to store anything from code to binaries, and it works seamlessly with your existing Git tools.

🚀 Quick Start for anihpj: In just 5 minutes, migrate your anihpj Django job portal from GitHub/GitLab to CodeCommit, integrate with CodePipeline for automated CI/CD, and leverage IAM for secure team access control.
5 Steps:
  1. Create CodeCommit repository
  2. Configure IAM permissions (HTTPS or SSH)
  3. Clone and push anihpj code
  4. Set up CodePipeline trigger
  5. Start committing and auto-deploying! ✅
🎯 CodeCommit at a Glance
🔒 Security First

Encryption at rest (AWS KMS) and in transit (HTTPS/SSH). IAM-based access control with MFA support.

📦 Unlimited Repos

Create unlimited repositories with no size limits. Store code, binaries, and large files effortlessly.

🔗 AWS Native

Direct integration with CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, Lambda, and SNS for notifications.

⚡ High Performance

Fast git operations with low latency. Automatic scaling handles any team size or repository load.

💰 Cost Effective

Free for first 5 users. $1/user/month after that. No hidden costs for storage or transfer within AWS.

🔄 Git Compatible

Works with standard Git commands. Migrate from GitHub/GitLab with simple remote URL change.

⚖️ Why Choose CodeCommit for anihpj?
  • ✅ Zero Cost: First 5 developers free (perfect for small teams)
  • ✅ AWS Ecosystem: Native integration with CI/CD pipeline (CodePipeline → CodeBuild → CodeDeploy)
  • ✅ Security Compliance: Built-in encryption, IAM roles, and audit logs via CloudTrail
  • ✅ No Infrastructure: Fully managed - no Git servers to maintain or scale
  • ✅ Disaster Recovery: Automatic backups and multi-AZ redundancy
  • ✅ Fast Network: Repositories hosted in AWS = low latency for EC2/CodeBuild access
📊 Should You Use CodeCommit? Decision Tree
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              CODECOMMIT vs GITHUB/GITLAB - DECISION TREE                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                          │
│  START: Where should I host anihpj source code?                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    ├─ Q1: Is your entire infrastructure on AWS?                         │
│    │    ├─ YES → CodeCommit ✅ (Native integration)                     │
│    │    └─ NO  → Continue to Q2                                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    ├─ Q2: Do you need public open-source hosting?                       │
│    │    ├─ YES → GitHub ✅ (Public repos)                               │
│    │    └─ NO  → Continue to Q3                                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    ├─ Q3: Team size < 5 developers?                                     │
│    │    ├─ YES → CodeCommit ✅ (Free)                                   │
│    │    └─ NO  → Continue to Q4                                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    ├─ Q4: Need advanced features (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)?           │
│    │    ├─ YES → GitHub/GitLab ✅ (Rich features)                       │
│    │    └─ NO  → Continue to Q5                                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    ├─ Q5: Using AWS CodePipeline for CI/CD?                             │
│    │    ├─ YES → CodeCommit ✅ (Seamless integration)                   │
│    │    └─ NO  → Continue to Q6                                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    ├─ Q6: Need fine-grained IAM access control?                         │
│    │    ├─ YES → CodeCommit ✅ (IAM-based permissions)                  │
│    │    └─ NO  → Continue to Q7                                         │
│    │                                                                     │
│    └─ Q7: Prefer familiar GitHub UI/ecosystem?                          │
         ├─ YES → GitHub ✅ (Better UX)                                   │
         └─ NO  → CodeCommit ✅ (AWS-first approach)                      │
│                                                                          │
│  🎯 anihpj Recommendation:                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  Use CodeCommit for PRODUCTION private repos                   │    │
│  │  Reason: Free tier, native CI/CD, IAM security, no egress cost │    │
│  │                                                                 │    │
│  │  Use GitHub for PUBLIC/open-source or if need Actions         │    │
│  │  Hybrid: GitHub for public docs + CodeCommit for private code  │    │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🏗️ CodeCommit Architecture for anihpj Django
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       ANIHPJ GIT WORKFLOW WITH AWS CODECOMMIT                             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃   DEVELOPER   ┃   ┃   CODECOMMIT    ┃   ┃  CODEPIPELINE ┃   ┃  CODEBUILD   ┃   ┃ CODEDEPLOY ┃
┃   WORKSTATION ┃───▶┃   REPOSITORY    ┃───▶┃  AUTOMATION   ┃───▶┃  BUILD/TEST  ┃───▶┃  TO EC2    ┃
┃               ┃   ┃                 ┃   ┃               ┃   ┃              ┃   ┃            ┃
┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━┛

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 1: LOCAL DEVELOPMENT                                                                 │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                            │
│  Developer's Machine:                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │  ~/projects/anihpj/                                                          │          │
│  │  ├── jobpost/                    # Django app                                │          │
│  │  │   ├── models.py                # JobPost, Skills, Author models           │          │
│  │  │   ├── views.py                 # CRUD views                               │          │
│  │  │   └── urls.py                  # URL routing                              │          │
│  │  ├── manage.py                    # Django management                        │          │
│  │  ├── requirements.txt             # Dependencies                             │          │
│  │  ├── .gitignore                   # Ignore patterns                          │          │
│  │  └── README.md                    # Documentation                            │          │
│  │                                                                               │          │
│  │  $ git add .                                                                 │          │
│  │  $ git commit -m "Add job filtering by location"                            │          │
│  │  $ git push origin main  ──────────────────────────────────────────────▶    │          │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                        │
                                        │  HTTPS/SSH (IAM Auth)
                                        ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 2: AWS CODECOMMIT REPOSITORY                                                         │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                            │
│  Repository: anihpj-jobportal                                                              │
│  Region: us-east-1                                                                         │
│  Branches:                                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │  main          ← Production branch (protected)                               │          │
│  │  develop       ← Development branch                                          │          │
│  │  feature/*     ← Feature branches                                            │          │
│  │  hotfix/*      ← Emergency fixes                                             │          │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                                                                                            │
│  Security:                                                                                 │
│  ├─ Encryption at Rest: AWS KMS                                                           │
│  ├─ Encryption in Transit: TLS 1.2+                                                       │
│  ├─ IAM Permissions: Developer role = read/write, Manager role = approve                  │
│  └─ Audit Logging: CloudTrail tracks all git operations                                   │
│                                                                                            │
│  Triggers:                                                                                 │
│  ├─ On Push to main  → Trigger CodePipeline                                               │
│  ├─ On Pull Request  → Run CodeBuild tests                                                │
│  └─ On Tag Creation  → Deploy to production                                               │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                        │
                                        │  CloudWatch Event
                                        ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 3: AUTOMATED CI/CD PIPELINE                                                          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                            │
│  CodePipeline: anihpj-cicd-pipeline                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│  │                                                                                   │     │
│  │  [Source Stage]  ──▶  [Build Stage]  ──▶  [Deploy Stage]  ──▶  [Monitor]        │     │
│  │   CodeCommit          CodeBuild            CodeDeploy          CloudWatch        │     │
│  │                                                                                   │     │
│  │   • Fetch code        • pip install        • Blue/Green       • Metrics          │     │
│  │   • Detect changes    • Run tests          • Rolling deploy   • Alarms           │     │
│  │   • Create artifact   • Build package      • Health checks    • SNS alerts       │     │
│  │                                                                                   │     │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          📊 CODECOMMIT KEY FEATURES                                        │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                            │
│  🔒 SECURITY                     📦 STORAGE                   🔗 INTEGRATION              │
│  ├─ IAM-based access control    ├─ Unlimited repositories    ├─ CodePipeline             │
│  ├─ Encrypted at rest (KMS)     ├─ No size limits            ├─ CodeBuild                │
│  ├─ Encrypted in transit (SSL)  ├─ Large file support (LFS)  ├─ CodeDeploy               │
│  ├─ MFA support                  └─ Binary file storage      ├─ Lambda triggers          │
│  ├─ IP restriction policies                                  ├─ SNS notifications        │
│  └─ CloudTrail audit logs       💰 PRICING                    └─ CloudWatch events        │
│                                  ├─ $0 for 5 users                                        │
│  ⚡ PERFORMANCE                   ├─ $1/user/month after      🔄 GIT OPERATIONS           │
│  ├─ Low latency (AWS network)   ├─ $0.06/GB storage         ├─ clone, pull, push        │
│  ├─ Auto-scaling                 └─ Free AWS data transfer   ├─ branch, merge            │
│  ├─ 99.99% availability                                      ├─ tag, rebase              │
│  └─ Multi-AZ redundancy          🌍 GLOBAL AVAILABILITY       └─ All standard Git cmds    │
│                                  ├─ us-east-1, us-west-2                                  │
│                                  ├─ eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1                             │
│                                  └─ 15+ regions worldwide                                 │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🎓 2. Core Concepts & Terminology

1. Repository

A CodeCommit repository is a Git repository hosted in AWS. Each repository is isolated, secure, and can contain unlimited files.

Creating anihpj Repository: 
# Create repository via AWS CLI
aws codecommit create-repository \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --repository-description "Django job posting platform with JobPost, Skills, Author models" \
    --tags Team=Development,Project=anihpj,Environment=Production

# Response includes clone URLs:
# HTTPS: https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal
# SSH: ssh://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal
2. Branches & Branch Protection

CodeCommit supports all Git branching operations. You can protect branches to prevent direct pushes and require pull request approvals.

Branch Type Purpose anihpj Usage Protection Level
main Production code Deployed to EC2 production 🔒 Protected, requires approval
develop Integration branch Staging environment ⚠️ Semi-protected
feature/* New features Job filtering, search improvements ✅ Open
hotfix/* Emergency fixes Critical bug fixes 🚨 Fast-track approval
3. Pull Requests

Pull requests in CodeCommit allow you to review code changes before merging. Supports inline comments, approval workflows, and automatic merge checks.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    PULL REQUEST WORKFLOW                            │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│  1. Developer creates feature branch                                │
│     $ git checkout -b feature/job-location-filter                   │
│                                                                     │
│  2. Make changes and commit                                         │
│     $ git add jobpost/views.py                                      │
│     $ git commit -m "Add location-based job filtering"              │
│     $ git push origin feature/job-location-filter                   │
│                                                                     │
│  3. Create pull request in AWS Console or CLI                       │
│     Source: feature/job-location-filter                             │
│     Target: develop                                                 │
│     Reviewers: @team-lead, @senior-dev                              │
│                                                                     │
│  4. Code review process                                             │
│     ├─ Reviewers add inline comments                                │
│     ├─ Developer responds and makes changes                         │
│     └─ Automated tests run via CodeBuild                            │
│                                                                     │
│  5. Approval and merge                                              │
│     ├─ 2 approvals required                                         │
│     ├─ All comments resolved                                        │
│     ├─ Tests passing ✅                                             │
│     └─ Merge to develop                                             │
│                                                                     │
│  6. Auto-trigger CI/CD pipeline                                     │
│     develop → CodePipeline → CodeBuild → Deploy to Staging          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
4. Triggers & Notifications

CodeCommit can trigger AWS Lambda functions or SNS notifications on repository events (push, pull request creation, etc.).

Example: Notify Slack on Push 
{
  "repositoryName": "anihpj-jobportal",
  "triggers": [{
    "name": "NotifySlackOnPush",
    "destinationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-git-notifications",
    "events": ["all"],
    "branches": ["main", "develop"],
    "customData": "New commit to anihpj!"
  }]
}
5. IAM Permissions

Access to CodeCommit is controlled via IAM policies. You can grant fine-grained permissions for specific repositories, branches, or operations.

Permission Level Actions Allowed anihpj Use Case
Read-Only GitPull, ListBranches Junior devs, QA team
Developer GitPull, GitPush (feature branches) Full-time developers
Maintainer All Developer + CreatePullRequest, ApprovePullRequest Senior developers
Admin All + DeleteRepository, ModifyBranchProtection DevOps team
6. Authentication Methods

CodeCommit supports multiple authentication methods for secure access.

🔑 HTTPS (Git Credentials)

Generate username/password in IAM. Easy setup, works everywhere.

🔐 SSH Keys

Upload SSH public key to IAM. More secure, no password storage.

🎫 AWS CLI Credential Helper

Uses AWS credentials automatically. Best for automation/CI/CD.

🚀 3. Getting Started with CodeCommit

Prerequisites
  • ✅ AWS Account with IAM user
  • ✅ Git installed locally (v2.14+)
  • ✅ AWS CLI installed and configured
  • ✅ IAM permissions for CodeCommit
Step 1: Create Repository
# Via AWS Console: CodeCommit → Create Repository
# Or via CLI:

aws codecommit create-repository \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --repository-description "Job Portal - Django Application" \
    --region us-east-1
Step 2: Setup Git Credentials (HTTPS)
# Option A: Generate HTTPS Git Credentials
# 1. Go to IAM → Users → [Your User] → Security Credentials
# 2. Scroll to "HTTPS Git credentials for AWS CodeCommit"
# 3. Click "Generate credentials" and save username/password

# Configure git credential helper
git config --global credential.helper '!aws codecommit credential-helper $@'
git config --global credential.UseHttpPath true
Step 3: Clone Repository
# Get clone URL from AWS Console or CLI
aws codecommit get-repository --repository-name anihpj-jobportal

# Clone the repository
git clone https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal

cd anihpj-jobportal
Step 4: Make Your First Commit
# Add your anihpj Django project files
cp -r ~/anihpj-local/* .

# Create .gitignore for Django
cat > .gitignore << EOF
*.pyc
__pycache__/
db.sqlite3
media/
staticfiles/
.env
venv/
*.log
EOF

# Add and commit
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit: anihpj Django job portal"

# Push to CodeCommit
git push origin main
Step 5: (Optional) Migrate from GitHub
# If you already have code on GitHub, migrate it:

# Clone your existing GitHub repo
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/anihpj.git
cd anihpj

# Add CodeCommit as new remote
git remote add codecommit https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal

# Push all branches to CodeCommit
git push codecommit --all
git push codecommit --tags

# (Optional) Remove GitHub remote and set CodeCommit as origin
git remote remove origin
git remote rename codecommit origin
✅ Setup Complete! Your anihpj code is now hosted on CodeCommit. Next: Integrate with CodePipeline for automated CI/CD.

🔑 4. Key Features & Components

1. Encryption & Security

CodeCommit automatically encrypts your repositories at rest and in transit.

Security Feature Implementation Benefit
🔒 Encryption at Rest AWS KMS (Customer or AWS managed) Protects stored code from unauthorized access
🔐 Encryption in Transit TLS 1.2+ (HTTPS/SSH) Prevents man-in-the-middle attacks
👤 IAM Integration Fine-grained access policies Control who can read/write/delete
🔑 MFA Support Require MFA for sensitive operations Extra layer of authentication
📝 Audit Logging CloudTrail integration Track all repository actions
2. Branch Protection Rules

Protect your main branches from accidental or unauthorized changes.

Protect main branch for anihpj: 
# Via AWS CLI (no direct branch protection, use approval rules)
aws codecommit put-approval-rule-template \
    --approval-rule-template-name require-two-approvals \
    --approval-rule-template-content '{
      "Version": "2018-11-08",
      "DestinationReferences": ["refs/heads/main"],
      "Statements": [{
        "Type": "Approvers",
        "NumberOfApprovalsNeeded": 2,
        "ApprovalPoolMembers": [
          "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/senior-dev-1",
          "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/senior-dev-2",
          "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/tech-lead"
        ]
      }]
    }'
3. Code Comments & Reviews

Add inline comments to pull requests for effective code reviews.

4. Event Notifications

Get notified about repository events via SNS or trigger Lambda functions.

📧 Email Notifications

SNS → Email when code is pushed to main

💬 Slack Integration

Lambda → Slack webhook for PR events

🔔 CloudWatch Events

Trigger CodePipeline on commit

💰 5. Pricing & Cost Optimization

💵 Pricing Model
Tier Active Users Cost anihpj Monthly
Free Tier First 5 users ✅ $0 $0 (3 developers)
Additional Users User 6+ $1/user/month N/A
Storage N/A $0.06/GB-month ~$0.30 (5GB repo)
Git Requests N/A $0.001/request (after 2000 free) ~$0.10
📊 Cost Comparison: CodeCommit vs Alternatives
Service Free Tier 5 Users Cost 10 Users Cost Best For
🟢 AWS CodeCommit 5 users $0 $5/mo AWS-native teams, CI/CD
GitHub Private Unlimited $0 (Team: $44) $88/mo Public repos, GitHub Actions
GitLab SaaS Unlimited $95/mo (Premium) $190/mo Full DevOps platform
Bitbucket 5 users $0 $15/mo Atlassian ecosystem
💰 anihpj Cost Breakdown:
• 3 Developers: $0/month (within free tier)
• Storage: ~$0.30/month (5GB repository)
• Git Requests: ~$0.10/month
• Total Monthly Cost: $0.40/month 🎉
• vs GitHub Team: Save $43.60/month
• Annual Savings: $523.20/year

📈 6. Scalability & High Availability

⚡ Performance & Scalability

CodeCommit automatically scales to handle repositories of any size and team of any scale.

Metric Limit/Capability Notes
Repository Size Unlimited No hard limit on repo size
File Size 2 GB per file Use Git LFS for larger files
Concurrent Users Unlimited Auto-scales with demand
Branches Unlimited Create as many as needed
Pull Requests 1,000 open per repo Soft limit, contact AWS for more
Git Operations High throughput Optimized for fast clones/pushes
🌐 High Availability & Durability

CodeCommit is designed for 99.99% availability with automatic multi-AZ redundancy.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              CODECOMMIT HIGH AVAILABILITY ARCHITECTURE          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Region: us-east-1                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  Availability Zone A          Availability Zone B       │   │
│  │  ┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐      │   │
│  │  │ CodeCommit       │◄────────►│ CodeCommit       │      │   │
│  │  │ Replica 1        │  Sync   │ Replica 2        │      │   │
│  │  │                  │         │                  │      │   │
│  │  │ anihpj-jobportal │         │ anihpj-jobportal │      │   │
│  │  └──────────────────┘         └──────────────────┘      │   │
│  │           │                            │                 │   │
│  │           └────────────┬───────────────┘                 │   │
│  │                        │                                 │   │
│  │                   ┌────▼────┐                            │   │
│  │                   │ AWS KMS │                            │   │
│  │                   │ Encrypt │                            │   │
│  │                   └─────────┘                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  Features:                                                      │
│  ✅ Automatic replication across AZs                           │
│  ✅ No data loss during AZ failure                             │
│  ✅ Transparent failover                                        │
│  ✅ Continuous backup                                           │
│  ✅ Point-in-time recovery                                      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🎯 anihpj Benefit: Your job portal code is automatically protected across multiple data centers. If one AZ goes down, CodeCommit seamlessly fails over with zero data loss and no manual intervention required.

🎓 7. Advanced Topics

✅ Approval Rule Templates

Create reusable approval rules to enforce code review standards across all repositories.

Require 2 Approvals for anihpj Production: 
{
  "approvalRuleTemplateName": "require-two-senior-approvals",
  "approvalRuleTemplateDescription": "Require 2 senior dev approvals for main branch",
  "approvalRuleTemplateContent": {
    "Version": "2018-11-08",
    "DestinationReferences": ["refs/heads/main"],
    "Statements": [{
      "Type": "Approvers",
      "NumberOfApprovalsNeeded": 2,
      "ApprovalPoolMembers": [
        "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/alice-senior-dev",
        "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/bob-tech-lead",
        "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/carol-architect"
      ]
    }]
  }
}

# Apply to repository
aws codecommit associate-approval-rule-template-with-repository \
    --approval-rule-template-name require-two-senior-approvals \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal
📦 Git Large File Storage (LFS)

Store large files (images, videos, binaries) efficiently with Git LFS support.

# Install Git LFS
git lfs install

# Track large files (e.g., anihpj company logos, demo videos)
git lfs track "*.png"
git lfs track "*.mp4"
git lfs track "media/videos/*"

# Add .gitattributes
git add .gitattributes

# Commit as usual - LFS files stored separately
git add media/company-logos/
git commit -m "Add company logos via Git LFS"
git push origin main
🔗 Cross-Account Repository Access

Share repositories across AWS accounts for multi-account architectures.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           CROSS-ACCOUNT ACCESS SCENARIO                         │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Account A (Dev Team)              Account B (CI/CD)            │
│  ┌──────────────────┐             ┌──────────────────┐         │
│  │ CodeCommit       │             │ CodePipeline     │         │
│  │ anihpj-jobportal │◄────────────│ Build Pipeline   │         │
│  │                  │  IAM Role   │                  │         │
│  │ Repo             │  Assume     │ Access Repo      │         │
│  └──────────────────┘             └──────────────────┘         │
│                                                                 │
│  Steps:                                                         │
│  1. Create IAM role in Account A with CodeCommit permissions   │
│  2. Allow Account B to assume role                             │
│  3. Configure CodePipeline in Account B with assume role       │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
IAM Role Policy in Account A: 
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Principal": {
      "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::999888777666:root"
    },
    "Action": "sts:AssumeRole"
  }]
}

// Attach policy allowing CodeCommit access
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": [
      "codecommit:GitPull",
      "codecommit:GetBranch",
      "codecommit:GetCommit"
    ],
    "Resource": "arn:aws:codecommit:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobportal"
  }]
}
🔒 VPC Endpoints for Private Access

Access CodeCommit privately from VPC without traversing the internet.

# Create VPC endpoint for CodeCommit
aws ec2 create-vpc-endpoint \
    --vpc-id vpc-abc123 \
    --service-name com.amazonaws.us-east-1.codecommit \
    --route-table-ids rtb-xyz789 \
    --subnet-ids subnet-123abc

# Configure git to use VPC endpoint
git config --global url."https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.vpce-abc123.codecommit.us-east-1.vpce.amazonaws.com/".insteadOf \
    "https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/"

✨ 8. Best Practices

🔒 Security Best Practices
  • ✅ Use IAM roles instead of IAM users for application access
  • ✅ Enable MFA for users with delete permissions
  • ✅ Use SSH keys instead of HTTPS credentials when possible
  • ✅ Rotate credentials regularly (every 90 days)
  • ✅ Enable CloudTrail logging for audit compliance
  • ✅ Use VPC endpoints for private repository access
  • ✅ Encrypt sensitive files before committing (use git-crypt)
  • ✅ Never commit secrets, API keys, or passwords
⚠️ Security Alert: Always use .gitignore to exclude .env, settings_local.py, and credential files from anihpj commits. Use AWS Secrets Manager for production credentials.
🔄 Workflow Best Practices
Practice Recommendation anihpj Example
Branch Strategy Use Git Flow: main, develop, feature/* feature/job-filters, hotfix/login-bug
Commit Messages Use conventional commits format feat: Add location-based job search
Pull Requests Always create PR for main/develop Require 2 approvals before merge
Code Reviews Review within 24 hours Senior dev reviews Django models changes
Branch Protection Protect main and develop branches No direct pushes, only via approved PRs
👥 Team Collaboration
📝 Clear Commit Messages

Use descriptive messages: "Fix job search pagination bug" instead of "fix bug"

🏷️ Tag Releases

Tag production releases: v1.0.0, v1.1.0 for easy rollback and tracking

📚 Document Changes

Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md with each significant change

🧪 Test Before Push

Run tests locally before pushing to avoid breaking CI/CD pipeline

📦 Repository Management
  • ✅ Keep repositories focused: One repo per service/app (anihpj-web, anihpj-api)
  • ✅ Use .gitignore effectively: Exclude build artifacts, dependencies, IDEs
  • ✅ Regular cleanup: Delete merged feature branches to reduce clutter
  • ✅ Archive old repos: Instead of deleting, archive inactive repositories
  • ✅ Consistent naming: Use kebab-case for repo names (anihpj-jobportal)
  • ✅ Tag important milestones: v1.0-production, v2.0-major-refactor
Recommended .gitignore for anihpj Django: 
# Python
*.pyc
__pycache__/
*.py[cod]
*$py.class
venv/
env/
.Python

# Django
*.log
db.sqlite3
media/
staticfiles/
.env
settings_local.py

# IDEs
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Testing
.coverage
htmlcov/
.pytest_cache/

💼 9. Use Cases

🐍 Django Web Applications (anihpj)

Perfect for managing Django projects with multiple environments and team collaboration.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         ANIHPJ DJANGO PROJECT STRUCTURE IN CODECOMMIT           │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Repository: anihpj-jobportal                                   │
│  ├── main (production)                                          │
│  ├── develop (staging)                                          │
│  ├── feature/employer-dashboard                                 │
│  ├── feature/job-recommendations                                │
│  └── hotfix/email-notifications                                 │
│                                                                 │
│  Structure:                                                     │
│  anihpj-jobportal/                                              │
│  ├── anihpj/              # Django project settings             │
│  │   ├── settings.py     # Base settings                       │
│  │   ├── settings_prod.py # Production config                   │
│  │   └── urls.py         # URL routing                         │
│  ├── jobpost/            # Job posting app                      │
│  │   ├── models.py       # Job, Company, Application models    │
│  │   ├── views.py        # View logic                          │
│  │   └── templates/      # HTML templates                      │
│  ├── requirements.txt    # Django, psycopg2, boto3             │
│  ├── manage.py                                                  │
│  └── .gitignore          # Exclude db.sqlite3, .env            │
│                                                                 │
│  CI/CD Integration:                                             │
│  CodeCommit → CodePipeline → CodeBuild → Elastic Beanstalk     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
✅ anihpj Success Story: By using CodeCommit with CodePipeline, the anihpj team automated deployments and reduced deployment time from 30 minutes to 3 minutes while saving $500+/year compared to GitHub Teams plan.
🔧 Microservices Architecture

Manage multiple service repositories with consistent workflows and cross-repo triggers.

Multi-Repo Setup for anihpj Microservices: 
# Create separate repos for each service
aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-auth-service
aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-job-service
aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-notification-service
aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-analytics-service

# Each service has independent CI/CD pipeline
# Triggers on push → Build → Test → Deploy to Lambda/ECS
📁 Monorepo Pattern

Single repository containing multiple projects with shared code and dependencies.

anihpj-monorepo/
├── packages/
│   ├── web/              # Django web app
│   ├── api/              # FastAPI backend
│   ├── mobile/           # React Native app
│   └── shared/           # Shared utilities
├── infrastructure/       # Terraform/CDK
├── docs/                 # Documentation
└── .gitignore

# Benefits:
# - Atomic commits across projects
# - Shared code reusability
# - Unified version control
# - Simplified dependency management

🔧 10. Troubleshooting

❌ Common Errors & Solutions
Error Cause Solution
fatal: unable to access Invalid credentials Run aws configure or regenerate Git credentials
EncryptionKeyAccessDeniedException Missing KMS permissions Add kms:Decrypt to IAM policy
Repository does not exist Wrong region or repo name Verify region in URL: us-east-1
RepositoryNameExistsException Duplicate repo name Use unique name or delete old repo first
PullRequestAlreadyClosedException Updating closed PR Create new PR instead
File too large File exceeds 2 GB limit Use Git LFS: git lfs track "*.mp4"
🐞 Debugging Tips
# Enable Git verbose output
GIT_CURL_VERBOSE=1 git clone https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal

# Test AWS credentials
aws codecommit list-repositories

# Verify IAM permissions
aws iam get-user
aws iam list-user-policies --user-name your-username

# Check Git config
git config --list | grep codecommit

# Test SSH connection
ssh git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

# View CloudTrail logs for CodeCommit events
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
    --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=ResourceType,AttributeValue=AWS::CodeCommit::Repository \
    --max-results 10
❓ Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Can I migrate from GitHub?

Yes! Use git remote add to add CodeCommit as new origin and push all branches.

Q: Is Git LFS supported?

Yes, CodeCommit fully supports Git LFS for large files (videos, models, datasets).

Q: Can I use git hooks?

Yes, use client-side hooks locally. For server-side, use CodeCommit triggers with Lambda.

Q: What about code search?

Use AWS CLI or integrate with CodeGuru Reviewer for advanced code search and analysis.

Q: Can I use with Jenkins?

Yes! Install AWS CodeCommit plugin for Jenkins or use git CLI directly in build jobs.

Q: How do I backup repositories?

Clone repos regularly or use AWS Backup to automate CodeCommit repository backups.

💻 11. API & CLI Reference

⌨️ Essential AWS CLI Commands
Repository Management: 
# Create repository
aws codecommit create-repository \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --repository-description "Django job portal application"

# List repositories
aws codecommit list-repositories

# Get repository details
aws codecommit get-repository \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal

# Delete repository (CAREFUL!)
aws codecommit delete-repository \
    --repository-name old-project

# Update repository description
aws codecommit update-repository-description \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --repository-description "Updated Django job portal with AI recommendations"
Branch Operations: 
# List branches
aws codecommit list-branches \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal

# Get branch details
aws codecommit get-branch \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --branch-name main

# Create branch from commit
aws codecommit create-branch \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --branch-name feature/new-search \
    --commit-id abc123def456

# Delete branch
aws codecommit delete-branch \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --branch-name feature/old-feature
Pull Request Commands: 
# Create pull request
aws codecommit create-pull-request \
    --title "Add job recommendation algorithm" \
    --description "Implements ML-based job matching for anihpj users" \
    --targets repositoryName=anihpj-jobportal,sourceReference=feature/recommendations,destinationReference=develop

# List pull requests
aws codecommit list-pull-requests \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --pull-request-status OPEN

# Get pull request details
aws codecommit get-pull-request \
    --pull-request-id 47

# Merge pull request
aws codecommit merge-pull-request-by-fast-forward \
    --pull-request-id 47 \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal
Trigger Management: 
# Create trigger for Lambda notification
aws codecommit put-repository-triggers \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --triggers file://trigger-config.json

# trigger-config.json:
{
  "triggers": [{
    "name": "NotifyOnPush",
    "destinationArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:NotifyTeam",
    "events": ["all"]
  }]
}

# List triggers
aws codecommit get-repository-triggers \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal
🐍 Python Boto3 SDK Examples
Automate Repository Operations for anihpj: 
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

codecommit = boto3.client('codecommit', region_name='us-east-1')

# Create repository
response = codecommit.create_repository(
    repositoryName='anihpj-jobportal',
    repositoryDescription='Django job portal application'
)
print(f"Repository created: {response['repositoryMetadata']['cloneUrlHttp']}")

# List all repositories
repos = codecommit.list_repositories()
for repo in repos['repositories']:
    print(f"- {repo['repositoryName']}")

# Get commits from last 30 days
from datetime import timedelta
since = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30)

commits = codecommit.get-commit-history(
    repositoryName='anihpj-jobportal',
    afterDate=since
)

# Create pull request programmatically
pr = codecommit.create_pull_request(
    title='Add employer verification feature',
    description='Implements email verification for employers posting jobs',
    targets=[{
        'repositoryName': 'anihpj-jobportal',
        'sourceReference': 'feature/employer-verification',
        'destinationReference': 'develop'
    }]
)
print(f"Pull Request created: #{pr['pullRequest']['pullRequestId']}")

# Approve pull request
codecommit.update_pull_request_approval_state(
    pullRequestId=pr['pullRequest']['pullRequestId'],
    revisionId=pr['pullRequest']['revisionId'],
    approvalState='APPROVE'
)

# Post comment on PR
codecommit.post_comment_for_pull_request(
    pullRequestId=pr['pullRequest']['pullRequestId'],
    repositoryName='anihpj-jobportal',
    beforeCommitId=pr['pullRequest']['pullRequestTargets'][0]['sourceCommit'],
    afterCommitId=pr['pullRequest']['pullRequestTargets'][0]['destinationCommit'],
    content='LGTM! Great work on the employer verification flow. 🚀'
)

🔗 12. AWS Service Integration

⚙️ CodePipeline Integration

Automatically trigger CI/CD pipelines when code is pushed to CodeCommit repositories.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│       ANIHPJ CI/CD PIPELINE WITH CODECOMMIT                     │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐     │
│  │ CodeCommit   │───►│ CodePipeline │───►│ CodeBuild    │     │
│  │              │    │              │    │              │     │
│  │ Push to main │    │ Detect change│    │ Build Django │     │
│  │              │    │ Start pipeline    │ Run tests    │     │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────┬───────┘     │
│                                                  │              │
│                                         ┌────────▼───────┐     │
│                                         │ CodeDeploy     │     │
│                                         │                │     │
│                                         │ Deploy to ECS  │     │
│                                         │ Blue/Green     │     │
│                                         └────────────────┘     │
│                                                                 │
│  Benefits:                                                      │
│  ✅ Automated testing on every commit                          │
│  ✅ Zero-downtime deployments                                  │
│  ✅ Rollback on failure                                        │
│  ✅ Full audit trail                                           │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Create Pipeline for anihpj: 
# Create pipeline that watches CodeCommit
aws codepipeline create-pipeline --cli-input-json file://pipeline-config.json

# pipeline-config.json:
{
  "pipeline": {
    "name": "anihpj-production-pipeline",
    "roleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodePipelineServiceRole",
    "stages": [
      {
        "name": "Source",
        "actions": [{
          "name": "SourceAction",
          "actionTypeId": {
            "category": "Source",
            "owner": "AWS",
            "provider": "CodeCommit",
            "version": "1"
          },
          "configuration": {
            "RepositoryName": "anihpj-jobportal",
            "BranchName": "main",
            "PollForSourceChanges": false
          },
          "outputArtifacts": [{"name": "SourceOutput"}]
        }]
      },
      {
        "name": "Build",
        "actions": [{
          "name": "BuildAction",
          "actionTypeId": {
            "category": "Build",
            "owner": "AWS",
            "provider": "CodeBuild",
            "version": "1"
          },
          "inputArtifacts": [{"name": "SourceOutput"}],
          "outputArtifacts": [{"name": "BuildOutput"}]
        }]
      }
    ]
  }
}
🏗️ CodeBuild Integration

Build and test Django applications directly from CodeCommit source code.

buildspec.yml for anihpj Django: 
version: 0.2

phases:
  install:
    runtime-versions:
      python: 3.11
    commands:
      - echo "Installing dependencies for anihpj..."
      - pip install -r requirements.txt
      
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Running Django tests..."
      - python manage.py test
      - echo "Checking code style..."
      - flake8 jobpost/ anihpj/
      
  build:
    commands:
      - echo "Collecting static files..."
      - python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
      - echo "Running migrations..."
      - python manage.py migrate --check
      - echo "Building Docker image..."
      - docker build -t anihpj-jobportal:latest .
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Build completed successfully"
      - docker tag anihpj-jobportal:latest $ECR_REPO:$CODEBUILD_BUILD_NUMBER
      - docker push $ECR_REPO:$CODEBUILD_BUILD_NUMBER

artifacts:
  files:
    - '**/*'
  name: anihpj-build-$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
⚡ Lambda Integration

Trigger Lambda functions on CodeCommit events (pushes, PR updates, comments).

Lambda Function for Slack Notifications: 
import json
import boto3
import os
from urllib.request import Request, urlopen

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Notify Slack channel when code is pushed to anihpj-jobportal
    """
    codecommit = boto3.client('codecommit')
    
    # Parse CodeCommit event
    for record in event['Records']:
        repo_name = record['eventSourceARN'].split(':')[-1]
        branch = record['codecommit']['references'][0]['ref'].split('/')[-1]
        commit_id = record['codecommit']['references'][0]['commit']
        
        # Get commit details
        commit = codecommit.get_commit(
            repositoryName=repo_name,
            commitId=commit_id
        )
        
        author = commit['commit']['author']['name']
        message = commit['commit']['message']
        
        # Send to Slack
        slack_message = {
            "text": f"🚀 New commit to *{repo_name}* ({branch})",
            "attachments": [{
                "color": "#36a64f",
                "fields": [
                    {"title": "Author", "value": author, "short": True},
                    {"title": "Commit", "value": commit_id[:7], "short": True},
                    {"title": "Message", "value": message}
                ]
            }]
        }
        
        req = Request(os.environ['SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL'], 
                     data=json.dumps(slack_message).encode('utf-8'),
                     headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})
        urlopen(req)
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}
📊 SNS & CloudWatch Integration

Monitor repository activity and send notifications through SNS topics.

# Create SNS topic for CodeCommit notifications
aws sns create-topic --name anihpj-codecommit-notifications

# Subscribe email to topic
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-codecommit-notifications \
    --protocol email \
    --notification-endpoint team@anihpj.com

# Create CloudWatch Event Rule for CodeCommit pushes
aws events put-rule \
    --name anihpj-codecommit-push-rule \
    --event-pattern '{
      "source": ["aws.codecommit"],
      "detail-type": ["CodeCommit Repository State Change"],
      "detail": {
        "event": ["referenceCreated", "referenceUpdated"],
        "repositoryName": ["anihpj-jobportal"]
      }
    }'

# Add SNS as target
aws events put-targets \
    --rule anihpj-codecommit-push-rule \
    --targets "Id"="1","Arn"="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-codecommit-notifications"

🚀 13. Migration Strategies

🔄 Migrating from GitHub

Complete step-by-step guide to migrate anihpj repository from GitHub to CodeCommit.

Method 1: Mirror Migration (Preserves All History): 
# Step 1: Clone existing GitHub repo as bare mirror
git clone --mirror https://github.com/your-org/anihpj-jobportal.git
cd anihpj-jobportal.git

# Step 2: Create CodeCommit repository
aws codecommit create-repository \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --repository-description "Migrated from GitHub"

# Step 3: Add CodeCommit as remote
git remote add codecommit https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal

# Step 4: Push all branches and tags
git push codecommit --all
git push codecommit --tags

# Step 5: Verify migration
aws codecommit list-branches --repository-name anihpj-jobportal

# Step 6: Update team's local clones
cd /path/to/local/anihpj-jobportal
git remote set-url origin https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal
git fetch origin
git branch -u origin/main main
Method 2: Fresh Start (Clean Migration): 
# Clone GitHub repo normally
git clone https://github.com/your-org/anihpj-jobportal.git
cd anihpj-jobportal

# Create CodeCommit repo
aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-jobportal

# Change remote to CodeCommit
git remote remove origin
git remote add origin https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal

# Push main branch only (start fresh)
git push -u origin main
⚠️ Migration Checklist:
  • ✅ Notify team 48 hours before migration
  • ✅ Update CI/CD pipelines to use CodeCommit
  • ✅ Update webhook integrations (Slack, Jira)
  • ✅ Archive GitHub repo (don't delete immediately)
  • ✅ Update documentation with new clone URLs
  • ✅ Test deployment pipeline with CodeCommit
🦊 Migrating from GitLab
# Same process as GitHub migration
git clone --mirror https://gitlab.com/your-org/anihpj-jobportal.git
cd anihpj-jobportal.git

aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-jobportal

git remote add codecommit https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal
git push codecommit --all
git push codecommit --tags
🪣 Migrating from Bitbucket
# Clone Bitbucket repo
git clone --mirror https://bitbucket.org/your-org/anihpj-jobportal.git
cd anihpj-jobportal.git

# Create CodeCommit repo and push
aws codecommit create-repository --repository-name anihpj-jobportal
git remote add codecommit https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-jobportal
git push codecommit --all
git push codecommit --tags
💡 Post-Migration Best Practice: Keep GitHub/GitLab repo as read-only archive for 30 days. Add README stating: "This repository has been migrated to AWS CodeCommit. New URL: [CodeCommit HTTPS URL]"

🔒 14. Compliance & Security

📝 Audit Logging with CloudTrail

Track all CodeCommit API calls for security audits and compliance requirements.

Enable CloudTrail for CodeCommit: 
# Create CloudTrail trail for CodeCommit events
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
    --name anihpj-codecommit-audit \
    --s3-bucket-name anihpj-audit-logs

# Enable logging
aws cloudtrail start-logging --name anihpj-codecommit-audit

# Query recent CodeCommit events
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
    --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=ResourceType,AttributeValue=AWS::CodeCommit::Repository \
    --max-results 20 \
    --query 'Events[*].[EventTime,EventName,Username,Resources[0].ResourceName]' \
    --output table

# Example output:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------
# |  EventTime           | EventName      | Username  | Repo     |
# |----------------------|----------------|-----------|----------|
# | 2024-01-15 14:32:10  | GitPush        | alice     | anihpj   |
# | 2024-01-15 13:15:42  | CreateBranch   | bob       | anihpj   |
# | 2024-01-14 16:20:33  | DeleteBranch   | carol     | anihpj   |
# ----------------------------------------------------------------
Event Type CloudTrail EventName Use Case
Code Push GitPush Track who pushed code and when
Branch Created CreateBranch Monitor branch creation patterns
Branch Deleted DeleteBranch Audit branch deletions for compliance
PR Merged MergePullRequest Track code review approvals
Repo Deleted DeleteRepository Alert on accidental deletions
✅ Compliance Standards

CodeCommit meets various compliance requirements for regulated industries.

🏥 HIPAA Compliant

Eligible for HIPAA workloads when used with BAA. Encrypted at rest and in transit.

💳 PCI DSS

Meets PCI DSS requirements for storing payment card data in code repositories.

🛡️ SOC 1/2/3

Covered under AWS SOC compliance programs for audit controls.

🌍 ISO 27001

ISO 27001 certified for information security management.

🇪🇺 GDPR Ready

Supports GDPR compliance with data residency and encryption controls.

🏛️ FedRAMP

Available in GovCloud regions for US government workloads.

🛡️ Data Protection & Encryption
Comprehensive Security Setup for anihpj: 
# 1. Create KMS key for encryption
aws kms create-key \
    --description "CodeCommit encryption key for anihpj" \
    --key-policy file://kms-policy.json

# 2. Create repository with encryption
aws codecommit create-repository \
    --repository-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --kms-key-id alias/codecommit-anihpj

# 3. Enable MFA delete protection (via IAM policy)
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Deny",
    "Action": [
      "codecommit:DeleteRepository",
      "codecommit:DeleteBranch"
    ],
    "Resource": "*",
    "Condition": {
      "BoolIfExists": {"aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "false"}
    }
  }]
}

# 4. Restrict access by IP address
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Deny",
    "Action": "codecommit:*",
    "Resource": "*",
    "Condition": {
      "NotIpAddress": {
        "aws:SourceIp": ["203.0.113.0/24", "198.51.100.0/24"]
      }
    }
  }]
}

# 5. Enable versioning for audit S3 bucket
aws s3api put-bucket-versioning \
    --bucket anihpj-audit-logs \
    --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
🎉 Security Highlights:
  • ✅ All data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
  • ✅ IAM-based access control with MFA support
  • ✅ Full CloudTrail audit logs retained for 7 years
  • ✅ Automatic security patches (AWS-managed)
  • ✅ No public internet exposure (private VPC endpoints available)
  • ✅ Compliant with major standards (HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC, ISO)
🎯 anihpj Security Posture: By using CodeCommit with KMS encryption, CloudTrail logging, and IAM roles, your job portal code is protected with enterprise-grade security that would cost $1000s/month with other providers. You get bank-level security for FREE (5 active users).
🎉 You're Now a CodeCommit Expert!

You've learned everything about AWS CodeCommit - from basic Git operations to advanced security configurations. Here's what makes CodeCommit perfect for anihpj:

  • 💰 Cost: FREE for 5 users = $500+/year savings vs GitHub
  • 🔒 Security: Enterprise-grade encryption + compliance (HIPAA, PCI DSS)
  • ⚡ Performance: Unlimited repos, auto-scaling, 99.99% SLA
  • 🔗 Integration: Native AWS integration (CodePipeline, Lambda, ECS)
  • 🛡️ Control: IAM policies, VPC endpoints, CloudTrail auditing

🚀 Next Steps for anihpj:

  1. Migrate your Django job portal from GitHub to CodeCommit
  2. Set up CI/CD pipeline with CodePipeline + CodeBuild
  3. Configure approval rules for main branch protection
  4. Enable CloudTrail logging for compliance tracking
  5. Integrate Slack notifications via Lambda triggers

Happy coding with CodeCommit! 🎊

AWS CloudWatch

Category: Management & Governance, Monitoring  |  Use Case: Application Monitoring, Logging, Alerting  |  Pricing: Free tier + Pay-as-you-go  |  Last Updated: January 2026  |  Documentation: AWS CloudWatch Docs

Observability service for monitoring AWS resources and applications with metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards for proactive issue detection.

Real-Time Monitoring Centralized Logging Smart Alarms Custom Dashboards AWS Native
5 GB
FREE Logs Ingestion
10 Metrics
FREE Custom Metrics
10 Alarms
FREE per Month
1M API
FREE Requests/Month
3 Dashboards
FREE Custom Dashboards
1-Second
High-Resolution Metrics
💡 Perfect for anihpj: Monitor Django application health, track job posting metrics, analyze user behavior, set up alerts for errors, and keep logs centralized - all in one place. FREE tier covers most small to medium applications!

📋 Overview & Introduction

AWS CloudWatch is a comprehensive monitoring and observability service that provides data and actionable insights for AWS resources, applications, and services. It collects monitoring and operational data in the form of logs, metrics, and events, giving you a unified view of your entire infrastructure.

🎯 CloudWatch at a Glance for anihpj
📊 Primary Use: Monitor Django app health, logs, and performance metrics
💰 Cost (anihpj): ~$22/month (vs $55+ for Datadog)
⏱️ Setup Time: 30-60 minutes (agent + logging configuration)
🎓 Learning Curve: Moderate - Familiar if you know AWS Console
🔧 Best For: AWS-native apps, cost-conscious teams, compliance needs
🎯 Key Benefits
Benefit Description anihpj Example
📊 Unified Monitoring Single pane of glass for all resources View EC2, RDS, ALB metrics together
⚡ Real-Time Insights Monitor metrics with 1-second granularity Detect traffic spikes instantly
🤖 Automated Actions Trigger auto-scaling, notifications Scale EC2 when CPU > 80%
📝 Centralized Logs Aggregate logs from all services Django + Nginx + PostgreSQL logs
📈 Custom Dashboards Visual metrics in real-time Job postings, user signups dashboard
💰 Cost Optimization Track resource utilization Identify idle EC2 instances
💡 Quick Start for anihpj:
  1. Enable CloudWatch agent on EC2 instances
  2. Send Django application logs to CloudWatch Logs
  3. Create custom metrics for job postings (views, applications)
  4. Set up alarms for high CPU, errors, slow response times
  5. Build dashboard to visualize anihpj health in real-time
⚖️ CloudWatch vs. Alternative Monitoring Solutions
Solution Pros Cons Monthly Cost Best For
✅ CloudWatch • Native AWS
• Auto metrics
• FREE tier		 • Basic UI
• Query learning curve		 $22 (anihpj) AWS-native apps
Datadog • Best UI/UX
• Great APM
• Multi-cloud		 • Expensive
• External SaaS		 $55+ Large teams, complex apps
New Relic • Powerful APM
• Microservices		 • Expensive
• Learning curve		 $99 Enterprise APM needs
Prometheus + Grafana • Open-source
• FREE
• Flexible		 • Self-host
• Maintenance		 FREE (+ EC2 costs) K8s, open-source fans
Splunk • Enterprise-grade
• Powerful logs		 • Very expensive
• Overkill for SMBs		 $150+ Enterprise, compliance
ELK Stack • Open-source
• Great for logs		 • Complex setup
• Requires hosting		 FREE (+ EC2 costs) Log-heavy workloads
CloudWatch:

Native AWS, automatic metrics, FREE tier generous, tight integration

Datadog:

Best UI/UX, great APM, expensive ($55+/month), external service

New Relic:

Powerful APM, good for microservices, $99/month, learning curve

Prometheus + Grafana:

Open-source, FREE, flexible, requires self-hosting & maintenance

Splunk:

Enterprise-grade, powerful log analysis, very expensive ($150+/month)

ELK Stack:

Open-source, great for logs, complex setup, EC2 costs for hosting

📊 CloudWatch Metrics

Collect and track metrics for AWS resources and custom applications

📝 CloudWatch Logs

Centralized log storage, analysis, and retention management

🚨 CloudWatch Alarms

Automated notifications and actions based on metric thresholds

📈 CloudWatch Dashboards

Visual representation of metrics and logs in real-time

🔍 CloudWatch Insights

Query and analyze logs using powerful SQL-like syntax

⚡ CloudWatch Events

Respond to state changes in AWS resources with automation

🤔 Why Choose CloudWatch for anihpj?
  • ✅ Native AWS Integration: Automatic metrics from EC2, RDS, Lambda, ALB
  • ✅ Django-Friendly: Easy to send custom logs and metrics from Python
  • ✅ Proactive Monitoring: Detect issues before users complain
  • ✅ Debug Production: Quickly find errors in logs without SSH
  • ✅ Cost Visibility: Track which resources cost the most
  • ✅ Compliance: Retain logs for audit requirements (HIPAA, SOC 2)
🏗️ CloudWatch in anihpj Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        ANIHPJ CLOUDWATCH MONITORING ARCHITECTURE                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                           AWS CLOUD ENVIRONMENT                               │  │
│  │                                                                               │  │
│  │  ┌─────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐       ┌─────────────────┐  │  │
│  │  │  EC2 Instance   │───────►│  CloudWatch      │◄──────│  RDS PostgreSQL │  │  │
│  │  │  (Django App)   │ Metrics│  Metrics         │ Stats │  (Database)     │  │  │
│  │  │                 │        │                  │       │                 │  │  │
│  │  │  • CPU Usage    │        │  • CPU %         │       │  • Connections  │  │  │
│  │  │  • Memory       │        │  • Disk I/O      │       │  • Query Time   │  │  │

│  │  └─────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘       └─────────────────┘  │  │
│  │           │                          │                          │            │  │
│  │           │ Django Logs              │ Custom Metrics           │ DB Logs    │  │
│  │           ▼                          ▼                          ▼            │  │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │  │
│  │  │                        CloudWatch Logs                                 │  │  │
│  │  │  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐                │  │  │
│  │  │  │ /var/log/    │  │ Django       │  │ PostgreSQL   │                │  │  │
│  │  │  │ django/      │  │ Application  │  │ Slow Queries │                │  │  │
│  │  │  │ access.log   │  │ Errors       │  │              │                │  │  │
│  │  │  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘                │  │  │
│  │  │                                                                        │  │  │
│  │  │  Query with CloudWatch Logs Insights:                                 │  │  │
│  │  │  • "Find all 500 errors in last hour"                                 │  │  │
│  │  │  • "Which job postings got most views?"                               │  │  │
│  │  │  • "Show slow database queries"                                       │  │  │
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │  │
│  │                                  │                                           │  │
│  │                                  ▼                                           │  │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │  │
│  │  │                        CloudWatch Alarms                               │  │  │

│  │  │  │ CPU > 80%    │  │ 500 Errors   │  │ DB Connections│                │  │  │
│  │  │  │ → Auto Scale │  │ → SNS Alert  │  │ → SNS + Slack│                │  │  │

│  │  │  │                                                                 │   │  │  │
│  │  │  │  [CPU]  [Memory]  [Requests/min]  [Error Rate]  [DB Latency]  │   │  │  │
