# anihpj-simple-stack.yaml (updated)
Resources:
  AnihpjStaticBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "anihpj-static-${EnvironmentName}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      VersioningConfiguration:  # ← NEW: Enable versioning
        Status: Enabled
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      LifecycleConfiguration:  # ← NEW: Delete old versions
        Rules:
          - Id: DeleteOldVersions
            Status: Enabled
            NoncurrentVersionExpirationInDays: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "anihpj-static-${EnvironmentName}"
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
        - Key: Application
          Value: anihpj
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation
Create Change Set (Preview Changes)
# Create change set to preview what will change
aws cloudformation create-change-set \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage \
    --change-set-name enable-versioning \
    --template-body file://anihpj-simple-stack.yaml \
    --parameters ParameterKey=EnvironmentName,ParameterValue=dev

# Wait for change set creation
aws cloudformation wait change-set-create-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage \
    --change-set-name enable-versioning

# Describe the change set (see what will change)
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage \
    --change-set-name enable-versioning \
    --query 'Changes[*].[ResourceChange.Action,ResourceChange.LogicalResourceId,ResourceChange.ResourceType,ResourceChange.Replacement]' \
    --output table

# Output:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------
# |                    DescribeChangeSet                        |
# +--------+--------------------+------------------+-------------+
# | Action | LogicalResourceId  | ResourceType     | Replacement |
# +--------+--------------------+------------------+-------------+
# | Modify | AnihpjStaticBucket | AWS::S3::Bucket  | False       |
# +--------+--------------------+------------------+-------------+

# ✅ Safe change: Modifies existing bucket, no replacement
# ⚠️  If Replacement=True, resource would be deleted and recreated!

# Execute the change set (apply changes)
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage \
    --change-set-name enable-versioning

# Wait for update to complete
aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage

# ✅ Stack updated successfully with versioning enabled!
🗑️ Delete Stack (Cleanup)
Delete Stack and All Resources
# Delete the entire stack
aws cloudformation delete-stack \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage

# Wait for deletion to complete
aws cloudformation wait stack-delete-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage

# Verify stack is deleted
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name anihpj-dev-storage
# Error: Stack with id anihpj-dev-storage does not exist

# ✅ Stack and all resources deleted
# ✅ S3 bucket removed (if empty)
# ✅ No more charges

# ⚠️  Note: If S3 bucket has objects, deletion will fail!
# You need to empty bucket first or set DeletionPolicy
🏆 Complete Production Template for anihpj
Full Django Stack Template (anihpj-production-stack.yaml)
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "Complete production infrastructure for anihpj Django job portal"

# ============================================================================
# PARAMETERS - Values provided at stack creation
# ============================================================================
Parameters:
  EnvironmentName:
    Type: String
    Default: production
    AllowedValues: [dev, staging, production]
    Description: "Environment name"

  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues: [t3.micro, t3.small, t3.medium, m5.large, m5.xlarge]
    Description: "EC2 instance type for web servers"

  DBInstanceClass:
    Type: String
    Default: db.t3.medium
    AllowedValues: [db.t3.micro, db.t3.small, db.t3.medium, db.m5.large]
    Description: "RDS instance type"

  DBPassword:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    MinLength: 12
    Description: "Database master password (min 12 characters)"

  KeyPairName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: "EC2 Key Pair for SSH access"

  SSLCertificateArn:
    Type: String
    Description: "ACM certificate ARN for HTTPS"
    Default: ""

# ============================================================================
# MAPPINGS - Region-specific configurations
# ============================================================================
Mappings:
  RegionMap:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0c7217cdde317cfec  # Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-0d70546e43a941d70
    eu-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0d71ea30463e0ff8d

# ============================================================================
# CONDITIONS - Control resource creation
# ============================================================================
Conditions:
  IsProduction: !Equals [!Ref EnvironmentName, production]
  HasSSLCertificate: !Not [!Equals [!Ref SSLCertificateArn, ""]]
  CreateMultiAZ: !Condition IsProduction

# ============================================================================
# RESOURCES - AWS infrastructure components
# ============================================================================
Resources:

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # VPC NETWORKING
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  AnihpjVPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-vpc"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-igw"

  AttachGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  # Public Subnets (for ALB)
  PublicSubnetA:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs ""]
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-public-a"

  PublicSubnetB:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.2.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs ""]
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-public-b"

  # Private Subnets (for EC2, RDS)
  PrivateSubnetA:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.11.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs ""]
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-private-a"

  PrivateSubnetB:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.12.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs ""]
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-private-b"

  # NAT Gateway for private subnet internet access
  NATGatewayEIP:
    Type: AWS::EC2::EIP
    DependsOn: AttachGateway
    Properties:
      Domain: vpc

  NATGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NatGateway
    Properties:
      AllocationId: !GetAtt NATGatewayEIP.AllocationId
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnetA
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-nat"

  # Route Tables
  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-public-rt"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: AttachGateway
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnetARouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnetA
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  PublicSubnetBRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnetB
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  PrivateRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-private-rt"

  PrivateRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      NatGatewayId: !Ref NATGateway

  PrivateSubnetARouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnetA
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  PrivateSubnetBRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnetB
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # SECURITY GROUPS
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ALBSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Security group for Application Load Balancer"
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: "HTTP from anywhere"
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: "HTTPS from anywhere"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-alb-sg"

  WebServerSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Security group for web servers"
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 8000
          ToPort: 8000
          SourceSecurityGroupId: !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
          Description: "Django from ALB"
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 10.0.0.0/16
          Description: "SSH from VPC"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-web-sg"

  DatabaseSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Security group for RDS database"
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 5432
          ToPort: 5432
          SourceSecurityGroupId: !Ref WebServerSecurityGroup
          Description: "PostgreSQL from web servers"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-db-sg"

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # APPLICATION LOAD BALANCER
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ApplicationLoadBalancer:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-alb"
      Type: application
      Scheme: internet-facing
      IpAddressType: ipv4
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
      Subnets:
        - !Ref PublicSubnetA
        - !Ref PublicSubnetB
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-alb"

  ALBTargetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-tg"
      Port: 8000
      Protocol: HTTP
      VpcId: !Ref AnihpjVPC
      HealthCheckEnabled: true
      HealthCheckPath: /health/
      HealthCheckProtocol: HTTP
      HealthCheckIntervalSeconds: 30
      HealthCheckTimeoutSeconds: 5
      HealthyThresholdCount: 2
      UnhealthyThresholdCount: 3
      TargetType: instance
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-tg"

  ALBListenerHTTP:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref ApplicationLoadBalancer
      Port: 80
      Protocol: HTTP
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: !If [HasSSLCertificate, redirect, forward]
          RedirectConfig: !If
            - HasSSLCertificate
            - Protocol: HTTPS
              Port: 443
              StatusCode: HTTP_301
            - !Ref AWS::NoValue
          TargetGroupArn: !If [HasSSLCertificate, !Ref AWS::NoValue, !Ref ALBTargetGroup]

  ALBListenerHTTPS:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Condition: HasSSLCertificate
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref ApplicationLoadBalancer
      Port: 443
      Protocol: HTTPS
      Certificates:
        - CertificateArn: !Ref SSLCertificateArn
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: forward
          TargetGroupArn: !Ref ALBTargetGroup

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # IAM ROLE FOR EC2 INSTANCES
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  EC2Role:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/CloudWatchAgentServerPolicy
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: AnihpjS3Access
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:DeleteObject
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt StaticFilesBucket.Arn
                  - !Sub "${StaticFilesBucket.Arn}/*"
                  - !GetAtt MediaFilesBucket.Arn
                  - !Sub "${MediaFilesBucket.Arn}/*"

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2Role

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LAUNCH TEMPLATE & AUTO SCALING
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  LaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-lt"
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !FindInMap [RegionMap, !Ref "AWS::Region", AMI]
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyPairName
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Arn: !GetAtt EC2InstanceProfile.Arn
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref WebServerSecurityGroup
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash
            set -e
            
            # Update system
            apt-get update
            apt-get upgrade -y
            
            # Install Python and dependencies
            apt-get install -y python3-pip python3-venv postgresql-client git
            
            # Install CloudWatch agent
            wget https://s3.amazonaws.com/amazoncloudwatch-agent/ubuntu/amd64/latest/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb
            dpkg -i -E ./amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb
            
            # Create application user
            useradd -m -s /bin/bash anihpj
            
            # Clone Django application
            su - anihpj -c "git clone https://github.com/yourorg/anihpj.git /home/anihpj/app"
            cd /home/anihpj/app
            
            # Setup Python virtual environment
            python3 -m venv /home/anihpj/venv
            source /home/anihpj/venv/bin/activate
            pip install --upgrade pip
            pip install -r requirements.txt
            pip install gunicorn boto3
            
            # Set environment variables
            cat > /home/anihpj/.env << EOF
            DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.production
            DATABASE_URL=postgresql://anihpj:${DBPassword}@${DatabaseInstance.Endpoint.Address}:5432/anihpj
            AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME=${StaticFilesBucket}
            AWS_MEDIA_BUCKET_NAME=${MediaFilesBucket}
            ALLOWED_HOSTS=${ApplicationLoadBalancer.DNSName}
            DEBUG=False
            EOF
            
            # Run migrations
            source /home/anihpj/.env
            python manage.py migrate --no-input
            python manage.py collectstatic --no-input
            
            # Create systemd service
            cat > /etc/systemd/system/anihpj.service << 'EOF'
            [Unit]
            Description=anihpj Django Application
            After=network.target
            
            [Service]
            User=anihpj
            WorkingDirectory=/home/anihpj/app
            EnvironmentFile=/home/anihpj/.env
            ExecStart=/home/anihpj/venv/bin/gunicorn \
                --workers 3 \
                --bind 0.0.0.0:8000 \
                --timeout 60 \
                --access-logfile /var/log/anihpj/access.log \
                --error-logfile /var/log/anihpj/error.log \
                anihpj.wsgi:application
            
            [Install]
            WantedBy=multi-user.target
            EOF
            
            # Start Django application
            mkdir -p /var/log/anihpj
            chown -R anihpj:anihpj /var/log/anihpj
            systemctl daemon-reload
            systemctl enable anihpj
            systemctl start anihpj
            
            # Send success signal to CloudFormation
            /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal -e $? --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource AutoScalingGroup --region ${AWS::Region}

  AutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      AutoScalingGroupName: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-asg"
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      MinSize: !If [IsProduction, 2, 1]
      MaxSize: !If [IsProduction, 10, 3]
      DesiredCapacity: !If [IsProduction, 3, 1]
      HealthCheckType: ELB
      HealthCheckGracePeriod: 300
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref PrivateSubnetA
        - !Ref PrivateSubnetB
      TargetGroupARNs:
        - !Ref ALBTargetGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-web"
          PropagateAtLaunch: true
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
          PropagateAtLaunch: true
    CreationPolicy:
      ResourceSignal:
        Count: !If [IsProduction, 2, 1]
        Timeout: PT15M

  # Auto Scaling Policies
  ScaleUpPolicy:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
    Properties:
      AutoScalingGroupName: !Ref AutoScalingGroup
      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
      TargetTrackingConfiguration:
        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
          PredefinedMetricType: ASGAverageCPUUtilization
        TargetValue: 70.0

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # RDS DATABASE
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  DBSubnetGroup:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBSubnetGroup
    Properties:
      DBSubnetGroupDescription: "Subnet group for anihpj RDS"
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PrivateSubnetA
        - !Ref PrivateSubnetB
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-db-subnet-group"

  DatabaseInstance:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    DeletionPolicy: Snapshot
    Properties:
      DBInstanceIdentifier: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-db"
      DBName: anihpj
      Engine: postgres
      EngineVersion: "15.4"
      DBInstanceClass: !Ref DBInstanceClass
      AllocatedStorage: 100
      StorageType: gp3
      StorageEncrypted: true
      MasterUsername: anihpj
      MasterUserPassword: !Ref DBPassword
      VPCSecurityGroups:
        - !Ref DatabaseSecurityGroup
      DBSubnetGroupName: !Ref DBSubnetGroup
      MultiAZ: !If [CreateMultiAZ, true, false]
      BackupRetentionPeriod: !If [IsProduction, 7, 1]
      PreferredBackupWindow: "03:00-04:00"
      PreferredMaintenanceWindow: "sun:04:00-sun:05:00"
      EnableCloudwatchLogsExports:
        - postgresql
      DeletionProtection: !Condition IsProduction
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-db"

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # S3 BUCKETS
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  StaticFilesBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "anihpj-static-${EnvironmentName}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: false
        BlockPublicPolicy: false
        IgnorePublicAcls: false
        RestrictPublicBuckets: false
      CorsConfiguration:
        CorsRules:
          - AllowedOrigins: ["*"]
            AllowedMethods: [GET, HEAD]
            AllowedHeaders: ["*"]
            MaxAge: 3600
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-static"

  StaticFilesBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref StaticFilesBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Statement:
          - Sid: PublicReadGetObject
            Effect: Allow
            Principal: "*"
            Action: s3:GetObject
            Resource: !Sub "${StaticFilesBucket.Arn}/*"

  MediaFilesBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "anihpj-media-${EnvironmentName}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: DeleteOldVersions
            Status: Enabled
            NoncurrentVersionExpirationInDays: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${EnvironmentName}-anihpj-media"

# ============================================================================
# OUTPUTS - Values to export
# ============================================================================
Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: "VPC ID"
    Value: !Ref AnihpjVPC
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VpcId"

  LoadBalancerURL:
    Description: "Application Load Balancer URL"
    Value: !Sub "http://${ApplicationLoadBalancer.DNSName}"
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LoadBalancerURL"

  LoadBalancerDNS:
    Description: "ALB DNS name for Route 53"
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationLoadBalancer.DNSName
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LoadBalancerDNS"

  DatabaseEndpoint:
    Description: "RDS database endpoint"
    Value: !GetAtt DatabaseInstance.Endpoint.Address
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-DatabaseEndpoint"

  StaticBucketName:
    Description: "S3 bucket for static files"
    Value: !Ref StaticFilesBucket
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-StaticBucket"

  MediaBucketName:
    Description: "S3 bucket for media files"
    Value: !Ref MediaFilesBucket
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-MediaBucket"
🎉 Complete Stack Template!

This template creates 30+ AWS resources in the correct order:

  • ✅ VPC with public/private subnets across 2 AZs
  • ✅ Internet Gateway + NAT Gateway for outbound access
  • ✅ Security Groups with least-privilege rules
  • ✅ Application Load Balancer with HTTPS redirect
  • ✅ Auto Scaling Group (2-10 instances based on CPU)
  • ✅ RDS PostgreSQL with Multi-AZ and automated backups
  • ✅ S3 buckets for static/media files with versioning
  • ✅ IAM roles with CloudWatch and S3 access
  • ✅ Automated Django deployment via UserData
  • ✅ CloudFormation wait conditions for health checks

Deploy this and your entire anihpj infrastructure is production-ready in 15 minutes!

Deploy Complete Stack
# Deploy the complete production stack
aws cloudformation create-stack \
    --stack-name anihpj-production \
    --template-body file://anihpj-production-stack.yaml \
    --parameters \
        ParameterKey=EnvironmentName,ParameterValue=production \
        ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
        ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,ParameterValue=db.t3.medium \
        ParameterKey=DBPassword,ParameterValue=YourSecurePassword123! \
        ParameterKey=KeyPairName,ParameterValue=your-key-pair \
        ParameterKey=SSLCertificateArn,ParameterValue=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123 \
    --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=CostCenter,Value=Engineering

# Monitor stack creation progress
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
    --stack-name anihpj-production \
    --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId]' \
    --output table

# Wait for completion (15-20 minutes)
aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-production

# Get stack outputs
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name anihpj-production \
    --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs' \
    --output table

# Output:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------
# |                         Outputs                             |
# +----------------------+-------------------------------------+
# | LoadBalancerURL      | http://anihpj-alb-123.us-east-1... |
# | DatabaseEndpoint     | anihpj-db.abc.us-east-1.rds...     |
# | StaticBucketName     | anihpj-static-production-1234...   |
# | MediaBucketName      | anihpj-media-production-1234...    |
# +----------------------+-------------------------------------+

# ✅ Your complete Django infrastructure is now live!
# Visit LoadBalancerURL to see your anihpj application running!

🔑 4. Key Features

Powerful features that make CloudFormation essential for infrastructure management.

🎯 Feature Overview
📋 Declarative Templates
Define desired state
CloudFormation handles how
YAML or JSON format
🔄 Automatic Rollback
Rollback on failure
Protect production
No manual cleanup needed
🔗 Dependency Management
Auto-detect dependencies
Create in correct order
Parallel creation when possible
🔍 Change Sets
Preview before apply
See what will change
Identify risky changes
📦 Stack Sets
Multi-account deployment
Multi-region deployment
Centralized management
🔐 Drift Detection
Detect manual changes
Keep templates in sync
Compliance enforcement
🔍 Drift Detection

Detect when your actual infrastructure differs from your CloudFormation template (manual changes via console). Essential for maintaining infrastructure integrity for anihpj.

🎯 Drift Detection Workflow
📄
Template State

EC2: t3.medium
RDS: db.t3.small
SG: Port 8000 from ALB

⚠️
🖥️
Actual State

EC2: t3.large ❌
RDS: db.t3.small ✅
SG: Port 8000 from 0.0.0.0/0 ❌

→
🔧
Fix Drift

Update stack
Restore compliance
Security restored ✅

🔍 Detect and Fix Drift for anihpj
# Step 1: Start drift detection
aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
    --stack-name anihpj-production

# Output:
# {
#     "StackDriftDetectionId": "abc123-drift-detection-id"
# }

# Step 2: Check drift detection status
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
    --stack-drift-detection-id abc123-drift-detection-id

# Step 3: Get detailed drift results
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
    --stack-name anihpj-production \
    --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED DELETED \
    --output table

# Example Output:
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# |              DescribeStackResourceDrifts                   |
# +-----------------+-------------------+----------------------+
# | LogicalResourceId | ResourceType    | DriftStatus         |
# +-----------------+-------------------+----------------------+
# | WebServerSG     | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | MODIFIED    |
# |   Expected: Port 8000 from ALB only                        |
# |   Actual:   Port 8000 from 0.0.0.0/0  ← SECURITY RISK!    |
# +-----------------+-------------------+----------------------+
# | WebServerEC2    | AWS::EC2::Instance      | MODIFIED    |
# |   Expected: t3.medium                                      |
# |   Actual:   t3.large  ← COST INCREASE!                    |
# +-----------------+-------------------+----------------------+
# | DatabaseRDS     | AWS::RDS::DBInstance    | IN_SYNC     |
# |   No drift detected                                        |
# +-----------------+-------------------+----------------------+

# Step 4: Fix drift - Update stack to overwrite manual changes
aws cloudformation update-stack \
    --stack-name anihpj-production \
    --use-previous-template \
    --parameters UsePreviousValue=true

# This will:
# ✅ Restore EC2 to t3.medium (save costs)
# ✅ Fix security group to ALB-only access (security compliance)
# ✅ Bring infrastructure back into compliance
🎯 Drift Detection Best Practices for anihpj:
  • 🔄 Schedule regular drift checks: Weekly CloudWatch event to detect drift
  • 🚨 Alert on drift: SNS notification when drift detected
  • 🔒 Prevent drift: Use IAM policies to restrict console modifications
  • 📊 Track drift history: Log all drift events for compliance audits
  • ✅ Auto-remediate: Lambda function to auto-fix non-critical drift
# ✅ Security group reverted to template configuration # ✅ Stack and infrastructure now in sync
🌍 Stack Sets (Multi-Account/Multi-Region)

Deploy the same stack across multiple AWS accounts and regions from a single template.

💡 Stack Sets Use Cases for anihpj
Scenario Traditional Approach Stack Sets Approach Benefit
Multi-Region Deployment Deploy to each region manually One command deploys to 5 regions 95% time savings
Dev/Staging/Prod Accounts Manually replicate in each account Auto-replicate across accounts Consistent environments
Security Baselines Apply security in each account Centralized security policies Compliance guaranteed
Global Job Portal Deploy anihpj in each region separately Deploy to US, EU, Asia simultaneously Faster global launch
📦 Nested Stacks

Break large templates into smaller, reusable components for better organization and maintenance.

🏗️ Nested Stack Architecture for anihpj
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  MASTER STACK (anihpj-master.yaml)            │
│                                                                │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  NetworkStack (Import: network-stack.yaml)               │ │
│  │  ├─ VPC                                                   │ │
│  │  ├─ Subnets (Public/Private)                             │ │
│  │  ├─ Internet Gateway                                     │ │
│  │  ├─ NAT Gateway                                          │ │
│  │  └─ Route Tables                                         │ │
│  │  Outputs: VpcId, PublicSubnets, PrivateSubnets           │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                         │                                      │
│                         ↓                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  SecurityStack (Import: security-stack.yaml)             │ │
│  │  ├─ ALB Security Group                                   │ │
│  │  ├─ Web Server Security Group                            │ │
│  │  ├─ Database Security Group                              │ │
│  │  └─ IAM Roles                                            │ │
│  │  Inputs: VpcId (from NetworkStack)                       │ │
│  │  Outputs: SecurityGroupIds                               │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                         │                                      │
│                         ↓                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  DatabaseStack (Import: database-stack.yaml)             │ │
│  │  ├─ RDS PostgreSQL Instance                              │ │
│  │  ├─ DB Subnet Group                                      │ │
│  │  ├─ Read Replica (if production)                         │ │
│  │  └─ CloudWatch Alarms                                    │ │
│  │  Inputs: VpcId, PrivateSubnets, SecurityGroupId          │ │
│  │  Outputs: DatabaseEndpoint                               │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                         │                                      │
│                         ↓                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ComputeStack (Import: compute-stack.yaml)               │ │
│  │  ├─ Application Load Balancer                            │ │
│  │  ├─ Launch Template                                      │ │
│  │  ├─ Auto Scaling Group                                   │ │
│  │  └─ Scaling Policies                                     │ │
│  │  Inputs: VpcId, Subnets, SecurityGroups, DatabaseEndpoint│ │
│  │  Outputs: LoadBalancerDNS                                │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                         │                                      │
│                         ↓                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  StorageStack (Import: storage-stack.yaml)               │ │
│  │  ├─ S3 Static Files Bucket                               │ │
│  │  ├─ S3 Media Files Bucket                                │ │
│  │  ├─ CloudFront Distribution                              │ │
│  │  └─ Bucket Policies                                      │ │
│  │  Outputs: BucketNames, CloudFrontDomain                  │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                │
│  Benefits:                                                     │
│  ✓ Reusable components across environments                    │
│  ✓ Easier to maintain and update                             │
│  ✓ Team can work on different stacks independently           │
│  ✓ Better organization for large projects                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

💰 5. Pricing & Cost Optimization

Understanding CloudFormation costs is crucial for anihpj budget planning. The service itself is FREE - you only pay for the AWS resources created.

💵 Pricing Model (FREE Service!)
🎉 CloudFormation Itself is 100% FREE!
  • ✅ Template storage: Unlimited templates in CloudFormation service
  • ✅ Stack operations: Create, update, delete operations
  • ✅ Change Sets: Preview changes before applying
  • ✅ Drift Detection: Monitor resource configuration changes
  • ✅ Stack Sets: Multi-account/multi-region deployments
  • ✅ Event monitoring: Real-time deployment tracking
Component CloudFormation Cost Resource Cost (Your Bill) anihpj Example
📄 Templates $0 FREE $0 Store unlimited infrastructure templates
🔧 Stack Operations $0 FREE $0 Create, update, delete stacks
🖥️ EC2 Instances $0 FREE ~$50/month t3.medium for Django app
🗄️ RDS Database $0 FREE ~$25/month db.t3.micro PostgreSQL
🔁 Load Balancer $0 FREE ~$20/month Application Load Balancer
📦 S3 Storage $0 FREE ~$5/month Static files, media uploads
🌍 Route53 DNS $0 FREE ~$0.50/month anihpj.com hosted zone
📊 Infrastructure Cost Examples for anihpj
💻
Development Environment

Monthly Cost: ~$35

  • EC2 t3.micro: $8
  • RDS db.t3.micro: $15
  • ALB (optional): $15
  • S3 + Route53: $2
  • CF Service: FREE
🏭
Production Environment

Monthly Cost: ~$110

  • EC2 t3.medium (2x): $70
  • RDS db.t3.small: $25
  • ALB: $15
  • S3, Route53, CloudWatch: $8
  • CF Service: FREE
🌍
Enterprise Multi-Region

Monthly Cost: ~$300

  • EC2 Auto Scaling (4x regions): $200
  • RDS Multi-AZ + Read Replica: $80
  • ALB + CloudFront: $35
  • S3, Route53, Monitoring: $15
  • CF Service: FREE
🎯 Cost Optimization Strategies for anihpj
💡 Template-Level Savings:
  • 🔄 Conditional Resources: Create expensive resources only in production
  • 📅 Scheduled Scaling: Auto-scale down during off-hours
  • 💾 Storage Classes: Use S3 Intelligent Tiering in templates
  • 🏷️ Resource Tagging: Automatic cost tracking with CF tags
🚀 Deployment Savings:
  • 🌙 Dev Environment Automation: Tear down after hours
  • 📋 Stack Sets: Bulk regional deployment reduces management overhead
  • 🔍 Drift Detection: Prevent unauthorized expensive changes
  • 📊 Cost Alarms: CloudWatch billing alerts in templates
💰 anihpj Cost Optimization Template Example
# Cost-optimized CloudFormation template
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Cost-optimized anihpj infrastructure with conditional resources'

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: development
    AllowedValues: [development, staging, production]
  
  AutoShutdown:
    Type: String
    Default: 'true'
    AllowedValues: ['true', 'false']
    Description: 'Automatically shutdown development resources after hours'

Conditions:
  IsProduction: !Equals [!Ref Environment, production]
  IsNotProduction: !Not [!Equals [!Ref Environment, production]]
  EnableAutoShutdown: !And [!Condition IsNotProduction, !Equals [!Ref AutoShutdown, 'true']]

Resources:
  # Basic EC2 for dev, larger for production
  WebServer:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !If [IsProduction, t3.medium, t3.micro]
      # Auto-shutdown Lambda for dev environments
      UserData: !If
        - EnableAutoShutdown
        - !Base64 |
            #!/bin/bash
            # Schedule shutdown at 6 PM daily
            echo "0 18 * * * /sbin/shutdown -h now" | crontab -
        - !Ref AWS::NoValue
  
  # RDS only in staging and production
  Database:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Condition: IsNotDevelopment
    Properties:
      DBInstanceClass: !If [IsProduction, db.t3.small, db.t3.micro]
      MultiAZ: !If [IsProduction, true, false]
      
  # S3 bucket with intelligent tiering
  StaticFilesBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      IntelligentTieringConfigurations:
        - Id: EntireBucket
          Status: Enabled
          Prefix: ""
      
  # Cost monitoring alarm
  CostAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-cost-alarm'
      MetricName: EstimatedCharges
      Namespace: AWS/Billing
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 86400
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: !If [IsProduction, 200, 50]  # $200 prod, $50 dev
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold

Outputs:
  MonthlyCostEstimate:
    Description: Estimated monthly cost for this stack
    Value: !If
      - IsProduction
      - "$110/month (production resources)"
      - "$35/month (development resources)"

📈 6. Scalability & Performance

CloudFormation can handle large-scale deployments efficiently. Understanding scalability patterns and performance optimization is crucial for enterprise anihpj deployments.

📄 Large Template Management

Managing complex templates with hundreds of resources requires strategic approaches:

🔨 Nested Stacks
Break large templates into smaller, reusable components
📦 Stack Sets
Deploy across multiple accounts and regions efficiently
🏗️ Modular Design
Create reusable template modules for common patterns
📋 Parameter Files
Separate configuration from infrastructure templates
🔧 anihpj Nested Stack Architecture
# Master template: anihpj-master.yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Master stack for anihpj Django application'

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: production
    AllowedValues: [development, staging, production]
  
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    
Resources:
  # Network Stack
  NetworkStack:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack
    Properties:
      TemplateURL: !Sub 'https://anihpj-cf-templates.s3.amazonaws.com/network-${Environment}.yaml'
      Parameters:
        Environment: !Ref Environment
        VpcCidr: '10.0.0.0/16'
        
  # Database Stack  
  DatabaseStack:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack
    DependsOn: NetworkStack
    Properties:
      TemplateURL: !Sub 'https://anihpj-cf-templates.s3.amazonaws.com/database-${Environment}.yaml'
      Parameters:
        Environment: !Ref Environment
        VpcId: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.VpcId
        DatabaseSubnets: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.DatabaseSubnets
        
  # Application Stack
  ApplicationStack:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack
    DependsOn: [NetworkStack, DatabaseStack]
    Properties:
      TemplateURL: !Sub 'https://anihpj-cf-templates.s3.amazonaws.com/application-${Environment}.yaml'
      Parameters:
        Environment: !Ref Environment
        VpcId: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.VpcId
        PublicSubnets: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.PublicSubnets
        PrivateSubnets: !GetAtt NetworkStack.Outputs.PrivateSubnets
        DatabaseEndpoint: !GetAtt DatabaseStack.Outputs.DatabaseEndpoint
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType

Outputs:
  WebsiteURL:
    Description: 'anihpj website URL'
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationStack.Outputs.WebsiteURL
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${Environment}-anihpj-website-url'
⚡ Parallel Deployments

Optimize deployment time through parallel resource creation:

🏗️ Strategy ⏱️ Time Impact 🔄 Use Case 💰 Cost Impact
Independent Resources ⚡ 60% faster S3, CloudWatch, IAM roles ❌ No impact
Stack Sets (Multi-Region) ⚡ 75% faster Global deployment 💰 Parallel charges
Change Sets ⚡ Preview in 2 min Production updates ❌ No cost
Nested Stacks ⚡ 40% faster Complex applications ❌ No impact
🏁 Performance Optimization

Best practices for fast, reliable CloudFormation deployments:

⚡ Performance Optimization Checklist for anihpj
# Performance-optimized CloudFormation practices

# 1. ✅ Use Intrinsic Functions Efficiently
Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: production

# ❌ DON'T: Complex nested functions
BadExample: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-${AWS::Region}-${!Join ['-', [!Ref Environment, 'ec2']]}'

# ✅ DO: Simple, readable functions  
GoodExample: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-${Environment}-ec2'

# 2. ✅ Minimize Resource Dependencies
Resources:
  # Independent resources can be created in parallel
  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    
  IAMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    # These can be created simultaneously
    
  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    DependsOn: [S3Bucket, IAMRole]  # Explicit dependencies only when needed

# 3. ✅ Use Conditions for Optional Resources
Conditions:
  IsProd: !Equals [!Ref Environment, 'production']
  CreateReplica: !And 
    - !Condition IsProd
    - !Equals [!Ref CreateMultiAZ, 'true']

Resources:
  MasterDatabase:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    # ... properties
    
  ReadReplica:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Condition: CreateReplica
    # Only created when conditions are met

# 4. ✅ Template Size Optimization
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: 'AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31'  # Use SAM for shorter syntax

# 5. ✅ Efficient Parameter Usage
Parameters:
  # Use Parameter Store for frequently changing values
  DatabasePassword:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value
    Default: '/anihpj/prod/db/password'
    NoEcho: true
💡 anihpj Performance Tips:
  • Stack Size: Keep templates under 51,200 bytes (use nested stacks for larger deployments)
  • Resource Limit: Max 500 resources per stack (use Stack Sets for larger infrastructure)
  • Deployment Time: Typical anihpj full stack: 12-18 minutes
  • Update Time: Application-only updates: 3-8 minutes
  • Rollback Time: Automatic rollback: 5-15 minutes

🎓 7. Advanced Topics

Master advanced CloudFormation features to build sophisticated, enterprise-grade infrastructure for anihpj.

⚙️ Custom Resources

Extend CloudFormation beyond native AWS resources using Lambda functions. Perfect for anihpj-specific logic like database initialization or third-party integrations.

💡 When to Use Custom Resources for anihpj:
🗄️ Database Initialization

Run Django migrations, create initial superuser, load fixtures after RDS creation

🔔 Slack Notifications

Send deployment notifications to team Slack channel when stack is created/updated

🌐 DNS Validation

Auto-create DNS validation records for ACM SSL certificates

📊 Custom Metrics

Push deployment metrics to external monitoring (Datadog, New Relic)

🔧 CloudFormation Macros

CloudFormation Macros enable you to perform custom processing on templates, from simple actions like find-and-replace to full template transformation and validation.

💡 Use Cases for anihpj Macros
  • Template Validation: Enforce naming conventions, required tags, security policies
  • Code Generation: Auto-generate repetitive resource definitions
  • Template Transformation: Convert shorthand notation to full CloudFormation syntax
  • Policy Enforcement: Ensure all resources have encryption, logging, backup policies
# Example: Macro that adds required tags to all resources
Resources:
  AddTagsMacro:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Macro
    Properties:
      Name: AddRequiredTags
      FunctionName: !GetAtt AddTagsFunction.Arn
      
  AddTagsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Runtime: python3.9
      Handler: index.handler
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def handler(event, context):
              template = event['fragment']
              required_tags = [
                  {'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                  {'Key': 'ManagedBy', 'Value': 'CloudFormation'}
              ]
              
              for resource in template['Resources'].values():
                  if 'Properties' in resource:
                      if 'Tags' not in resource['Properties']:
                          resource['Properties']['Tags'] = []
                      resource['Properties']['Tags'].extend(required_tags)
              
              return {
                  'requestId': event['requestId'],
                  'status': 'success',
                  'fragment': template
              }
🔄 Transforms & SAM

AWS CloudFormation Transforms simplify template authoring using macros hosted by AWS. The AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM) is a popular transform for serverless applications.

🚀 AWS::Serverless Transform

Simplifies serverless application definition with shorthand syntax for Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, and more.

Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Resources:
  AnihpjApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Runtime: python3.9
      Handler: app.handler
      Events:
        GetJobs:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            Path: /jobs
            Method: get
📦 AWS::Include Transform

Include template snippets stored in S3, enabling template reuse and modularization.

Transform:
  Name: AWS::Include
  Parameters:
    Location: s3://anihpj-templates/common.yaml
💡 For anihpj: Use SAM transform if building serverless APIs or microservices. It reduces template complexity by 60-70% compared to raw CloudFormation.
🌍 Cross-Account Deployments

Deploy CloudFormation stacks across multiple AWS accounts for multi-tenant SaaS applications or organizational infrastructure standards.

Method Use Case Complexity Best For
StackSets Deploy same template to multiple accounts/regions Low Standard infrastructure (VPC, IAM, monitoring)
Cross-Account Roles Assume role in target account to deploy Medium CI/CD pipelines deploying to multiple accounts
Service Catalog Self-service infrastructure provisioning Medium Give teams controlled access to deploy approved stacks
🔧 anihpj Multi-Account Strategy
# Deploy anihpj foundation to all accounts
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
    --stack-set-name anihpj-foundation \
    --template-body file://foundation.yaml \
    --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
    --accounts 111111111111 222222222222 333333333333 \
    --regions us-east-1 us-west-2

# Accounts:
# 111111111111 - anihpj-dev
# 222222222222 - anihpj-staging  
# 333333333333 - anihpj-production
🚀 cfn-init & Helper Scripts

CloudFormation helper scripts enable you to install software, start services, and configure EC2 instances during stack creation. Essential for bootstrapping anihpj Django servers.

cfn-init
Reads and executes configuration metadata from template
cfn-signal
Signals CloudFormation when instance configuration completes
cfn-get-metadata
Retrieves metadata defined in template
cfn-hup
Daemon that detects metadata changes and runs hooks
🐍 Bootstrap anihpj Django Server with cfn-init
Resources:
  AnihpjWebServer:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Metadata:
      AWS::CloudFormation::Init:
        config:
          packages:
            apt:
              python3-pip: []
              python3-venv: []
              nginx: []
              postgresql-client: []
          
          files:
            /etc/nginx/sites-available/anihpj:
              content: |
                server {
                    listen 80;
                    server_name anihpj.com;
                    location / {
                        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000;
                    }
                }
              mode: '000644'
              owner: root
              group: root
          
          commands:
            01_clone_repo:
              command: git clone https://github.com/yourname/anihpj.git /var/www/anihpj
            02_create_venv:
              command: python3 -m venv /var/www/anihpj/venv
              cwd: /var/www/anihpj
            03_install_deps:
              command: /var/www/anihpj/venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt
              cwd: /var/www/anihpj
            04_collect_static:
              command: /var/www/anihpj/venv/bin/python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
              cwd: /var/www/anihpj
            05_migrate:
              command: /var/www/anihpj/venv/bin/python manage.py migrate
              cwd: /var/www/anihpj
          
          services:
            sysvinit:
              nginx:
                enabled: true
                ensureRunning: true
    
    Properties:
      ImageId: ami-0abcdef1234567890
      InstanceType: t3.medium
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash -xe
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y python3-pip
          pip3 install https://s3.amazonaws.com/cloudformation-examples/aws-cfn-bootstrap-py3-latest.tar.gz
          
          # Run cfn-init
          /usr/local/bin/cfn-init -v --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource AnihpjWebServer --region ${AWS::Region}
          
          # Signal success or failure
          /usr/local/bin/cfn-signal -e $? --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource AnihpjWebServer --region ${AWS::Region}
    
    CreationPolicy:
      ResourceSignals:
        Timeout: PT15M  # Wait up to 15 minutes for signal
✅ Pro Tip: Use cfn-init instead of complex UserData bash scripts. It's more maintainable, supports Windows, and provides better error handling with cfn-signal.
🎨 CloudFormation Designer

CloudFormation Designer is a visual tool for creating, viewing, and modifying CloudFormation templates through a drag-and-drop interface.

🎨 Visual Template Builder

Drag resources onto canvas, connect them with relationships, auto-generates CloudFormation JSON/YAML

📊 Template Visualization

Import existing templates to see resource relationships and dependencies in visual diagram

🔍 Template Validation

Integrated validation checks for syntax errors, missing parameters, circular dependencies

📝 Code Editor

Split view with visual designer and code editor, changes sync in real-time

🎯 When to Use Designer for anihpj:
  • Learning: Great for beginners to understand CloudFormation resource relationships
  • Documentation: Generate visual architecture diagrams from existing templates
  • Quick Prototyping: Rapidly sketch out infrastructure before coding
  • Template Debugging: Visualize complex templates to identify dependency issues
💡 Best Practice: Use Designer for initial architecture design and learning, but write templates in code editor (VS Code) for production. Version control works better with text files than Designer's visual format.
🚀 Access CloudFormation Designer
1. Open AWS CloudFormation Console
2. Click "Designer" in left navigation
3. Options:
   • Create template - Start with blank canvas
   • Open - Import existing template from file/S3
   • Validate template - Check for errors

4. Drag resources from Resource Types panel:
   • EC2 → Instance
   • RDS → DBInstance
   • ELB → LoadBalancer
   • VPC → VPC, Subnet, SecurityGroup

5. Connect resources by dragging arrows between them

6. View generated code in bottom panel (JSON/YAML)

7. Download template or save to S3
💵 Pricing Model
🎉 CloudFormation is FREE!

There is no additional charge for using AWS CloudFormation. You only pay for the AWS resources (EC2, RDS, S3, etc.) that CloudFormation creates.

✅ FREE Features:
• Template creation
• Stack creation/updates
• Change sets
• Drift detection
• Stack sets
• Nested stacks
💰 You Pay For:
• EC2 instances ($70/mo)
• RDS databases ($35/mo)
• S3 storage ($5/mo)
• Data transfer ($10/mo)
• Load balancers ($18/mo)
• NAT Gateway ($32/mo)
📊 Cost Examples for anihpj
💵 Infrastructure Costs (CloudFormation-Managed)

│         MONTHLY COST BREAKDOWN (anihpj Production)            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                               │
│  COMPUTE (from CloudFormation template)                      │
│  ├─ EC2 Auto Scaling Group                                   │
│  │  ├─ 3× t3.medium instances (24/7)       $90.00           │
│  │  ├─ EBS volumes (3× 30GB gp3)           $7.20            │
│  │  └─ Data transfer (100GB out)           $9.00            │

│  │                                                            │

│  ├─ Storage (100GB gp3)                     $23.00           │
│  └─ Backup storage (100GB snapshots)        $9.50            │
│                                    Subtotal: $100.50          │
│                                                               │

│  ├─ S3 media files (50GB)                   $1.15            │
│  └─ S3 requests (1M GET)                    $0.40            │
│                                    Subtotal: $1.78            │
│                                                               │

│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ │

│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ │
│                                                               │
│  💡 Cost Optimization with CloudFormation:                   │
│  • Delete dev/staging stacks when not in use: -$180/month    │
│  • Schedule instances with Lambda: -$80/month                │
│  • Use Reserved Instances via template: -$40/month           │

💡 Cost Optimization Strategies
🎯 Save Money with CloudFormation
🗑️ Delete Unused Stacks
Delete entire dev/staging environments
One command removes all resources
Savings: $180+/month
⏰ Schedule Stack Creation
Create stacks at 9 AM weekdays
Delete at 6 PM automatically
Savings: 65% on dev costs
📊 Use Parameters
t3.micro for dev ($7/mo)
t3.medium for prod ($30/mo)
Savings: Rightsized resources
🔄 Automate Cleanup
DeletionPolicy: Delete for dev
DeletionPolicy: Retain for prod
Savings: No orphaned resources

✨ 8. Best Practices

Following these best practices ensures your anihpj infrastructure is secure, maintainable, and cost-effective.

📝 Template Best Practices
🎯 Parameterize Everything

Make templates reusable across dev/staging/prod environments. Use Parameters for instance types, database sizes, environment names.

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    AllowedValues: [dev, staging, prod]
📋 Use Nested Stacks

Break large templates into logical components (VPC, Database, Application). Each nested stack is independently testable and reusable.

NetworkStack:
  Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack
  Properties:
    TemplateURL: s3://bucket/vpc.yaml
🔍 Always Use Change Sets

Preview production changes before applying. See exactly what resources will be modified, added, or deleted.

aws cloudformation create-change-set \
  --stack-name anihpj-prod \
  --change-set-name update-v2
🛡️ Set DeletionPolicy

Protect critical resources from accidental deletion. Retain databases, S3 buckets with user data even if stack is deleted.

Database:
  Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
  DeletionPolicy: Retain
🏷️ Tag Everything

Add consistent tags for cost tracking, environment identification, and resource management.

Tags:
  - Key: Environment
    Value: !Ref Environment
  - Key: Application
    Value: anihpj
📦 Version Control Templates

Store templates in Git alongside Django code. Track infrastructure changes just like application code.

anihpj/
├── infrastructure/
│   ├── vpc.yaml
│   ├── database.yaml
│   └── app.yaml
🔒 Security Best Practices

Security should be built into your templates from the start. Here's how to secure anihpj infrastructure:

🔐 Never Hardcode Secrets

Problem: Database passwords in templates

Solution: Use Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store

DBPassword:
  Type: AWS::SecretsManager::Secret
  Properties:
    GenerateSecretString:
      PasswordLength: 32
🛡️ Enable Encryption

Required: Encrypt all data at rest

Resources: RDS, S3, EBS volumes, EFS

StorageEncrypted: true
KmsKeyId: !Ref MyKMSKey
🚫 Block Public Access

Default: All resources private by default

Exception: Only ALB should be public

PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
  BlockPublicAcls: true
  BlockPublicPolicy: true
🔍 Least Privilege IAM

Principle: Grant minimum required permissions

Implementation: Specific resource ARNs, no wildcards

Effect: Allow
Action: s3:GetObject
Resource: !Sub arn:aws:s3:::${BucketName}/*
🚀 Deployment Best Practices
Recommended Deployment Workflow for anihpj
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              CLOUDFORMATION DEPLOYMENT WORKFLOW             │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  Step 1: Development                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ 1. Write template in VS Code                         │  │
│  │ 2. Validate syntax: aws cloudformation validate...   │  │
│  │ 3. Lint with cfn-lint                                │  │
│  │ 4. Commit to Git feature branch                      │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                         ↓                                   │
│  Step 2: Testing in Dev Environment                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ 5. Deploy to dev account/region                      │  │
│  │ 6. Test stack creation                               │  │
│  │ 7. Test application functionality                    │  │
│  │ 8. Delete stack (verify cleanup works)               │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
