│      │     amazonaws.com/anihpj-web:v1.0.42                     │                      │
│      │ $ docker push ...anihpj-web:latest                       │                      │
│      │                                                           │                      │
│      │ Pushing: ████████████████████████████████ 100%           │                      │
│      │ v1.0.42: digest: sha256:a1b2c3d4...                      │                      │
│      │ ✅ Push complete: 518 MB uploaded                        │                      │
│      └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘                      │
│                                                                                          │
│  ⏱️  Total Build Time: 4m 32s                                                           │
│                                                                                          │
└──────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                   │
                                   │  Image Pushed
                                   ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 5: AMAZON ECR                                                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                          │
│  📦 Repository: anihpj-web                                                               │
│  🔗 URI: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-web                       │
│                                                                                          │

│  └─ Pushed: 2026-01-14 15:30:22 UTC                                                     │
│                                                                                          │
│  🔄 Automated Actions:                                                                   │

│                                                                                          │

│                                                                                          │
└──────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                   │
                                   │  Deployment Triggered
                                   ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

│                                                                                          │
│  📋 Task Definition Update:                                                              │

│  └─ New Image: anihpj-web:v1.0.42 ← FROM ECR                                            │
│                                                                                          │

│                                                                                          │
│    OLD TASKS (v1.0.41)         NEW TASKS (v1.0.42)                                      │

│    │ 🟦 │ │ 🟦 │ │ 🟦 │   →   │ 🟩 │ │ 🟩 │ │ 🟩 │                                    │
│    └────┘ └────┘ └────┘       └────┘ └────┘ └────┘                                    │
│     RUNNING   RUNNING   RUNNING   │                                                      │
│                                   ▼                                                      │
│                            Health checks pass                                            │
│                                   │                                                      │
│                                   ▼                                                      │

│    │ 🟦 │ │ 🟩 │ │ 🟩 │   →   │ 🟩 │ │ 🟩 │ │ 🟩 │                                    │
│    └────┘ └────┘ └────┘       └────┘ └────┘ └────┘                                    │
│    DRAINING                     RUNNING   RUNNING   RUNNING                              │
│                                                                                          │
│  ✅ Deployment Steps:                                                                    │
│  1️⃣  Pull image from ECR                        ✅ Complete (2s)                        │
│  2️⃣  Start new tasks with v1.0.42               ✅ Complete (15s)                       │
│  3️⃣  Health checks passing                      ✅ Complete (30s)                       │
│  4️⃣  Drain old tasks (v1.0.41)                  ✅ Complete (60s)                       │

💡 KEY BENEFITS OF THIS PIPELINE:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✅ Fully Automated:       No manual steps from git push to production
✅ Fast Deployments:      Average 6-8 minutes from code commit to live
✅ Zero Downtime:         Rolling updates with health checks
✅ Secure:                Image scanning, IAM roles, encrypted storage
✅ Traceable:             Full audit trail via CloudTrail
✅ Cost-Effective:        Pay only for build minutes and image storage
✅ Scalable:              Handles 100+ deploys per day with ease
🏗️ CodeBuild buildspec.yml for anihpj
version: 0.2

phases:
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo Logging in to Amazon ECR...
      - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com
      - REPOSITORY_URI=$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com/anihpj-web
      - COMMIT_HASH=$(echo $CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION | cut -c 1-7)
      - IMAGE_TAG=${COMMIT_HASH:=latest}
  build:
    commands:
      - echo Build started on `date`
      - echo Building the Docker image...
      - docker build -t $REPOSITORY_URI:latest .
      - docker tag $REPOSITORY_URI:latest $REPOSITORY_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker tag $REPOSITORY_URI:latest $REPOSITORY_URI:v1.0.$CODEBUILD_BUILD_NUMBER
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo Build completed on `date`
      - echo Pushing the Docker images...
      - docker push $REPOSITORY_URI:latest
      - docker push $REPOSITORY_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker push $REPOSITORY_URI:v1.0.$CODEBUILD_BUILD_NUMBER
      - echo Writing image definitions file...
      - printf '[{"name":"anihpj-web","imageUri":"%s"}]' $REPOSITORY_URI:v1.0.$CODEBUILD_BUILD_NUMBER > imagedefinitions.json
artifacts:
  files:
    - imagedefinitions.json
🐙 GitHub Actions Workflow
name: Deploy anihpj to ECR

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, production]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  ECR_REPOSITORY: anihpj-web

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v3

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v2
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to Amazon ECR
        id: login-ecr
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v1

      - name: Build, tag, and push image to Amazon ECR
        env:
          ECR_REGISTRY: ${{ steps.login-ecr.outputs.registry }}
          IMAGE_TAG: ${{ github.sha }}
        run: |
          docker build -t $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:$IMAGE_TAG .
          docker tag $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:$IMAGE_TAG $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:latest
          docker push $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:$IMAGE_TAG
          docker push $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:latest

      - name: Scan image with Trivy
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ steps.login-ecr.outputs.registry }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ github.sha }}
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'

      - name: Deploy to ECS
        run: |
          aws ecs update-service --cluster anihpj-production \
            --service anihpj-web-service --force-new-deployment
💡 Best Practice: Tag images with Git commit SHA for traceability, build numbers for versioning, and 'latest' for convenience. This gives you flexibility in rollbacks and debugging.

🎯 Common Use Cases

🔄 CI/CD Pipelines for anihpj

Build automated pipelines that build, test, scan, and deploy container images.

🏗️ Complete CI/CD Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         anihpj CI/CD Pipeline with ECR                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────┐         ┌───────────────┐
  │  Developer  │─push───▶│    GitHub    │─webhook─▶│ CodePipeline  │
  │  (git push) │         │  Repository  │         │   (Trigger)   │
  └─────────────┘         └──────────────┘         └───────┬───────┘
                                                            │
                                                            ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                          STAGE 1: Source                                │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ Pull source code from GitHub                                   │    │
  │  │ • Dockerfile                                                   │    │
  │  │ • requirements.txt                                             │    │
  │  │ • anihpj application code                                      │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                                         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                          STAGE 2: Build                                 │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ AWS CodeBuild                                                  │    │
  │  │ ─────────────                                                  │    │
  │  │ 1. Install dependencies (pip install -r requirements.txt)      │    │
  │  │ 2. Run unit tests (pytest)                                     │    │
  │  │ 3. Build Docker image                                          │    │
  │  │ 4. Login to ECR                                                │    │
  │  │ 5. Tag image (commit SHA, version, latest)                     │    │
  │  │ 6. Push to ECR                                                 │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                                         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                          STAGE 3: Security Scan                         │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ ECR Image Scanning (Automatic)                                 │    │
  │  │ ─────────────────────────────                                  │    │
  │  │ • Scan for vulnerabilities (CVEs)                              │    │
  │  │ • Check dependencies for known issues                          │    │
  │  │ • Generate security report                                     │    │
  │  │ • Fail build if CRITICAL vulnerabilities found                 │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                                         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                          STAGE 4: Deploy to Staging                     │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ ECS Fargate (Staging Cluster)                                  │    │
  │  │ ────────────────────────────                                   │    │
  │  │ • Update task definition with new image                        │    │
  │  │ • Deploy to staging environment                                │    │
  │  │ • Run integration tests                                        │    │
  │  │ • Run smoke tests                                              │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                                         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                          STAGE 5: Manual Approval                       │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ 👤 Human Review Required                                       │    │
  │  │ ────────────────────────                                       │    │
  │  │ • Review staging deployment                                    │    │
  │  │ • Check CloudWatch metrics                                     │    │
  │  │ • Approve/Reject production deployment                         │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                                         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                          STAGE 6: Deploy to Production                  │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ ECS Fargate (Production Cluster)                               │    │
  │  │ ───────────────────────────────                                │    │
  │  │ • Blue/Green deployment                                        │    │
  │  │ • Update task definition                                       │    │
  │  │ • Gradual traffic shift (10% → 50% → 100%)                     │    │
  │  │ • Monitor CloudWatch alarms                                    │    │
  │  │ • Automatic rollback if failures detected                      │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                        ECR Repositories for anihpj                      │
  │  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐    │
  │  │  anihpj-web      │  │  anihpj-worker   │  │  anihpj-nginx    │    │
  │  │  (Django app)    │  │  (Celery tasks)  │  │  (Web server)    │    │
  │  │  • v1.0.0        │  │  • v1.0.0        │  │  • v1.0.0        │    │
  │  │  • v1.0.1        │  │  • v1.0.1        │  │  • v1.0.1        │    │
  │  │  • latest        │  │  • latest        │  │  • latest        │    │
  │  │  • a1b2c3d       │  │  • a1b2c3d       │  │  • a1b2c3d       │    │
  │  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘    │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Key Benefits:
  ✅ Automated builds on every commit
  ✅ Automatic vulnerability scanning
  ✅ Staging environment for testing
  ✅ Manual approval gate for production
  ✅ Blue/Green deployment for zero downtime
  ✅ Automatic rollback on failures
💡 Time Savings: This automated pipeline reduces deployment time from 2 hours (manual) to 15 minutes (automated), and eliminates human errors in the build/deploy process.
🌐 Multi-Region Deployment

Deploy anihpj globally with ECR cross-region replication for low-latency image pulls.

🗺️ Global Deployment Architecture
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      anihpj Multi-Region Deployment                           │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

                               ┌─────────────────┐
                               │   US-EAST-1     │
                               │  (Primary ECR)  │
                               │  ┌───────────┐  │
                               │  │ anihpj-*  │  │
                               │  │ (Source)  │  │
                               │  └─────┬─────┘  │
                               └────────┼─────────┘
                                        │
         ┌──────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┐
         │                              │                              │
         ▼                              ▼                              ▼
┌─────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────┐          ┌─────────────────┐
│   EU-WEST-1     │           │   AP-SOUTH-1    │          │   SA-EAST-1     │
│  (Replica ECR)  │           │  (Replica ECR)  │          │  (Replica ECR)  │
│  ┌───────────┐  │           │  ┌───────────┐  │          │  ┌───────────┐  │
│  │ anihpj-*  │  │           │  │ anihpj-*  │  │          │  │ anihpj-*  │  │
│  │ (Replica) │  │           │  │ (Replica) │  │          │  │ (Replica) │  │
│  └─────┬─────┘  │           │  └─────┬─────┘  │          │  └─────┬─────┘  │
└────────┼─────────┘           └────────┼─────────┘          └────────┼─────────┘
         │                              │                              │
         ▼                              ▼                              ▼
┌─────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────┐          ┌─────────────────┐
│  ECS Cluster    │           │  ECS Cluster    │          │  ECS Cluster    │
│  (Europe)       │           │  (Asia)         │          │  (S. America)   │
│  • 3 tasks      │           │  • 3 tasks      │          │  • 3 tasks      │
│  • ALB          │           │  • ALB          │          │  • ALB          │
└─────────────────┘           └─────────────────┘          └─────────────────┘
         │                              │                              │
         │                              │                              │
         └──────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┘
                                        ▼
                              ┌─────────────────┐
                              │  Route 53       │
                              │  (Global DNS)   │
                              │  anihpj.com     │
                              └─────────────────┘
                                        │
                                        ▼
                              ┌─────────────────┐
                              │  End Users      │
                              │  Worldwide      │
                              └─────────────────┘

Auto-Replication Flow:
  1. Developer pushes image to us-east-1
  2. ECR automatically replicates to all regions (5-10 minutes)
  3. Regional ECS clusters pull from local ECR (low latency)
  4. Route 53 routes users to nearest region
🔧 Setup Cross-Region Replication
# Create replication configuration
aws ecr put-replication-configuration --replication-configuration '{
  "rules": [
    {
      "destinations": [
        {"region": "eu-west-1", "registryId": "123456789012"},
        {"region": "ap-south-1", "registryId": "123456789012"},
        {"region": "sa-east-1", "registryId": "123456789012"}
      ],
      "repositoryFilters": [
        {"filter": "anihpj-*", "filterType": "PREFIX_MATCH"}
      ]
    }
  ]
}'

# Verify replication status
aws ecr describe-registry --region us-east-1

# List images in replica region
aws ecr list-images --repository-name anihpj-web --region eu-west-1
📊 Multi-Region Benefits for anihpj
Metric Single Region Multi-Region
Image Pull Time 30-60 seconds (cross-region) 5-10 seconds (same region)
Deployment Time 5-10 minutes 2-3 minutes
Disaster Recovery Single point of failure Automatic failover
Data Transfer Cost $0.09/GB (cross-region) Free (same region)
Global Latency 200-500ms 50-100ms

🔧 Troubleshooting & FAQs

⚠️ Common Issues & Solutions
❌ Issue 1: "denied: Your authorization token has expired"

Cause: ECR authorization tokens expire after 12 hours.

Solution:

# Re-authenticate to ECR
aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | \
  docker login --username AWS \
  --password-stdin 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

# For automated systems, add this to your CI/CD pipeline
# before every docker push/pull operation
❌ Issue 2: "no basic auth credentials" or "denied: User is not authorized"

Cause: IAM permissions are insufficient or missing.

Solution:

# Check current IAM user/role
aws sts get-caller-identity

# Verify IAM permissions
aws iam get-user-policy --user-name your-username --policy-name ECRAccess

# Required IAM permissions for push:
{
  "Effect": "Allow",
  "Action": [
    "ecr:GetAuthorizationToken",
    "ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability",
    "ecr:InitiateLayerUpload",
    "ecr:UploadLayerPart",
    "ecr:CompleteLayerUpload",
    "ecr:PutImage"
  ],
  "Resource": "*"
}
❌ Issue 3: "Layer already exists" but push fails

Cause: Image tag immutability is enabled, preventing overwriting existing tags.

Solution:

# Option 1: Use a new tag (recommended)
docker tag anihpj-web:latest 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-web:v1.0.1
docker push 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-web:v1.0.1

# Option 2: Disable immutability (not recommended for production)
aws ecr put-image-tag-mutability \
  --repository-name anihpj-web \
  --image-tag-mutability MUTABLE

# Check immutability status
aws ecr describe-repositories --repository-names anihpj-web | \
  grep imageTagMutability
❌ Issue 4: "CannotPullContainerError" in ECS

Cause: ECS task execution role lacks ECR permissions.

Solution:

# Attach required policy to ECS task execution role
aws iam attach-role-policy \
  --role-name ecsTaskExecutionRole \
  --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly

# Verify role has correct permissions
aws iam list-attached-role-policies --role-name ecsTaskExecutionRole

# Check CloudWatch Logs for detailed error messages
aws logs tail /ecs/anihpj-web --follow
❌ Issue 5: "repository does not exist" error

Cause: Repository name is misspelled or doesn't exist in the specified region.

Solution:

# List all ECR repositories in current region
aws ecr describe-repositories --region us-east-1 | grep repositoryName

# Create repository if it doesn't exist
aws ecr create-repository \
  --repository-name anihpj-web \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --image-scanning-configuration scanOnPush=true \
  --encryption-configuration encryptionType=AES256

# Verify repository was created
aws ecr describe-repositories --repository-names anihpj-web
❌ Issue 6: Slow image pulls from ECR

Cause: Cross-region data transfer or large image sizes.

Solution:

# 1. Enable cross-region replication for faster pulls
aws ecr put-replication-configuration --replication-configuration '{
  "rules": [{
    "destinations": [{"region": "eu-west-1", "registryId": "123456789012"}],
    "repositoryFilters": [{"filter": "anihpj-*", "filterType": "PREFIX_MATCH"}]
  }]
}'

# 2. Optimize Docker image size
# Use multi-stage builds, smaller base images (alpine/slim)
# Add .dockerignore to exclude unnecessary files

# 3. Use VPC endpoints for private traffic
aws ec2 create-vpc-endpoint \
  --vpc-id vpc-123456 \
  --service-name com.amazonaws.us-east-1.ecr.dkr \
  --route-table-ids rtb-123456
❌ Issue 7: Image scan findings show vulnerabilities

Cause: Outdated packages or known CVEs in base image/dependencies.

Solution:

# 1. View detailed scan findings
aws ecr describe-image-scan-findings \
  --repository-name anihpj-web \
  --image-id imageTag=v1.0.0

# 2. Update base image to latest version
# In Dockerfile:
FROM python:3.12-slim  # Instead of python:3.9

# 3. Update dependencies
pip install --upgrade django  # Update to latest secure version

# 4. Rebuild and rescan
docker build -t anihpj-web:v1.0.1 .
docker push 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-web:v1.0.1

# 5. Set up automatic scanning on push
aws ecr put-image-scanning-configuration \
  --repository-name anihpj-web \
  --image-scanning-configuration scanOnPush=true
❓ Frequently Asked Questions
Q1: How many images can I store in ECR?

A: There are no limits on the number of repositories or images you can store. You pay only for storage used ($0.10 per GB/month). For anihpj with 5 repositories and 10 images each at 500 MB, you'd pay ~$2.50/month.

Q2: What's the difference between ECR Public and ECR Private?

A:

  • ECR Private: Images are private by default. Used for internal applications like anihpj.
  • ECR Public: Images are publicly accessible (like Docker Hub). First 50 GB storage free. Perfect for open-source projects.
  • Recommendation: Use Private for anihpj production, Public for anihpj community edition.
Q3: Can I use ECR with non-AWS services (on-premises, other clouds)?

A: Yes! ECR is accessible from anywhere with internet access. Just authenticate with AWS credentials and use standard Docker commands. You can pull ECR images from Google Cloud, Azure, on-premises Kubernetes, or local development environments.

Q4: How do I automate image cleanup to reduce costs?

A: Use ECR Lifecycle Policies to automatically delete old or untagged images:

# Keep only last 15 images, delete rest
aws ecr put-lifecycle-policy \
  --repository-name anihpj-web \
  --lifecycle-policy-text '{
    "rules": [{
      "rulePriority": 1,
      "selection": {
        "tagStatus": "any",
        "countType": "imageCountMoreThan",
        "countNumber": 15
      },
      "action": {"type": "expire"}
    }]
  }'
Q5: What happens if I delete an image that's currently running in ECS?

A: Running containers are NOT affected. ECS/EKS/Lambda pull images once during task/pod creation. Once running, they don't re-pull unless the container is restarted. However, new deployments will fail if the image is deleted. Always use lifecycle policies carefully and keep production images!

Q6: How do I troubleshoot "manifest unknown" errors?

A: This error means the image tag doesn't exist in ECR. Common causes:

  • Typo in image tag (v1.0.0 vs v1.0.1)
  • Image was deleted by lifecycle policy
  • Wrong region (image in us-east-1 but pulling from eu-west-1)
  • Wrong repository name (anihpj-web vs anihpj-web-prod)
# List all available tags
aws ecr list-images --repository-name anihpj-web --region us-east-1
Q7: Can I scan existing images for vulnerabilities?

A: Yes! You can manually trigger scans or enable continuous scanning:

# Manual scan of specific image
aws ecr start-image-scan \
  --repository-name anihpj-web \
  --image-id imageTag=v1.0.0

# Enable continuous scanning (Enhanced Scanning)
aws ecr put-registry-scanning-configuration \
  --scan-type ENHANCED \
  --rules '[{"repositoryFilters": [{"filter": "anihpj-*", "filterType": "WILDCARD"}], "scanFrequency": "CONTINUOUS_SCAN"}]'
Q8: What's the maximum image size supported by ECR?

A: ECR supports images up to 10 GB per layer and 100 layers per image (1 TB theoretical maximum). However, keep images small (<1 GB) for faster deployments. For anihpj, aim for 300-500 MB per image using multi-stage builds and alpine/slim base images.

✅ Compliance & Governance

🏆 Security Certifications & Compliance Programs

Amazon ECR meets rigorous security standards and compliance requirements for regulated industries.

🏥

HIPAA Eligible

Compliant with Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act for healthcare applications.

  • BAA available for healthcare workloads
  • Encryption at rest and in transit
  • Audit logging with CloudTrail
💳

PCI DSS Level 1

Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard certified for financial applications.

  • Secure storage of payment app containers
  • Network segmentation with VPC endpoints
  • Access control with IAM policies
🌍

ISO 27001/27017/27018

International standards for information security management and cloud privacy.

  • ISO 27001: Information security
  • ISO 27017: Cloud security
  • ISO 27018: Cloud privacy
📊

SOC 1, 2, 3

Service Organization Controls reports for security, availability, and confidentiality.

  • SOC 1: Financial reporting controls
  • SOC 2 Type II: Security & availability
  • SOC 3: Public summary report
🇪🇺

GDPR Ready

General Data Protection Regulation compliant for EU customer data.

  • Data residency in EU regions
  • Right to erasure (delete images)
  • Encryption and access controls
🛡️

FedRAMP Authorized

Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program for US government workloads.

  • FedRAMP Moderate and High
  • Available in GovCloud regions
  • Continuous monitoring & auditing
💡 For anihpj: If you're building healthcare (HIPAA), financial (PCI DSS), or government (FedRAMP) applications, ECR provides the compliance certifications you need out-of-the-box. No additional configuration required!
📋 Production Readiness Checklist

Ensure your anihpj ECR setup is production-ready with this comprehensive checklist.

🔒 Security
  • ✅ Enable image scanning (Basic or Enhanced)
  • ✅ Enable encryption at rest with KMS
  • ✅ Enable image tag immutability for production repos
  • ✅ Use IAM roles instead of access keys
  • ✅ Implement least-privilege IAM policies
  • ✅ Enable MFA for AWS root and admin accounts
  • ✅ Use VPC endpoints for private ECR access
💰 Cost Optimization
  • ✅ Implement lifecycle policies to delete old images
  • ✅ Use multi-stage Docker builds to reduce image size
  • ✅ Choose alpine/slim base images
  • ✅ Deploy in same region as compute resources
  • ✅ Clean up untagged images regularly
  • ✅ Monitor storage usage with CloudWatch
⚡ Reliability & Availability
  • ✅ Enable cross-region replication for DR
  • ✅ Use semantic versioning for image tags
  • ✅ Test image pulls in all deployment regions
  • ✅ Implement health checks in containers
  • ✅ Document rollback procedures
  • ✅ Set up CloudWatch alarms for failures
🔧 Operations
  • ✅ Automate image builds with CI/CD pipelines
  • ✅ Enable CloudTrail logging for audit trails
  • ✅ Tag repositories with project/environment/owner
  • ✅ Document tagging strategy for team
  • ✅ Set up automated vulnerability alerts
  • ✅ Create runbooks for common issues
📜 Compliance
  • ✅ Review compliance requirements (HIPAA, PCI, etc.)
  • ✅ Enable encryption for regulated workloads
  • ✅ Implement access logging and monitoring
  • ✅ Regular security audits and reviews
  • ✅ Document data residency requirements
🔄 CI/CD Integration
  • ✅ Integrate image scanning in build pipeline
  • ✅ Fail builds on critical vulnerabilities
  • ✅ Automate tagging with commit SHA
  • ✅ Implement automated testing before push
  • ✅ Set up staging environment validation
  • ✅ Use blue/green or canary deployments
📊 Monitoring & Alerting
  • ✅ Monitor ECR storage usage with CloudWatch
  • ✅ Set up alerts for high storage costs
  • ✅ Track image pull metrics
  • ✅ Monitor scan findings and trends
  • ✅ Alert on authentication failures
  • ✅ Dashboard for ECR health metrics
✅ anihpj Production Deployment Score

Complete all 45 checklist items above to ensure a production-ready ECR setup. Track your progress:

  • Security (7 items): Critical for protecting your container images
  • Cost Optimization (6 items): Reduce ECR costs by 60-80%
  • Reliability (6 items): Ensure high availability and quick recovery
  • Operations (6 items): Streamline daily management tasks
  • Compliance (5 items): Meet regulatory requirements
  • CI/CD (6 items): Automate build and deployment workflows
  • Monitoring (6 items): Gain visibility into ECR operations

📖 ECR & Container Registry Glossary

Essential terms for understanding Amazon ECR and container registries.

📦 Container Image
A lightweight, standalone executable package that includes everything needed to run a piece of software: code, runtime, libraries, and dependencies. For anihpj, this includes Django, Python, Gunicorn, and all pip packages.
🏷️ Image Tag
A label assigned to a container image for versioning and identification (e.g., v1.0.0, latest, production). Best practice: use semantic versioning for production images.
📚 Repository
A collection of related container images with different tags (versions). Example: anihpj-web repository contains all versions of the web application image.
🏛️ Registry
A service that stores and distributes container images. Amazon ECR is a fully managed Docker container registry. Other examples: Docker Hub, GitHub Container Registry, Google Container Registry.
📊 Image Manifest
A JSON file that describes the image layers, configuration, and metadata. Used by Docker to pull and reconstruct the image.
🧱 Image Layer
A read-only filesystem representing a single instruction in a Dockerfile (e.g., RUN pip install django). Layers are cached and reused across builds for efficiency.
🔐 Image Digest
A SHA256 hash uniquely identifying an image version. Unlike tags (which can be overwritten), digests are immutable. Example: sha256:a1b2c3d4...
♻️ Lifecycle Policy
Rules that automatically delete old or unused images to reduce storage costs. Example: "Keep only last 15 images" or "Delete untagged images after 7 days".
🔍 Image Scanning
Automated vulnerability assessment that checks container images for known security issues (CVEs) in OS packages and dependencies.
🛡️ CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures)
A standardized identifier for known security vulnerabilities. Example: CVE-2024-1234 might be a SQL injection vulnerability in Django 3.2.10.
🔒 Image Tag Immutability
A setting that prevents overwriting existing image tags. When enabled, pushing anihpj-web:v1.0.0 twice will fail the second time. Recommended for production repositories.
🔑 IAM Policy
AWS Identity and Access Management rules controlling who can push/pull images, create repositories, or scan images in ECR.
📜 Repository Policy
Resource-based permissions attached to a specific ECR repository. Used for cross-account access (e.g., allowing production account to pull from dev account).
🔐 KMS Encryption
AWS Key Management Service encryption for ECR images at rest. Provides additional security over default AES-256 encryption by using customer-managed keys.
🌐 Cross-Region Replication
Automatic copying of images from a primary region to one or more destination regions for disaster recovery and low-latency global deployments.
🔌 VPC Endpoint
A private connection between your VPC and ECR that doesn't require internet access. Improves security and reduces data transfer costs.
🎫 Authorization Token
A temporary password (valid for 12 hours) used to authenticate Docker client with ECR. Obtained with aws ecr get-login-password.
🐳 Docker Push
The process of uploading a container image from your local machine to a registry like ECR. Example: docker push 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-web:v1.0.0
📥 Docker Pull
The process of downloading a container image from a registry to your local machine or compute service (ECS, EKS, Lambda).
🏗️ Multi-Stage Build
A Dockerfile technique using multiple FROM statements to create smaller final images by excluding build-time dependencies. Can reduce image size by 50-70%.
📦 Base Image
The foundation image specified in FROM statement of Dockerfile. Examples: python:3.12, python:3.12-slim, python:3.12-alpine.
🔄 Image Retagging
Assigning additional tags to an existing image without rebuilding. Example: docker tag anihpj-web:v1.0.0 anihpj-web:latest
🌍 Public Registry (ECR Public)
An ECR variant where images are publicly accessible (like Docker Hub). First 50 GB storage free. Ideal for open-source projects.
🔒 Private Registry (ECR Private)
The default ECR mode where images require authentication and IAM permissions. Used for internal applications like anihpj production.
🚀 Task Execution Role
An IAM role used by ECS/Fargate to pull images from ECR and write logs to CloudWatch. Must have AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly policy attached.
💡 Quick Reference: Bookmark this glossary for easy reference when working with ECR. These 25 terms cover 90% of ECR operations you'll encounter with anihpj deployments.

🚀 Get Started with anihpj on ECR
  1. Create ECR repositories for anihpj services (web, worker, nginx)
  2. Enable image scanning and encryption for security
  3. Set up lifecycle policies to optimize costs
  4. Configure CI/CD pipeline with CodePipeline or GitHub Actions
  5. Deploy to ECS Fargate or EKS for production
  6. Enable cross-region replication for multi-region deployments
  7. Monitor with CloudWatch and set up alerts
💡 Pro Tip

Start with the free tier (500 MB storage for 12 months), experiment with basic features, then gradually enable advanced features like Enhanced Scanning and cross-region replication as your anihpj project grows.

AWS CloudTrail

Category: Management & Governance, Security & Compliance  |  Use Case: Audit Logging, Compliance, Security Analysis, Governance  |  Pricing: FREE for Management Events (90 days), Pay for Data Events  |  Last Updated: January 2026  |  Documentation: AWS CloudTrail Docs

Comprehensive audit logging service that records API activity across your AWS infrastructure. Perfect for tracking changes, ensuring compliance, and investigating security incidents in your anihpj Django job portal environment.

📋 Overview & Introduction

AWS CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your AWS account. With CloudTrail, you can log, continuously monitor, and retain account activity related to actions across your AWS infrastructure. CloudTrail provides event history of your AWS account activity, including actions taken through the AWS Management Console, AWS SDKs, command line tools, and other AWS services.

🎯 Key Benefits
📝

Complete Audit Trail

Track every API call made in your AWS account with comprehensive logging

  • All API activity captured
  • 90-day event history FREE
  • Immutable audit logs
💰

FREE Service

First trail completely free with management events at no cost

  • No setup fees
  • Management events free
  • Pay only for data events
🔒

Security & Compliance

Meet regulatory requirements with detailed audit trails

  • GDPR compliant
  • PCI DSS, HIPAA ready
  • SOC 2 evidence
⚡

Real-time Monitoring

CloudWatch integration for immediate security alerts

  • SNS notifications
  • Custom CloudWatch alarms
  • Lambda event processing
🔍

Forensics & Investigation

Detailed event history for security incident analysis

  • Complete API context
  • User identity tracking
  • Timeline reconstruction
🌍

Multi-Region Support

Single trail covers all AWS regions automatically

  • Global visibility
  • Centralized logging
  • Cross-region analysis
📊

Advanced Analysis

Query logs with Amazon Athena SQL for deep insights

  • SQL-based queries
  • Cost attribution reports
  • Security analysis
🤖

AI-Powered Insights

Machine learning detects unusual API activity patterns

  • Anomaly detection
  • Automated baselining
  • Threat intelligence
💡 When to Use CloudTrail for anihpj: Essential when you need to track who deployed Django code, monitor S3 access patterns for user uploads, audit database configuration changes, investigate security incidents, meet compliance requirements (PCI DSS for payment processing), or track resource costs by user activity.
⚖️ CloudTrail vs. Other AWS Services
Service Primary Focus What It Tracks Use with CloudTrail Cost
CloudTrail API audit logging All AWS API calls (who did what) - ✅ FREE (management events)
CloudWatch Performance monitoring Metrics, logs, alarms ✅ Receive CloudTrail logs $0.50/GB ingestion
AWS Config Configuration tracking Resource state changes ✅ Correlate who changed what $0.003 per config item
GuardDuty Threat detection ML-powered security threats ✅ Analyzes CloudTrail logs $4.50/million events
VPC Flow Logs Network traffic IP traffic flows ⚡ Complementary network logging $0.50/GB ingested
Access Analyzer Permission analysis IAM access patterns ✅ Validates IAM from CloudTrail ✅ FREE

│                     📊 WHAT CLOUDTRAIL TRACKS FOR ANIHPJ                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                  │
│  ✅ WHO:     Which IAM user/role performed the action                             │
│  ✅ WHAT:    Which API call was made (RunInstances, CreateBucket, etc.)           │
│  ✅ WHEN:    Exact timestamp with microsecond precision                           │
│  ✅ WHERE:   Source IP address, region, and availability zone                     │
│  ✅ HOW:     Method used (Console, CLI, SDK, CloudFormation, Terraform)           │
│  ✅ RESULT:  Success or failure with error codes                                  │
│  ✅ CONTEXT: Request parameters, response elements, resource ARNs                 │
│                                                                                  │
│  🎯 Critical Use Cases for anihpj Production:                                     │
│  • Track Django application deployments to EC2/ECS                               │
│  • Monitor S3 access for user-uploaded resumes and photos                        │
│  • Audit RDS database configuration changes                                      │
│  • Detect unauthorized IAM permission modifications                              │
│  • Investigate security incidents and data breaches                              │
│  • Meet compliance requirements (PCI DSS, GDPR, SOC 2)                           │
│  • Attribute costs - identify who created expensive resources                     │
│  • Debug infrastructure issues with change correlation                            │
│                                                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 Core Concepts & Components

Understanding CloudTrail core concepts is essential for implementing comprehensive audit logging for your anihpj Django job portal. Master these components to design effective governance and compliance strategies.

🛤️ Trail Types & Configuration
🛤️

Trail

Configuration that enables delivery of CloudTrail events to S3

  • Management events FREE
  • Data events optional
  • Multi-region support
  • Insight events available
📝

Event

JSON record of a single API call in your AWS account

  • 20+ fields of context
  • User identity details
  • Request parameters
  • Response information
📁

Event History

Built-in 90-day searchable repository

  • Completely FREE
  • No configuration needed
  • Console access
  • Management events only
🔍

CloudTrail Insights

ML-powered anomaly detection

  • Write event analysis
  • Baseline learning
  • Automatic alerts
  • Investigation assistance
📊 Event Types
⚙️

Management Events

Control plane operations (FREE for first trail)

Examples for anihpj:
  • CreateBucket, DeleteBucket
  • RunInstances, TerminateInstances
  • CreateDBInstance, ModifyDBInstance
  • PutBucketPolicy, PutRole
💰 Cost: FREE (first trail)
📊 Volume: Low to moderate
📦

Data Events

Data plane operations (high volume)

Examples for anihpj:
  • S3: GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject
  • Lambda: Invoke function
  • DynamoDB: GetItem, PutItem, Query
  • S3 Object Lambda: Access Point calls
💰 Cost: $0.10 per 100K events
📊 Volume: Very high
🤖

Insight Events

Anomalous activity patterns detected by ML

Detects for anihpj:
  • Unusual API call rate increase
  • Spike in error rate patterns
  • Abnormal write activity bursts
  • Suspicious IAM activity
💰 Cost: $0.35 per 100K analyzed
📊 Volume: Low (alerts only)
📋 anihpj CloudTrail Event Example
{
  "eventTime": "2026-01-13T14:30:45Z",
  "eventSource": "s3.amazonaws.com",
  "eventName": "PutObject",
  "eventVersion": "1.09",
  "awsRegion": "us-east-1",
  "sourceIPAddress": "203.0.113.42",
  "userAgent": "aws-cli/2.15.0 Python/3.11.6",
  "userIdentity": {
    "type": "IAMUser",
    "principalId": "AIDACKCEVSQ6C2EXAMPLE",
    "arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/anihpj-developer",
    "accountId": "123456789012",
    "userName": "anihpj-developer"
  },
  "requestParameters": {
    "bucketName": "anihpj-user-uploads",
    "key": "resumes/user-12345/john-doe-resume.pdf",
    "x-amz-acl": "private",
    "x-amz-server-side-encryption": "AES256"
  },
  "responseElements": {
    "x-amz-request-id": "79104EXAMPLEB723",
    "x-amz-id-2": "EXAMPLE123/5678abcdefghijklambdaisawesome/mnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGH",
    "x-amz-server-side-encryption": "AES256"
  },
  "resources": [
    {
      "type": "AWS::S3::Object",
      "ARN": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-user-uploads/resumes/user-12345/john-doe-resume.pdf"
    },
    {
      "type": "AWS::S3::Bucket", 
      "ARN": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-user-uploads"
    }
  ],
  "requestID": "79104EXAMPLEB723",
  "eventID": "e3c6f7ad-b3b0-4ead-9d18-example12345",
  "readOnly": false,
  "eventType": "AwsApiCall",
  "managementEvent": false,
  "recipientAccountId": "123456789012"
}

What's Tracked Control plane operations Data plane operations Anomalous activity patterns
Examples CreateBucket, RunInstances GetObject, PutObject, Invoke Unusual API call spikes
Cost ✅ FREE (first trail) $0.10 per 100K events $0.35 per 100K analyzed
Volume Low to moderate ⚠️ Very high Low (alerts only)
