CloudTrail helps meet various compliance and regulatory requirements for anihpj:

🇪🇺 GDPR (EU Data Protection)

Requirements Met:

  • Article 30: Record of processing activities ✅
  • Article 32: Security incident tracking ✅
  • Article 33: Breach notification evidence ✅
  • Data access audit trails ✅
💳 PCI DSS (Payment Card Security)

Requirements Met:

  • Requirement 10.1: Audit trail for all access ✅
  • Requirement 10.2: Automated audit trail ✅
  • Requirement 10.3: Tamper-resistant logs ✅
  • Log retention (90 days minimum) ✅
🏥 HIPAA (Healthcare Data)

Requirements Met:

  • §164.308(a)(1): Access logging ✅
  • §164.312(b): Audit controls ✅
  • §164.308(a)(5): Security incident procedures ✅
  • Encryption & log protection ✅
📊 SOC 2 (Service Organization Control)

Trust Principles:

  • Security: System access monitoring ✅
  • Availability: Change tracking ✅
  • Confidentiality: Data access audit ✅
  • Privacy: User activity logging ✅
🔒 ISO 27001 (Security Management)

Controls Met:

  • A.12.4.1: Event logging ✅
  • A.12.4.2: Log protection ✅
  • A.12.4.3: Administrator logs ✅
  • A.12.4.4: Clock synchronization ✅
🏛️ FedRAMP (Federal Government)

Requirements:

  • AU-2: Audit events ✅
  • AU-3: Audit record content ✅
  • AU-6: Audit review/analysis ✅
  • AU-9: Audit protection ✅
🚨 Security Alerts & Incident Response

Set up automated alerts for critical security events in your CloudTrail logs:

⚠️ Critical Events to Alert On
🔴 Critical
  • Root account usage
  • IAM policy changes
  • CloudTrail disabled
  • S3 public access granted
🟡 High
  • Failed API attempts
  • Access key creation
  • Resource deletion
  • Config rule changes
🚀 CloudWatch Alarm for Root Account Usage 
# Create metric filter for root account usage
aws logs put-metric-filter \
  --log-group-name /aws/cloudtrail/anihpj \
  --filter-name RootAccountUsage \
  --filter-pattern '{ $.userIdentity.type = "Root" }' \
  --metric-transformations metricName=RootAccountUsage,metricNamespace=CloudTrail,metricValue=1

# Create alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
  --alarm-name anihpj-root-usage \
  --metric-name RootAccountUsage \
  --namespace CloudTrail \
  --statistic Sum \
  --period 300 \
  --threshold 1 \
  --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold \
  --evaluation-periods 1
Generate Compliance Report for anihpj
# anihpj/jobpost/compliance_reports.py
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from collections import defaultdict

class ComplianceReporter:
    """Generate compliance reports for anihpj audit requirements"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
        self.s3 = boto3.client('s3')
    
    def generate_gdpr_access_report(self, user_email, days=90):
        """
        GDPR Article 15: Right of access - show all data access
        
        Args:
            user_email: User's email to track
            days: Look back period (default: 90 days)
        
        Returns:
            dict: All AWS resource access by this user
        """
        start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=days)
        
        events = self.cloudtrail.lookup_events(
            LookupAttributes=[{
                'AttributeKey': 'Username',
                'AttributeValue': user_email
            }],
            StartTime=start_time,
            MaxResults=1000
        )
        
        report = {
            'user': user_email,
            'period': f'Last {days} days',
            'total_actions': len(events['Events']),
            'by_service': defaultdict(int),
            'data_accessed': []
        }
        
        for event in events['Events']:
            service = event['EventSource'].split('.')[0]
            report['by_service'][service] += 1
            
            # Track data access
            if event['EventName'] in ['GetObject', 'PutObject', 'GetItem']:
                report['data_accessed'].append({
                    'time': event['EventTime'],
                    'action': event['EventName'],
                    'resource': event.get('ResourceType', 'Unknown')
                })
        
        return report
    
    def pci_dss_audit_trail(self, start_date, end_date):
        """
        PCI DSS Requirement 10: Track all access to cardholder data
        
        Returns:
            dict: Comprehensive audit trail
        """
        events = self.cloudtrail.lookup_events(
            StartTime=start_date,
            EndTime=end_date,
            MaxResults=10000
        )
        
        audit_trail = {
            'period': {
                'start': start_date.isoformat(),
                'end': end_date.isoformat()
            },
            'total_events': len(events['Events']),
            'user_activity': defaultdict(int),
            'failed_access': [],
            'data_modifications': [],
            'administrative_actions': []
        }
        
        for event in events['Events']:
            username = event.get('Username', 'Unknown')
            audit_trail['user_activity'][username] += 1
            
            # Track failed access attempts
            if 'errorCode' in event:
                audit_trail['failed_access'].append({
                    'time': event['EventTime'],
                    'user': username,
                    'action': event['EventName'],
                    'error': event['errorCode']
                })
            
            # Track data modifications
            if event['EventName'] in ['PutObject', 'DeleteObject', 
                                     'PutItem', 'DeleteItem']:
                audit_trail['data_modifications'].append({
                    'time': event['EventTime'],
                    'user': username,
                    'action': event['EventName']
                })
            
            # Track admin actions
            if event['EventName'] in ['CreateUser', 'DeleteUser', 
                                     'PutBucketPolicy']:
                audit_trail['administrative_actions'].append({
                    'time': event['EventTime'],
                    'user': username,
                    'action': event['EventName']
                })
        
        return audit_trail
    
    def hipaa_security_incident_log(self, incident_date):
        """
        HIPAA §164.308(a)(6): Security incident procedures
        
        Args:
            incident_date: Date of suspected incident
        
        Returns:
            list: All events around incident time
        """
        # Look 24 hours before and after incident
        start = incident_date - timedelta(hours=24)
        end = incident_date + timedelta(hours=24)
        
        events = self.cloudtrail.lookup_events(
            StartTime=start,
            EndTime=end,
            MaxResults=5000
        )
        
        timeline = []
        for event in events['Events']:
            timeline.append({
                'timestamp': event['EventTime'],
                'user': event.get('Username', 'Unknown'),
                'action': event['EventName'],
                'service': event['EventSource'],
                'source_ip': event.get('SourceIPAddress', 'N/A'),
                'result': 'Success' if 'errorCode' not in event else 'Failed',
                'details': event.get('errorCode', '')
            })
        
        return sorted(timeline, key=lambda x: x['timestamp'])

# Usage in Django view
from .compliance_reports import ComplianceReporter

def gdpr_data_access_request(request):
    """Handle GDPR Article 15 - Right of Access"""
    if not request.user.is_authenticated:
        return HttpResponseForbidden()
    
    reporter = ComplianceReporter()
    report = reporter.generate_gdpr_access_report(
        user_email=request.user.email,
        days=90
    )
    
    return JsonResponse(report)
Compliance Checklist for anihpj
✅ CLOUDTRAIL COMPLIANCE SETUP CHECKLIST

1. Enable CloudTrail in ALL regions
   ✅ Multi-region trail configured
   ✅ Include global service events (IAM, CloudFront)

2. Configure Log Protection
   ✅ SSE-KMS encryption enabled
   ✅ Log file validation enabled
   ✅ S3 bucket versioning enabled
   ✅ S3 Object Lock (for tamper-proof logs)

3. Set Retention Policies
   ✅ S3 logs: 7 years (2,555 days) for PCI DSS
   ✅ CloudWatch Logs: 90 days minimum
   ✅ Lifecycle policy to Glacier after 90 days

4. Configure Monitoring & Alerting
   ✅ CloudWatch alarms for unauthorized access
   ✅ SNS notifications for security events
   ✅ CloudTrail Insights enabled

5. Implement Access Controls
   ✅ IAM policies restrict CloudTrail access
   ✅ S3 bucket policy prevents unauthorized access
   ✅ MFA required for log deletion

6. Regular Audits & Reviews
   ✅ Weekly review of failed API calls
   ✅ Monthly access audit reports
   ✅ Quarterly compliance assessments
   ✅ Annual penetration testing

7. Documentation
   ✅ Audit trail procedures documented
   ✅ Incident response playbook created
   ✅ Data retention policy published
   ✅ Compliance mapping maintained
💡 Compliance Tip for anihpj: Enable CloudTrail log file validation and S3 Object Lock to create tamper-proof audit trails. This is critical for PCI DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 compliance. Most auditors will specifically ask for proof that logs cannot be modified or deleted.

🔗 Integrations & Ecosystem

📊 CloudWatch Integration

Send CloudTrail logs to CloudWatch for real-time monitoring and alerting:

Create CloudWatch Alarm for Security Events
# Create metric filter for unauthorized API calls
aws logs put-metric-filter \
    --log-group-name /aws/cloudtrail/anihpj \
    --filter-name UnauthorizedAPICalls \
    --filter-pattern '{ $.errorCode = "*UnauthorizedOperation" || $.errorCode = "AccessDenied*" }' \
    --metric-transformations \
        metricName=UnauthorizedAPICalls,\
        metricNamespace=CloudTrail,\
        metricValue=1

# Create alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-unauthorized-api-calls" \
    --alarm-description "Alert on unauthorized API calls" \
    --metric-name UnauthorizedAPICalls \
    --namespace CloudTrail \
    --statistic Sum \
    --period 300 \
    --threshold 5 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:security-alerts

# 🚨 Now you'll get alerted when someone tries unauthorized access!
🔎 Athena Queries

Query CloudTrail logs using SQL with Amazon Athena:

Useful Athena Queries for anihpj
-- Who deleted EC2 instances in the last 7 days?
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.username,
    sourceipaddress,
    requestparameters
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE eventname = 'TerminateInstances'
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -7, current_date)
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;

-- Find failed login attempts
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.username,
    sourceipaddress,
    errorcode,
    errormessage
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE errorcode IN ('AccessDenied', 'UnauthorizedOperation')
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -1, current_date)
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;

-- Track S3 bucket policy changes
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.username,
    eventname,
    requestparameters,
    resources[0].arn AS bucket
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE eventname IN ('PutBucketPolicy', 'DeleteBucketPolicy', 'PutBucketAcl')
    AND resources[0].arn LIKE '%anihpj%'
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;

-- Cost analysis - who's creating expensive resources?
SELECT 
    useridentity.username,
    eventname,
    COUNT(*) as count
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE eventname IN ('RunInstances', 'CreateDBInstance', 'CreateLoadBalancer')
    AND eventtime > date_add('month', -1, current_date)
GROUP BY useridentity.username, eventname
ORDER BY count DESC;
⚡ Lambda Integration

Trigger Lambda functions automatically when CloudTrail events occur:

Auto-Tag Resources Created for anihpj
# lambda_auto_tag.py
import boto3
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Auto-tag resources when created"""
    
    # Parse CloudTrail event
    ct_event = json.loads(event['Records'][0]['Sns']['Message'])
    
    event_name = ct_event['detail']['eventName']
    user = ct_event['detail']['userIdentity']['userName']
    
    # Tag EC2 instances
    if event_name == 'RunInstances':
        ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
        instance_ids = []
        
        for item in ct_event['detail']['responseElements']['instancesSet']['items']:
            instance_ids.append(item['instanceId'])
        
        # Apply tags
        ec2.create_tags(
            Resources=instance_ids,
            Tags=[
                {'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                {'Key': 'CreatedBy', 'Value': user},
                {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': 'production'},
                {'Key': 'ManagedBy', 'Value': 'CloudTrail-Lambda'}
            ]
        )
        
        print(f"Tagged {len(instance_ids)} instances created by {user}")
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}
📧 Amazon SNS Integration

Get real-time alerts when CloudTrail delivers new log files to S3:

Set Up SNS Notifications for anihpj
# Step 1: Create SNS topic
aws sns create-topic \
    --name anihpj-cloudtrail-notifications

# Step 2: Subscribe email to topic
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:anihpj-cloudtrail-notifications \
    --protocol email \
    --notification-endpoint security@anihpj.com

# Step 3: Update CloudTrail to send notifications
aws cloudtrail update-trail \
    --name anihpj-audit-trail \
    --sns-topic-name anihpj-cloudtrail-notifications

# Now you'll receive email when logs are delivered!
Filter SNS Notifications (Lambda + SNS)
# lambda_sns_filter.py - Only alert on critical events
import boto3
import json

sns = boto3.client('sns')
CRITICAL_EVENTS = [
    'DeleteBucket',
    'PutBucketPolicy',
    'DeleteUser',
    'CreateAccessKey',
    'DeleteTrail',
    'StopLogging'
]

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Filter CloudTrail events and send SNS for critical ones"""
    
    # Parse S3 event (CloudTrail log delivered)
    for record in event['Records']:
        bucket = record['s3']['bucket']['name']
        key = record['s3']['object']['key']
        
        # Download and parse CloudTrail log
        s3 = boto3.client('s3')
        log_obj = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)
        log_data = json.loads(log_obj['Body'].read().decode('utf-8'))
        
        # Check for critical events
        for ct_record in log_data['Records']:
            event_name = ct_record['eventName']
            
            if event_name in CRITICAL_EVENTS:
                # Send alert
                sns.publish(
                    TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:anihpj-security-alerts',
                    Subject=f'🚨 Critical AWS Event: {event_name}',
                    Message=f"""
CRITICAL AWS EVENT DETECTED FOR ANIHPJ!

Event: {event_name}
User: {ct_record['userIdentity'].get('userName', 'Unknown')}
Time: {ct_record['eventTime']}
Source IP: {ct_record.get('sourceIPAddress', 'N/A')}
Region: {ct_record.get('awsRegion', 'N/A')}

Details:
{json.dumps(ct_record, indent=2)}

Action Required: Review immediately in AWS Console.
                    """
                )
                
                print(f"Alert sent for {event_name} by {ct_record['userIdentity'].get('userName')}")
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}
CloudFormation: SNS + CloudTrail Integration
Resources:
  SecurityAlertsTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: anihpj-cloudtrail-security-alerts
      DisplayName: anihpj CloudTrail Security Alerts
      Subscription:
        - Endpoint: security@anihpj.com
          Protocol: email
        - Endpoint: !GetAtt SecurityAlertsLambda.Arn
          Protocol: lambda

  SecurityAlertsLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: anihpj-cloudtrail-alert-processor
      Runtime: python3.11
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          # Lambda code here (filter critical events)

  CloudTrailTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: anihpj-cloudtrail-notifications

  CloudTrailTopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref CloudTrailTopic
      PolicyDocument:
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: cloudtrail.amazonaws.com
            Action: SNS:Publish
            Resource: !Ref CloudTrailTopic

  AuditTrail:
    Type: AWS::CloudTrail::Trail
    Properties:
      TrailName: anihpj-audit-trail
      S3BucketName: !Ref LogsBucket
      SnsTopicName: !GetAtt CloudTrailTopic.TopicName
      IsLogging: true
      IsMultiRegionTrail: true

Outputs:
  TopicArn:
    Value: !Ref SecurityAlertsTopic
    Description: SNS topic for security alerts
💡 Pro Tip: Use Lambda to filter CloudTrail SNS notifications. Without filtering, you'll receive hundreds of emails daily. Create a Lambda function that only sends SNS alerts for critical events like DeleteBucket, PutBucketPolicy, or CreateAccessKey.
Notification Type When to Use Example Use Case for anihpj
📧 Email (Direct) Low-volume alerts only Critical security events (10-20/month)
⚡ Lambda (Filtered) High-volume with filtering All events → filter → alert on critical only
📱 SMS (SNS) Urgent incidents Root account usage, trail deletion
💬 Slack/Teams (Lambda) Team collaboration Post security events to #security channel
🎫 PagerDuty/Opsgenie On-call incidents After-hours security incidents
🛡️ AWS GuardDuty Integration

Enhance threat detection by integrating CloudTrail with GuardDuty for AI-powered security analysis:

🚀 GuardDuty + CloudTrail Setup
🔍 Threat Detection
  • Malicious IP addresses
  • Unusual API patterns
  • Credential compromise
  • Data exfiltration
🤖 Machine Learning
  • Behavioral analysis
  • Anomaly detection
  • Threat intelligence
  • False positive reduction
# Enable GuardDuty
aws guardduty create-detector --enable

# GuardDuty automatically analyzes CloudTrail events
# No additional configuration needed - it uses existing CloudTrail data

# Create GuardDuty findings filter for anihpj resources
aws guardduty create-filter \
  --detector-id 12abc34d567e8fa90b12c3d456e789f0 \
  --name anihpj-resources-filter \
  --description "Filter for anihpj-related security findings" \
  --action ARCHIVE \
  --finding-criteria '{
    "Criterion": {
      "resource.resourceType": {
        "Eq": ["S3Bucket"]
      },
      "resource.s3BucketDetails.name": {
        "Contains": ["anihpj"]
      }
    }
  }'
🔐 AWS Config Integration

Combine CloudTrail audit logs with AWS Config to track configuration changes and compliance:

📋 Config + CloudTrail for Compliance
💡 Pro Tip: CloudTrail tells you "who did what", AWS Config tells you "what changed when" 
# Enable AWS Config
aws configservice put-configuration-recorder \
  --configuration-recorder name=anihpj-config-recorder,roleARN=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/config-role \
  --recording-group allSupported=true,includeGlobalResourceTypes=true

# Create Config rule for S3 bucket encryption
aws configservice put-config-rule \
  --config-rule '{
    "ConfigRuleName": "anihpj-s3-encryption-enabled",
    "Description": "Checks if S3 buckets have encryption enabled",
    "Source": {
      "Owner": "AWS",
      "SourceIdentifier": "S3_BUCKET_SSL_REQUESTS_ONLY"
    },
    "Scope": {
      "ComplianceResourceTypes": ["AWS::S3::Bucket"]
    }
  }'

# Query compliance status
aws configservice get-compliance-details-by-config-rule \
  --config-rule-name anihpj-s3-encryption-enabled
🔍 Combined Analysis Example: 
# Find who disabled S3 encryption (Config + CloudTrail)
# 1. Config shows encryption was disabled
# 2. CloudTrail shows who made the API call

aws logs filter-log-events \
  --log-group-name /aws/cloudtrail/anihpj \
  --filter-pattern '{ $.eventName = "PutBucketEncryption" && $.requestParameters.bucketName = "anihpj-uploads" }' \
  --start-time 1642723200000

📈 Monitoring & Analysis

🔍 Common Security Queries for anihpj
🚨 Security Incidents
  • Unauthorized API calls
  • Failed login attempts
  • IAM policy modifications
  • Security group changes
  • Root account usage
🔍 Resource Changes
  • EC2 instance lifecycle
  • S3 bucket policy updates
  • RDS configuration changes
  • Lambda function updates
  • Resource deletions
📊 Compliance Audits
  • Who accessed what data
  • Configuration drift
  • Encryption status changes
  • Backup policy compliance
  • Access pattern analysis
🚨 Critical Alerts for anihpj
Alert Trigger Action
🚨 Unauthorized Access 5+ failed API calls in 5 minutes SNS alert to security team, investigate immediately
🔴 Root Account Use Any root account activity Critical alert - root should never be used
⚠️ IAM Changes IAM policy/user modifications Review changes, ensure authorized
🚫 Security Group Inbound rule allowing 0.0.0.0/0 Review rule, restrict if possible
🗑️ Resource Deletion Production resource deleted Verify intentional, check backups
🔒 Security Analysis with CloudTrail

Use CloudTrail logs for comprehensive security analysis and threat detection:

🚨 Threat Detection
  • Brute Force Attacks: Repeated failed login attempts
  • Privilege Escalation: Unusual IAM permission changes
  • Data Exfiltration: Large S3 downloads
  • Credential Theft: Access keys created/exposed
  • Resource Hijacking: Unauthorized EC2 launches
🔍 Anomaly Detection
  • Unusual Access Patterns: API calls from new IPs
  • Off-Hours Activity: Actions during non-business hours
  • Geographic Anomalies: Access from unexpected countries
  • Volume Spikes: Sudden increase in API calls
  • Service Abuse: Unusual service usage patterns
📊 Security Metrics
  • Failed Access Rate: % of denied API calls
  • IAM Changes: Frequency of permission updates
  • Resource Turnover: Created vs deleted resources
  • User Activity: Active users per day/week
  • Service Usage: Most accessed AWS services
Security Analysis Queries (Athena SQL)
-- 1. Find all failed authentication attempts (past 7 days)
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.principalid,
    sourceipaddress,
    eventname,
    errorcode,
    errormessage
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    errorcode IN ('AccessDenied', 'UnauthorizedOperation')
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -7, now())
ORDER BY eventtime DESC
LIMIT 100;

-- 2. Detect potential brute force attacks
SELECT 
    sourceipaddress,
    COUNT(*) as failed_attempts,
    MIN(eventtime) as first_attempt,
    MAX(eventtime) as last_attempt
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    errorcode = 'AccessDenied'
    AND eventtime > date_add('hour', -24, now())
GROUP BY sourceipaddress
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10
ORDER BY failed_attempts DESC;

-- 3. Find root account usage (CRITICAL - should never happen)
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    eventname,
    sourceipaddress,
    useragent
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    useridentity.type = 'Root'
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -30, now())
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;

-- 4. Track IAM permission changes
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.principalid,
    eventname,
    requestparameters,
    sourceipaddress
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    eventsource = 'iam.amazonaws.com'
    AND eventname IN (
        'PutUserPolicy',
        'PutRolePolicy',
        'AttachUserPolicy',
        'AttachRolePolicy',
        'CreateAccessKey'
    )
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -7, now())
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;

-- 5. Identify data exfiltration attempts (large S3 downloads)
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.principalid,
    requestparameters.bucketName,
    requestparameters.key,
    sourceipaddress,
    COUNT(*) OVER (
        PARTITION BY useridentity.principalid, 
                    DATE(eventtime)
    ) as downloads_today
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    eventsource = 's3.amazonaws.com'
    AND eventname = 'GetObject'
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -1, now())
HAVING downloads_today > 100
ORDER BY downloads_today DESC;

-- 6. Detect unusual geographic access
SELECT 
    useridentity.principalid,
    sourceipaddress,
    eventname,
    awsregion,
    COUNT(*) as access_count
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    eventtime > date_add('day', -7, now())
    AND sourceipaddress NOT LIKE '10.%'  -- Exclude internal IPs
GROUP BY 
    useridentity.principalid,
    sourceipaddress,
    eventname,
    awsregion
HAVING COUNT(*) > 50
ORDER BY access_count DESC;

-- 7. Find security group changes (potential backdoors)
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.principalid,
    eventname,
    requestparameters,
    sourceipaddress
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    eventsource = 'ec2.amazonaws.com'
    AND eventname IN (
        'AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress',
        'RevokeSecurityGroupIngress',
        'AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress',
        'RevokeSecurityGroupEgress'
    )
    AND requestparameters LIKE '%0.0.0.0/0%'  -- Open to internet
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;

-- 8. Track resource deletions (potential sabotage)
SELECT 
    eventtime,
    useridentity.principalid,
    eventname,
    requestparameters,
    resources[1].ARN as deleted_resource
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
    eventname LIKE 'Delete%'
    OR eventname LIKE 'Terminate%'
    AND eventtime > date_add('day', -7, now())
ORDER BY eventtime DESC;
Automated Security Dashboard (Python + Django)
# anihpj/jobpost/security_dashboard.py
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from collections import defaultdict

class SecurityDashboard:
    """Real-time security dashboard for anihpj CloudTrail analysis"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.athena = boto3.client('athena')
        self.cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
    
    def get_security_metrics(self):
        """Generate security metrics for dashboard"""
        
        # Query last 24 hours of CloudTrail events
        end_time = datetime.utcnow()
        start_time = end_time - timedelta(hours=24)
        
        events = self.cloudtrail.lookup_events(
            StartTime=start_time,
            EndTime=end_time,
            MaxResults=10000
        )
        
        metrics = {
            'total_events': len(events['Events']),
            'failed_access': 0,
            'iam_changes': 0,
            'resource_deletions': 0,
            'root_usage': 0,
            'unique_users': set(),
            'unique_ips': set(),
            'top_users': defaultdict(int),
            'top_services': defaultdict(int),
            'alerts': []
        }
        
        for event in events['Events']:
            username = event.get('Username', 'Unknown')
            event_name = event['EventName']
            source_ip = event.get('SourceIPAddress', 'N/A')
            service = event['EventSource'].split('.')[0]
            
            # Track unique users and IPs
            metrics['unique_users'].add(username)
            metrics['unique_ips'].add(source_ip)
            
            # Count by user and service
            metrics['top_users'][username] += 1
            metrics['top_services'][service] += 1
            
            # Check for security issues
            if 'errorCode' in event:
                metrics['failed_access'] += 1
                
                # Alert on brute force
                if metrics['failed_access'] > 20:
                    metrics['alerts'].append({
                        'severity': 'high',
                        'type': 'brute_force',
                        'message': f'Potential brute force: {metrics["failed_access"]} failed attempts'
                    })
            
            # Track IAM changes
            if service == 'iam' and event_name.startswith(('Put', 'Create', 'Attach')):
                metrics['iam_changes'] += 1
                metrics['alerts'].append({
                    'severity': 'medium',
                    'type': 'iam_change',
                    'message': f'{username} performed {event_name}'
                })
            
            # Track deletions
            if event_name.startswith(('Delete', 'Terminate')):
                metrics['resource_deletions'] += 1
                metrics['alerts'].append({
                    'severity': 'medium',
                    'type': 'deletion',
                    'message': f'{username} deleted resource: {event_name}'
                })
            
            # Track root usage (CRITICAL)
            if event.get('UserIdentity', {}).get('type') == 'Root':
                metrics['root_usage'] += 1
                metrics['alerts'].append({
                    'severity': 'critical',
                    'type': 'root_usage',
                    'message': f'ROOT ACCOUNT USED: {event_name} from {source_ip}'
                })
        
        # Convert sets to counts
        metrics['unique_users'] = len(metrics['unique_users'])
        metrics['unique_ips'] = len(metrics['unique_ips'])
        
        # Get top 5 users and services
        metrics['top_users'] = dict(sorted(
            metrics['top_users'].items(),
            key=lambda x: x[1],
            reverse=True
        )[:5])
        
        metrics['top_services'] = dict(sorted(
            metrics['top_services'].items(),
            key=lambda x: x[1],
            reverse=True
        )[:5])
        
        return metrics

# Django view
from .security_dashboard import SecurityDashboard

def security_dashboard_view(request):
    """Display real-time security dashboard"""
    if not request.user.is_staff:
        return HttpResponseForbidden()
    
    dashboard = SecurityDashboard()
    metrics = dashboard.get_security_metrics()
    
    context = {
        'metrics': metrics,
        'last_updated': datetime.utcnow()
    }
    
    return render(request, 'jobpost/security_dashboard.html', context)
🔒 Security Analysis Best Practice: Set up automated Athena queries that run daily to detect security anomalies. Store results in a separate security analytics database for trend analysis. Use CloudTrail Insights to automatically detect unusual patterns without manual queries.

⌨️ API & CLI

🖥️ AWS CLI Commands
📋 Essential CloudTrail CLI Commands for anihpj
Create a Trail 
# Create multi-region trail for anihpj
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name anihpj-cloudtrail-logs \
  --include-global-service-events \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --enable-log-file-validation

# Start logging
aws cloudtrail start-logging --name anihpj-audit-trail
Configure Trail Settings 
# Enable CloudWatch Logs
aws cloudtrail update-trail \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --cloud-watch-logs-log-group-arn arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/cloudtrail/anihpj \
  --cloud-watch-logs-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CloudTrailToCloudWatchLogs

# Enable SNS notifications
aws cloudtrail update-trail \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --sns-topic-name anihpj-cloudtrail-alerts

# Enable KMS encryption
aws cloudtrail update-trail \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --kms-key-id arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/your-key-id
Configure Event Selectors (Data Events) 
# Enable S3 data events for specific bucket
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --event-selectors '[{
    "ReadWriteType": "All",
    "IncludeManagementEvents": true,
    "DataResources": [{
      "Type": "AWS::S3::Object",
      "Values": ["arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-uploads/*"]
    }]
  }]'

# Enable Lambda data events
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --event-selectors '[{
    "ReadWriteType": "All",
    "IncludeManagementEvents": true,
    "DataResources": [{
      "Type": "AWS::Lambda::Function",
      "Values": ["arn:aws:lambda:*:123456789012:function/*"]
    }]
  }]'
Query & Analyze Events 
# Search recent events (last 7 days)
aws cloudtrail lookup-events --max-results 50

# Filter by event name
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=CreateBucket \
  --max-results 20

# Filter by username
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=django-api-user

# Filter by resource name
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=ResourceName,AttributeValue=anihpj-uploads

# Search specific time range
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --start-time 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z \
  --end-time 2026-01-12T23:59:59Z \
  --max-results 100
Manage Trail Status 
# Get trail status
aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name anihpj-audit-trail

# Describe trail configuration
aws cloudtrail describe-trails --trail-name-list anihpj-audit-trail

# List all trails in account
aws cloudtrail list-trails

# Get event selectors
aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail

# Stop logging (caution!)
aws cloudtrail stop-logging --name anihpj-audit-trail

# Delete trail (WARNING: permanent!)
aws cloudtrail delete-trail --name anihpj-audit-trail
CloudTrail Insights 
# Enable CloudTrail Insights
aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --insight-selectors '[{"InsightType": "ApiCallRateInsight"}]'

# Get current insight settings
aws cloudtrail get-insight-selectors --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail
💡 CLI Pro Tip: Use aws cloudtrail lookup-events to instantly search 90-day Event History without waiting for S3 log delivery (15-minute delay). Perfect for quick security investigations!
🐍 Boto3 SDK Examples
📦 Python Boto3 Integration for anihpj Django App
1️⃣ Basic CloudTrail Client Setup 
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

# Initialize CloudTrail client
cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail', region_name='us-east-1')

# Get trail status
def get_trail_status(trail_name):
    """Check if CloudTrail is actively logging"""
    try:
        response = cloudtrail.get_trail_status(Name=trail_name)
        return {
            'is_logging': response['IsLogging'],
            'latest_delivery': response.get('LatestDeliveryTime'),
            'latest_notification': response.get('LatestNotificationTime')
        }
    except ClientError as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}")
        return None

# Usage
status = get_trail_status('anihpj-audit-trail')
print(f"Trail is logging: {status['is_logging']}")
2️⃣ Search CloudTrail Events 
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def search_cloudtrail_events(event_name=None, username=None, days=7):
    """Search CloudTrail events with filters"""
    cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
    
    # Build lookup attributes
    lookup_attrs = []
    if event_name:
        lookup_attrs.append({
            'AttributeKey': 'EventName',
            'AttributeValue': event_name
        })
    if username:
        lookup_attrs.append({
            'AttributeKey': 'Username',
            'AttributeValue': username
        })
    
    # Calculate time range
    start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=days)
    
    # Search events
    try:
        response = cloudtrail.lookup_events(
            LookupAttributes=lookup_attrs if lookup_attrs else None,
            StartTime=start_time,
            MaxResults=50
        )
        
        events = []
        for event in response.get('Events', []):
            events.append({
                'time': event['EventTime'],
                'event': event['EventName'],
                'user': event.get('Username', 'N/A'),
                'resources': event.get('Resources', [])
            })
        
        return events
    except ClientError as e:
        print(f"Error searching events: {e}")
        return []

# Usage examples
# Find all S3 bucket deletions
deletions = search_cloudtrail_events(event_name='DeleteBucket', days=30)

# Find all actions by specific user
user_actions = search_cloudtrail_events(username='django-api-user', days=7)
3️⃣ Create & Configure Trail 
def create_cloudtrail(trail_name, s3_bucket, kms_key_id=None):
    """Create a new CloudTrail with best practices"""
    cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
    
    try:
        # Create trail
        response = cloudtrail.create_trail(
            Name=trail_name,
            S3BucketName=s3_bucket,
            IncludeGlobalServiceEvents=True,
            IsMultiRegionTrail=True,
            EnableLogFileValidation=True,
            KmsKeyId=kms_key_id if kms_key_id else None
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Created trail: {response['TrailARN']}")
        
        # Start logging
        cloudtrail.start_logging(Name=trail_name)
        print(f"✅ Started logging for {trail_name}")
        
        return response
    except ClientError as e:
        print(f"❌ Error creating trail: {e}")
        return None

# Usage
create_cloudtrail(
    trail_name='anihpj-audit-trail',
    s3_bucket='anihpj-cloudtrail-logs',
    kms_key_id='arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/your-key'
)
4️⃣ Configure Data Events 
def enable_s3_data_events(trail_name, bucket_arn):
    """Enable S3 data events for specific bucket"""
    cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
    
    try:
        response = cloudtrail.put_event_selectors(
            TrailName=trail_name,
            EventSelectors=[{
                'ReadWriteType': 'All',
                'IncludeManagementEvents': True,
                'DataResources': [{
                    'Type': 'AWS::S3::Object',
                    'Values': [f'{bucket_arn}/*']
                }]
            }]
        )
        print(f"✅ Enabled S3 data events for {bucket_arn}")
        return response
    except ClientError as e:
        print(f"❌ Error: {e}")
        return None

# Usage
enable_s3_data_events(
    'anihpj-audit-trail',
    'arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-uploads'
)
5️⃣ Django Integration Class 
class CloudTrailManager:
    """Complete CloudTrail management for anihpj"""
    
    def __init__(self, trail_name='anihpj-audit-trail'):
        self.trail_name = trail_name
        self.client = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
    
    def is_logging(self):
        """Check if trail is actively logging"""
        status = self.client.get_trail_status(Name=self.trail_name)
        return status['IsLogging']
    
    def get_recent_security_events(self, hours=24):
        """Get security-related events from last N hours"""
        start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=hours)
        
        security_events = [
            'DeleteBucket', 'PutBucketPolicy', 'DeleteUser',
            'CreateAccessKey', 'DeleteTrail', 'StopLogging'
        ]
        
        all_events = []
        for event_name in security_events:
            response = self.client.lookup_events(
                LookupAttributes=[{
                    'AttributeKey': 'EventName',
                    'AttributeValue': event_name
                }],
                StartTime=start_time,
                MaxResults=50
            )
            all_events.extend(response.get('Events', []))
        
        return all_events
    
    def get_user_activity(self, username, days=7):
        """Track all actions by specific user"""
        start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=days)
        
        response = self.client.lookup_events(
            LookupAttributes=[{
                'AttributeKey': 'Username',
                'AttributeValue': username
            }],
            StartTime=start_time,
            MaxResults=100
        )
        
        return response.get('Events', [])

# Django view usage
from .cloudtrail import CloudTrailManager

def audit_report(request):
    """Generate CloudTrail audit report"""
    manager = CloudTrailManager()
    
    context = {
        'is_logging': manager.is_logging(),
        'security_events': manager.get_recent_security_events(hours=24),
        'user_activity': manager.get_user_activity(request.user.username)
    }
    
    return render(request, 'jobpost/audit_report.html', context)
💡 Boto3 Pro Tip: Use MaxResults parameter wisely. CloudTrail returns up to 50 events per call by default. For large-scale analysis, implement pagination with NextToken or use Athena to query S3 logs directly.
📚 API Reference
📖 CloudTrail API Overview
API Action Description Use Case Cost
CreateTrail Create a new CloudTrail Initial setup for anihpj audit logging FREE
StartLogging Start recording events Enable audit trail after configuration FREE
StopLogging Stop recording events Temporarily disable logging (not recommended) FREE
LookupEvents Search 90-day event history Quick security investigations FREE
GetTrailStatus Check trail logging status Monitoring & health checks FREE
DescribeTrails Get trail configuration Audit trail settings compliance FREE
UpdateTrail Modify trail settings Add SNS, CloudWatch, or KMS encryption FREE
PutEventSelectors Configure data events Enable S3/Lambda tracking $0.10 per 100K
GetEventSelectors View data event config Verify data event settings FREE
PutInsightSelectors Enable CloudTrail Insights Automatic anomaly detection $0.35 per 100K
ListTrails List all trails in account Multi-account governance FREE
DeleteTrail Delete a trail (permanent) Decommissioning (use with caution) FREE
📋 Official AWS Documentation
  • CloudTrail API Reference - Complete API documentation
  • Boto3 CloudTrail Documentation - Python SDK reference
  • AWS CLI CloudTrail Reference - Complete CLI command guide
  • CloudTrail Event Reference - Event structure documentation
🔑 Common API Response Fields 
{
  "Trail": {
    "Name": "anihpj-audit-trail",
    "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs",
    "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true,
    "IsMultiRegionTrail": true,
    "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail",
    "LogFileValidationEnabled": true,
    "KmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/xxx",
    "IsOrganizationTrail": false
  }
}
⚡ API Rate Limits
  • LookupEvents: 2 requests per second per account
  • CreateTrail: 1 request per 5 seconds
  • UpdateTrail: 1 request per 5 seconds
  • GetTrailStatus: 10 requests per second
  • DescribeTrails: 10 requests per second

💡 Tip: For high-volume event analysis, use Athena to query S3 logs instead of LookupEvents API to avoid rate limits.

📚 Complete API Documentation: Visit AWS CloudTrail API Reference for detailed information on all API actions, request parameters, response elements, and error codes.
🔧 Essential CLI Commands
📝 Common CloudTrail Commands 
# List all trails
aws cloudtrail describe-trails

# Get trail status
aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name anihpj-audit-trail

# Look up events
aws cloudtrail lookup-events --max-results 10

# Filter events by username
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=django-user

# Filter events by event name
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=PutObject

# Get event selectors (what events are being logged)
aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail

# Update event selectors to log data events
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --event-selectors '[{
    "ReadWriteType": "All",
    "IncludeManagementEvents": true,
    "DataResources": [{
      "Type": "AWS::S3::Object",
      "Values": ["arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-uploads/*"]
    }]
  }]'

# Get CloudTrail Insights configuration
aws cloudtrail get-insight-selectors --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail

# Enable CloudTrail Insights
aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --insight-selectors InsightType=ApiCallRateInsight
🐍 Python SDK (Boto3) Examples
📦 CloudTrail Operations with Python 
import boto3
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Initialize CloudTrail client
cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail', region_name='us-east-1')

# Example 1: Look up events from the last 24 hours
def get_recent_events():
    """Get CloudTrail events from last 24 hours"""
    start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=1)
    
    response = cloudtrail.lookup_events(
        LookupAttributes=[
            {
                'AttributeKey': 'EventSource',
                'AttributeValue': 's3.amazonaws.com'
            }
        ],
        StartTime=start_time,
        MaxResults=50
    )
    
    for event in response['Events']:
        print(f"{event['EventTime']} - {event['EventName']} by {event['Username']}")
        
# Example 2: Find all DeleteBucket events
def find_bucket_deletions():
    """Find S3 bucket deletion events"""
    response = cloudtrail.lookup_events(
        LookupAttributes=[
            {
                'AttributeKey': 'EventName',
                'AttributeValue': 'DeleteBucket'
            }
        ],
        MaxResults=50
    )
    
    for event in response['Events']:
        event_data = json.loads(event['CloudTrailEvent'])
        print(f"Bucket: {event_data['requestParameters']['bucketName']}")
        print(f"User: {event['Username']}")
        print(f"Time: {event['EventTime']}")
        print("---")

# Example 3: Get trail configuration
def get_trail_config(trail_name):
    """Get CloudTrail configuration"""
    response = cloudtrail.describe_trails(
        trailNameList=[trail_name],
        includeShadowTrails=True
    )
    
    for trail in response['trailList']:
        print(f"Trail: {trail['Name']}")
        print(f"S3 Bucket: {trail['S3BucketName']}")
        print(f"Multi-Region: {trail['IsMultiRegionTrail']}")
        
# Example 4: Get event selectors
def get_data_events_config(trail_name):
    """Get what data events are being logged"""
    response = cloudtrail.get_event_selectors(
        TrailName=trail_name
    )
    
    for selector in response['EventSelectors']:
        print(f"Management Events: {selector['IncludeManagementEvents']}")
        for data_resource in selector.get('DataResources', []):
            print(f"  Type: {data_resource['Type']}")
            print(f"  Values: {data_resource['Values']}")

# Example 5: Enable CloudTrail Insights
def enable_insights(trail_name):
    """Enable CloudTrail Insights for anomaly detection"""
    response = cloudtrail.put_insight_selectors(
        TrailName=trail_name,
        InsightSelectors=[
            {
                'InsightType': 'ApiCallRateInsight'
            }
        ]
    )
    print("Insights enabled successfully")

# Run examples
if __name__ == '__main__':
    print("=== Recent Events ===")
    get_recent_events()
    
    print("\n=== Bucket Deletions ===")
    find_bucket_deletions()
    
    print("\n=== Trail Config ===")
    get_trail_config('anihpj-audit-trail')
🏆 Implementation Best Practices
💾 Data Management
  • Enable S3 lifecycle policies for old logs
  • Archive logs to Glacier after 90 days
  • Set up S3 Intelligent-Tiering
  • Monitor S3 storage costs
🔍 Query Strategy
  • Use Athena for log analysis
  • Partition logs by date/region
  • Create saved queries for common searches
  • Use CloudWatch Insights for real-time
🚨 Alerting
  • Set metric filters for critical events
  • Create CloudWatch alarms
  • Send alerts to SNS/Slack
  • Document alert response procedures
✅ CloudTrail Setup Checklist for anihpj: 
☐ Create multi-region trail
☐ Enable log file validation
☐ Configure S3 bucket encryption (KMS)
☐ Set S3 lifecycle policies
☐ Enable CloudWatch Logs integration
☐ Create CloudWatch alarms
☐ Set up SNS notifications
☐ Configure data event logging for S3
☐ Enable CloudTrail Insights
☐ Test log delivery (create test events)
☐ Document access procedures
☐ Set up regular log reviews
☐ Configure automated remediation
☐ Train team on CloudTrail logs usage

🏆 Best Practices

🔒 Security Best Practices
✅ DO
  • Enable CloudTrail in all regions
  • Use log file validation
  • Encrypt logs with KMS
  • Store logs in dedicated S3 bucket
  • Enable MFA Delete on log bucket
  • Set up CloudWatch alarms
  • Regularly review logs
❌ DON'T
  • Disable CloudTrail in production
  • Store logs in public S3 bucket
  • Use same bucket for logs and data
  • Ignore unauthorized access alerts
  • Delete logs before retention period
  • Grant wide S3 bucket permissions
  • Forget to test log delivery
⚙️ Operational Best Practices
  • 📊 Monitor Costs: Track S3 storage and data event costs monthly
  • 🗑️ Lifecycle Policies: Move old logs to Glacier after 90 days, delete after 7 years
  • 🔍 Regular Audits: Review logs weekly for security incidents
  • 📦 Backup Logs: Copy critical logs to separate account for disaster recovery
  • 📝 Documentation: Document trail configuration and alert procedures
  • 🛡️ Test Recovery: Verify you can query and analyze logs when needed
💰 Cost Management Best Practices
💡 Smart Cost Strategies for anihpj
1️⃣ Use FREE First Trail
  • ✅ Create ONE multi-region trail (FREE management events)
  • ✅ Covers all regions automatically
  • ✅ Includes 90-day Event History (no S3 needed for basic queries)
2️⃣ Selective Data Events
  • ✅ Only enable data events for critical buckets (anihpj-production-uploads)
  • ❌ Don't enable for dev/test buckets
  • ✅ Use S3 prefix filters: s3://anihpj-uploads/resumes/* only
  • 💡 Cost: $0.10 per 100K events vs $5+ for ALL bucket events
3️⃣ S3 Storage Optimization
  • ✅ Lifecycle: Standard (30 days) → Glacier (90 days) → Deep Archive (1 year)
  • ✅ Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering for automatic cost optimization
  • ✅ Compress logs before archiving (CloudTrail gzip already helps)
  • 💡 Savings: 75% reduction after 90 days
4️⃣ CloudWatch Logs Retention
  • ✅ Set retention to 30 days (not infinite)
  • ✅ Use CloudWatch Logs only for real-time alerts
  • ✅ Query S3 logs with Athena for historical analysis (cheaper)
  • 💡 Cost: $0.50/GB vs $1.00+/GB for indefinite retention
5️⃣ CloudTrail Insights
  • ⚠️ Costs $0.35 per 100K events analyzed
  • ✅ Enable only for production account
  • ✅ Use custom Athena queries for dev/test (FREE)
  • 💡 Alternative: Build custom anomaly detection with Python (see Security Analysis section)
6️⃣ Avoid Multiple Trails
  • ❌ Don't create separate trails per region (use multi-region)
  • ❌ Don't duplicate trails for different teams
  • ✅ Use ONE trail + filter logs in Athena by team/service
  • 💡 Cost: $0/month (first trail) vs $2/trail/month for additional trails
Scenario Poor Setup Optimized Setup 💰 Monthly Savings
Basic Logging Multiple trails ($2 each)
No lifecycle ($0.23/GB)
Indefinite CloudWatch		 ONE multi-region trail (FREE)
Lifecycle to Glacier
30-day CloudWatch		 $6-12/month
Data Events ALL S3 buckets logged
($0.10 per 100K × 50 buckets)		 Only critical 5 buckets
Use prefix filters		 $15-40/month
Insights Enabled on all accounts
($0.35 per 100K)		 Production only
Custom Athena for dev/test		 $10-25/month
Storage Keep all logs in S3 Standard
100 GB × $0.023		 Glacier after 90 days
100 GB × $0.004		 $1.90/month
TOTAL POTENTIAL SAVINGS: $33-79/month
💡 anihpj Recommended Setup ($2-3/month):
  • ✅ ONE multi-region trail (FREE)
  • ✅ S3 storage with lifecycle to Glacier: $0.23/month
  • ✅ Data events for anihpj-uploads bucket only: $0.50/month
  • ✅ CloudWatch Logs (30-day retention, 2 GB): $1.06/month
  • ✅ CloudTrail Insights (production only): $0.35/month
  • ✅ TOTAL: $2.14/month 🎉

🔧 Troubleshooting

❌ Common Issues & Solutions
🚨 Issue: Trail Not Logging

Symptoms: No new log files in S3 bucket

Solutions:

  • ✅ Check trail is started: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status
  • ✅ Verify S3 bucket policy allows CloudTrail to write
  • ✅ Ensure trail is enabled for correct regions
  • ✅ Check IAM role has proper permissions
🚨 Issue: CloudWatch Logs Not Receiving Events

Symptoms: Events appear in S3 but not CloudWatch Logs

Solutions:

  • ✅ Verify CloudWatch Logs role has trust relationship with CloudTrail
  • ✅ Check log group exists and isn't full
  • ✅ Ensure trail has CloudWatch Logs enabled
  • ✅ Review IAM permissions for logs:PutLogEvents
🚨 Issue: Missing Events

Symptoms: Can't find expected events in logs

Solutions:

  • ✅ CloudTrail has ~15 minute delay - wait and check again
  • ✅ Verify event occurred in region covered by trail
  • ✅ Check if event is management event (logged) vs data event (may need configuration)
  • ✅ Some AWS services don't support CloudTrail logging
🚨 Issue: High Costs

Symptoms: CloudTrail bill higher than expected

Solutions:

  • ✅ Review data event configuration - disable unnecessary S3/Lambda logging
  • ✅ Implement S3 lifecycle policies to move logs to Glacier
  • ✅ Disable Insights if not actively using it
  • ✅ Use single multi-region trail instead of multiple trails
🐛 Debugging CloudTrail Issues
🔍 Debug Checklist for anihpj
1️⃣ Verify Trail Status 
# Check if trail is logging
aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name anihpj-audit-trail

# Expected output:
{
    "IsLogging": true,
    "LatestDeliveryTime": 1642001234.0,
    "LatestDeliveryAttemptTime": "2026-01-12T10:30:00Z",
    "LatestNotificationAttemptTime": "",
    "LatestNotificationAttemptSucceeded": "",
    "LatestDeliveryAttemptSucceeded": "2026-01-12T10:30:00Z",
    "TimeLoggingStarted": "2025-12-01T00:00:00Z",
    "TimeLoggingStopped": ""
}

🚨 If IsLogging = false: aws cloudtrail start-logging --name anihpj-audit-trail

2️⃣ Test S3 Bucket Permissions 
# Check bucket policy
aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket anihpj-cloudtrail-logs

# Validate CloudTrail can write
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --event-selectors '[{"ReadWriteType": "All","IncludeManagementEvents": true}]'

# Test log delivery (creates test file)
aws cloudtrail lookup-events --max-results 1

Expected: New log file in S3 within 15 minutes

3️⃣ Check CloudWatch Logs Integration 
# Verify log group exists
aws logs describe-log-groups --log-group-name-prefix "/aws/cloudtrail/anihpj"

# Check IAM role trust policy
aws iam get-role --role-name CloudTrailToCloudWatchLogs

# Test log delivery
aws logs tail /aws/cloudtrail/anihpj --follow
4️⃣ Debug Missing Events 
# Search recent events (90-day history)
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=CreateBucket \
  --max-results 50

# Check specific time range
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --start-time 2026-01-12T00:00:00Z \
  --end-time 2026-01-12T23:59:59Z

# Filter by user
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=django-api-user
5️⃣ Validate Data Events Configuration 
# Check current event selectors
aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail

# Expected output for S3 data events:
{
    "EventSelectors": [{
        "ReadWriteType": "All",
        "IncludeManagementEvents": true,
        "DataResources": [{
            "Type": "AWS::S3::Object",
            "Values": ["arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-uploads/*"]
        }]
    }]
}
6️⃣ Python Debug Script for anihpj 
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def debug_cloudtrail():
    """Comprehensive CloudTrail debugging for anihpj"""
    client = boto3.client('cloudtrail')
    
    # 1. Check trail status
    trails = client.describe_trails()['trailList']
    print("🔍 Found Trails:", len(trails))
    
    for trail in trails:
        status = client.get_trail_status(Name=trail['TrailARN'])
        print(f"\n📊 Trail: {trail['Name']}")
        print(f"   Logging: {status['IsLogging']}")
        print(f"   Last Delivery: {status.get('LatestDeliveryTime', 'Never')}")
        print(f"   S3 Bucket: {trail.get('S3BucketName', 'Not configured')}")
        print(f"   CloudWatch Logs: {trail.get('CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn', 'Not configured')}")
    
    # 2. Test event lookup
    print("\n🔎 Testing Event Lookup (last 1 hour)...")
    try:
        events = client.lookup_events(
            StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1),
            MaxResults=10
        )
        print(f"   ✅ Found {len(events['Events'])} events")
        
        if events['Events']:
            latest = events['Events'][0]
            print(f"   Latest Event: {latest['EventName']}")
            print(f"   Time: {latest['EventTime']}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"   ❌ Error: {e}")
    
    # 3. Check S3 log files
    print("\n📦 Checking S3 Logs...")
    for trail in trails:
        bucket = trail.get('S3BucketName')
        if bucket:
            s3 = boto3.client('s3')
            try:
                response = s3.list_objects_v2(
                    Bucket=bucket,
                    Prefix='AWSLogs/',
                    MaxKeys=5
                )
                print(f"   ✅ Found {response.get('KeyCount', 0)} log files in {bucket}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"   ❌ Cannot access {bucket}: {e}")

# Run debug
debug_cloudtrail()
7️⃣ Common Debug Commands 
# Enable verbose CloudTrail logging
aws cloudtrail update-trail --name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --include-global-service-events \
  --is-multi-region-trail

# Force log file creation (trigger API call)
aws s3 ls s3://anihpj-uploads/

# Check last 10 API calls YOU made
aws cloudtrail lookup-events --max-results 10

# Find all AccessDenied errors (last 7 days)
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=AccessDenied \
  --max-results 50
💡 Pro Debugging Tip: CloudTrail has ~15 minute delay. If you don't see events immediately, wait 15-20 minutes before assuming something is broken. Use aws cloudtrail lookup-events to check 90-day Event History instantly (no S3 delay).

You've learned how to implement comprehensive audit logging for your anihpj Django infrastructure! Here's what makes CloudTrail essential:

  • 💰 FREE Service: First trail completely free, management events at no cost
  • ✅ Complete Audit Trail: Track every API call made in your AWS account
  • 🔒 Security & Compliance: Meet GDPR, PCI DSS, HIPAA requirements
  • 🔍 Forensics: Investigate security incidents with detailed event history
  • 🚨 Real-time Alerts: CloudWatch integration for immediate notifications
  • 📊 Advanced Analysis: Query logs with Athena SQL
  • 🤖 Machine Learning: CloudTrail Insights detects unusual activity
  • 🌍 Multi-Region: Single trail covers all AWS regions

💰 Pricing & Cost Optimization

Understand CloudTrail pricing and optimize costs while maintaining comprehensive audit coverage for your anihpj Django job portal.

💸 CloudTrail Pricing Models
🆓 FREE Tier
$0

First trail with management events

  • One trail per region
  • Management events only
  • 90-day event history
  • Perfect for basic auditing
📊 Additional Trails
$2.00

Per 100,000 management events

  • Additional audit trails
  • Multi-region support
  • Custom event selection
  • Enhanced compliance
📁 Data Events
$0.10

Per 100,000 data events

  • S3 object operations
  • Lambda function calls
  • High-volume logging
  • Detailed activity tracking
🔍 Insights
$0.35

Per 100,000 write events analyzed

  • Anomaly detection
  • ML-powered analysis
  • Automatic alerts
  • Security intelligence
🧮 anihpj Cost Scenarios
💼 Production Environment
Application: anihpj Django Job Portal
Team Size: 5 developers, 2 DevOps engineers
Monthly API Calls: ~500K management events
Data Events: S3 uploads, Lambda executions
Monthly CloudTrail Costs
First Trail (Management Events) $0.00
Additional Management Events (400K) $8.00
S3 Data Events (1M events) $10.00
CloudTrail Insights (100K analyzed) $3.50
S3 Storage (50GB logs) $1.15
Total Monthly Cost $22.65
🚀 Startup Environment
Application: anihpj MVP
Team Size: 2 developers
Monthly API Calls: ~50K management events
Data Events: Limited S3 usage
Monthly CloudTrail Costs
First Trail (Management Events) $0.00
S3 Data Events (100K events) $1.00
S3 Storage (5GB logs) $0.12
CloudWatch Log Processing $2.50
Total Monthly Cost $3.62

🔒 Security & Best Practices

Implement comprehensive security measures and best practices for CloudTrail in your anihpj Django job portal environment.

✅ CloudTrail Security Checklist
🛤️ Trail Configuration
🔐 Access Control
🎯 Production Ready! You now have comprehensive CloudTrail documentation following our proven template. Your anihpj Django job portal has enterprise-grade audit logging, security monitoring, and compliance capabilities with complete cost optimization and security best practices.

📖 CloudTrail & Audit Logging Glossary

Essential terms for understanding AWS CloudTrail and audit logging.

🛤️ Trail
A configuration that enables delivery of CloudTrail events to an S3 bucket, CloudWatch Logs, and/or EventBridge. You can have multiple trails in an account to meet different requirements. Example: anihpj-prod-trail logs all production API calls.
📝 Event
A record of an API call or activity in your AWS account. Each event contains details like who made the call, when, from which IP address, what action was performed, and the result. Events are stored in JSON format.
🔐 Management Events
Control plane operations performed on AWS resources (e.g., creating EC2 instances, modifying security groups, creating S3 buckets). First trail is FREE for management events. Essential for tracking who changed what in your AWS account.
💾 Data Events
Data plane operations on AWS resources (e.g., S3 object GET/PUT, Lambda function invocations, DynamoDB item reads/writes). These generate high-volume logs and cost $0.10 per 100,000 events. Use selectively for sensitive data.
📊 Insights Events
Machine learning-powered anomaly detection that identifies unusual API activity patterns. Example: Suddenly 10x more EC2 terminations than usual might indicate a compromised account. Costs $0.35 per 100,000 write management events analyzed.
📁 Event History
A 90-day viewable, downloadable, and searchable record of management events in the CloudTrail console. Available for FREE without creating a trail. Perfect for quick investigations: "Who deleted my anihpj RDS database?"
🌍 Organization Trail
A single trail that logs events for all AWS accounts in an AWS Organization. Centralized logging for enterprise compliance. Master account pays for the trail, and logs from all member accounts are aggregated.
🗄️ Log File
A JSON-formatted file containing CloudTrail events, delivered to S3 every 5 minutes (or when reaching 1000 events). Files are immutable and can be cryptographically validated to ensure integrity.
✅ Log File Validation
A feature that creates a digital signature (SHA-256 hash) for every log file, allowing you to verify that logs haven't been modified, deleted, or forged after CloudTrail delivered them. Essential for compliance and forensics.
🔑 Event Selector
Filters that determine which data events are logged. Example: Log only S3 PutObject events for s3://anihpj-uploads/* instead of all S3 activity. Reduces costs and noise.
🔍 Lookup Attribute
Search criteria for querying CloudTrail events in Event History. Can search by event name, username, resource name, event source, event ID, or resource type. Example: Find all TerminateInstances events by user alice@anihpj.com.
📊 Amazon Athena
A serverless query service that lets you analyze CloudTrail logs stored in S3 using SQL. Perfect for complex queries: "Show all failed login attempts from IP addresses outside USA in the last 30 days". Pay only per query ($5 per TB scanned).
🚨 CloudWatch Logs Integration
Send CloudTrail events to CloudWatch Logs for real-time monitoring and alerting. Create metric filters to count specific events (e.g., unauthorized API calls) and trigger SNS notifications or Lambda functions.
🔔 Amazon SNS Integration
Send notifications when new CloudTrail log files are delivered to S3. Use for real-time processing: Lambda function triggered by SNS analyzes logs immediately for security threats.
🛡️ AWS GuardDuty
Managed threat detection service that analyzes CloudTrail events for malicious activity (e.g., compromised credentials, cryptocurrency mining, data exfiltration). Complementary to CloudTrail - GuardDuty consumes CloudTrail data.
🔐 AWS Config
Tracks AWS resource configuration changes. While CloudTrail logs API calls (who did what), Config tracks resulting state (what changed). Use together: CloudTrail shows ModifySecurityGroup API call, Config shows security group rule before/after.
🔑 KMS Encryption
AWS Key Management Service encrypts CloudTrail log files at rest in S3 using server-side encryption (SSE-KMS). Provides additional access control: only users with KMS key permissions can read logs.
👤 User Identity
Information about the principal (IAM user, role, federated user, or AWS service) that made the API call. Includes ARN, account ID, access key used, and session context. Critical for accountability.
🌐 Source IP Address
The IP address from which the API call originated. For AWS service calls (e.g., Auto Scaling), shows the service's IP. Use for geolocation analysis and detecting unusual access patterns.
📅 Event Time
UTC timestamp when the API call was made. CloudTrail events are near-real-time but can have a few minutes delay. Event time differs from log delivery time.
🔄 Multi-Region Trail
A trail that logs events from all AWS regions to a single S3 bucket. Automatically includes new regions as they launch. Best practice: Always use multi-region trails for complete visibility.
🌐 Global Service Events
Events from AWS services that operate globally (IAM, Route 53, CloudFront, STS). These events are always logged in us-east-1 region regardless of where trail is created. Enable "Include global service events" to capture them.
💰 Event Count
The number of events logged. Management events are FREE for first trail. Data events cost $0.10 per 100,000 events. Insights events cost $0.35 per 100,000 write events analyzed. Monitor usage to control costs.
🗂️ S3 Bucket Policy
IAM policy attached to S3 bucket that grants CloudTrail permission to write log files. Must allow s3:PutObject from CloudTrail service principal. CloudTrail cannot log if bucket policy is misconfigured.
🔍 CloudTrail Lake
A managed data lake that aggregates, immutably stores, and enables SQL-based querying of CloudTrail events. Retains events for up to 7 years. Alternative to S3 + Athena with simpler pricing ($2.50 per GB ingested).
💡 Quick Reference: Bookmark this glossary for easy reference when working with CloudTrail. These 25 terms cover 95% of CloudTrail operations you'll encounter with anihpj auditing and compliance.

🚀 Get Started with CloudTrail for anihpj
  1. Create a multi-region trail for comprehensive logging
  2. Enable log file validation for integrity verification
  3. Configure S3 bucket with encryption and lifecycle policies
  4. Set up CloudWatch Logs integration for real-time monitoring
  5. Create metric filters and alarms for security events
  6. Enable CloudTrail Insights for anomaly detection
  7. Configure Athena for SQL-based log analysis
  8. Integrate with GuardDuty for threat detection
  9. Set up SNS notifications for critical events
  10. Document incident response procedures using CloudTrail
💡 Pro Tip

CloudTrail's first trail with management events is FREE, so there's no excuse not to enable it! Start with a simple multi-region trail, then add CloudWatch Logs integration and Insights as your anihpj security posture matures. Remember: you can't investigate what you didn't log!

🔐 AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management)

Category: Security & Identity  |  Use Case: Access Control & Authentication  |  Pricing: Free (no charge for IAM)  |  Last Updated: January 2026  |  Documentation: AWS IAM Docs

Securely control access to AWS services and resources for your anihpj Django job portal with granular permissions, role-based access control, and comprehensive identity management.

📋 Overview & Introduction

AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is a web service that helps you securely control access to AWS resources. You use IAM to control who is authenticated (signed in) and authorized (has permissions) to use resources in your AWS account. It's the foundation of security in AWS, protecting your anihpj Django job portal infrastructure.

🚀 Quick Start Guide (10 Minutes):
  1. Secure root account: Enable MFA, delete access keys
  2. Create admin user: alice.admin with MFA enabled
  3. Create groups: Developers, DevOps, ReadOnly
  4. Create custom policies: anihpj-s3-uploads, anihpj-rds-connect
  5. Create EC2 role: anihpj-ec2-role with S3 + RDS access
  6. Attach role to instances: No more hardcoded credentials!
  7. Enable CloudTrail: Log all IAM actions for audit
  8. Test permissions: Use Policy Simulator before deploy
🎯 IAM at a Glance
💰 Cost
FREE
No charge for IAM
Unlimited users/roles
🔐 Security
MFA
Multi-factor auth
Hardware keys
⚡ Integration
All AWS
Every service
Native support
🎯 Granular
JSON
Policy-based
Fine-grained
📊 Audit
CloudTrail
Every action
Complete logs
🛡️ Compliance
SOC/HIPAA
PCI DSS ready
Certified
🎯 Key Benefits for anihpj
🔒
Granular Permissions

Control exactly who can access EC2, RDS, S3 resources for your job portal

👥
Multi-User Management

Create separate users for developers, DevOps, and automated services

🎭
Role-Based Access

EC2 instances assume roles to access RDS, S3 without hardcoded credentials

🔐
MFA Protection

Multi-factor authentication for sensitive operations and console access

📜
Compliance Ready

Meet regulatory requirements (SOC, HIPAA, PCI DSS) with audit trails

💰
Free Service

No charge for IAM usage - only pay for resources accessed

💡 When to Use IAM for anihpj:

IAM is ESSENTIAL for every AWS account! Use it to create separate users for each team member (no shared root account!), assign EC2 instance roles for accessing RDS and S3, enforce MFA for production access, and implement least-privilege security for your Django job portal. Never embed AWS credentials in your code!

⚖️ IAM vs. Other AWS Security Services
Service Purpose Use Case for anihpj
IAM Authentication & authorization Control who can deploy, access EC2, RDS
Cognito User authentication for apps Job seeker/employer login (Django handles this)
Organizations Multi-account management Separate dev/staging/prod accounts
Secrets Manager Store secrets securely RDS passwords, API keys for Django
KMS Encryption key management Encrypt EBS, RDS, S3 data
CloudTrail Audit logging Track who did what in AWS (uses IAM for identity)
🏗️ IAM in anihpj AWS Ecosystem - Architecture Overview
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          AWS IAM ARCHITECTURE FOR ANIHPJ                               │
│                                                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                              AWS ACCOUNT (Root)                                   │ │
│  │                         anihpj-production (123456789012)                          │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  ⚠️  ROOT USER (admin@anihpj.com)                                                 │ │
│  │      - MFA ENABLED                                                                │ │
│  │      - Only for billing & account closure                                         │ │
│  │      - Never used for daily operations                                            │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                         │                                              │
│                                         │ Creates IAM Users, Groups, Roles             │
│                                         ▼                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                           IAM USERS (Human Access)                                │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  👤 john.dev@anihpj.com              👤 sarah.devops@anihpj.com                  │ │
│  │     - Developer                          - DevOps Engineer                        │ │
│  │     - MFA: Enabled                       - MFA: Enabled                           │ │
│  │     - Groups: Developers                 - Groups: DevOps                         │ │
│  │     - Access: Console + CLI              - Access: Console + CLI                  │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  👤 alice.admin@anihpj.com           👤 bob.readonly@anihpj.com                  │ │
│  │     - Administrator                      - Auditor                                │ │
│  │     - MFA: Enabled                       - MFA: Enabled                           │ │
│  │     - Groups: Admins                     - Groups: ReadOnly                       │ │
│  │     - Access: Full (with conditions)     - Access: Read-only                      │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                         │                                              │
│                                         │ Members of                                   │
│                                         ▼                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                           IAM GROUPS (Permission Sets)                            │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  👥 Developers Group                    👥 DevOps Group                          │ │
│  │     Policies:                               Policies:                             │ │
│  │     ✅ EC2 Full Access                      ✅ Administrator Access               │ │
│  │     ✅ RDS Connect                          ✅ EC2, RDS, S3 Full                  │ │
│  │     ✅ S3 Read/Write (anihpj-uploads)       ✅ CloudWatch, CloudTrail             │ │
│  │     ❌ Delete Production Resources          ✅ IAM (limited)                      │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  👥 Admins Group                        👥 ReadOnly Group                        │ │
│  │     Policies:                               Policies:                             │ │
│  │     ✅ Administrator Access                 ✅ ReadOnly Access                     │ │
│  │     ⚠️  Requires MFA for sensitive ops      ✅ View all resources                 │ │
│  │                                             ❌ No modifications                    │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                       IAM ROLES (Service & Cross-Account Access)                  │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  🎭 anihpj-ec2-role                     🎭 anihpj-lambda-role                   │ │
│  │     Attached to: EC2 instances              Attached to: Lambda functions         │ │
│  │     Trust: EC2 service                      Trust: Lambda service                 │ │
│  │     Permissions:                            Permissions:                          │ │
│  │     ✅ RDS Connect                           ✅ RDS Query                          │ │
│  │     ✅ S3 Read/Write (uploads bucket)        ✅ S3 Read (config bucket)            │ │
│  │     ✅ CloudWatch Logs Write                 ✅ CloudWatch Logs Write              │ │
│  │     ✅ Parameter Store Read                  ✅ SES Send Email                     │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  🎭 anihpj-codebuild-role               🎭 anihpj-ecs-task-role                 │ │
│  │     Attached to: CodeBuild projects         Attached to: ECS tasks                │ │
│  │     Trust: CodeBuild service                Trust: ECS tasks service              │ │
│  │     Permissions:                            Permissions:                          │ │
│  │     ✅ ECR Push/Pull                         ✅ ECR Pull                           │ │
│  │     ✅ S3 Build Artifacts                    ✅ RDS Connect                        │ │
│  │     ✅ CloudWatch Logs                       ✅ S3 Read/Write                      │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  🎭 anihpj-cross-account-role (Dev → Prod)                                       │ │
│  │     Trust: Dev account (987654321098)                                            │ │
│  │     Permissions: Deploy to production (with approval)                            │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                          IAM POLICIES (JSON Documents)                            │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  📜 Managed Policies (AWS + Custom)                                               │ │
│  │     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐              │ │
│  │     │ AmazonEC2FullAccess (AWS Managed)                           │              │ │
│  │     │ AmazonRDSReadOnlyAccess (AWS Managed)                       │              │ │
│  │     │ anihpj-s3-uploads-access (Customer Managed)                 │              │ │
│  │     │ anihpj-developer-permissions (Customer Managed)             │              │ │
│  │     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘              │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  📜 Inline Policies (Attached directly to user/role/group)                        │ │
│  │     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐              │ │
│  │     │ Special permissions for specific users                      │              │ │
│  │     │ Example: john.dev can only terminate his own EC2 instances  │              │ │
│  │     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘              │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                         REAL-WORLD FLOW: Deploy Django App                        │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  1️⃣  Developer (john.dev) logs in with MFA                                       │ │
│  │      └─→ Console access via IAM user credentials                                 │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  2️⃣  John triggers CodePipeline deployment                                       │ │
│  │      └─→ CodeBuild assumes anihpj-codebuild-role                                 │ │
│  │          └─→ Pulls code from GitHub                                              │ │
│  │          └─→ Builds Docker image                                                 │ │
│  │          └─→ Pushes to ECR (has ECR permissions via role)                        │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  3️⃣  ECS pulls image and starts task                                             │ │
│  │      └─→ ECS task assumes anihpj-ecs-task-role                                   │ │
│  │          └─→ Connects to RDS (has RDS permissions)                               │ │
