🔄 How ACM Works: Certificate Lifecycle Flow

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      🔄 ACM CERTIFICATE LIFECYCLE FOR ANIHPJ                                        │
│                                                                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                                  CERTIFICATE REQUEST FLOW                                    │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                                     │
│  ┌───────────┐    ┌───────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌────────────┐     │
│  │  REQUEST  │───▶│   PENDING     │───▶│  VALIDATION    │───▶│   ISSUED    │───▶│  DEPLOYED  │     │
│  │Certificate│    │  VALIDATION   │    │   COMPLETE     │    │             │    │            │     │
│  └───────────┘    └───────────────┘    └────────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └────────────┘     │
│       │                  │                    │                    │                  │            │
│       │                  │                    │                    │                  │            │
│       ▼                  ▼                    ▼                    ▼                  ▼            │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │ User runs   │  │ AWS sends       │  │ User adds      │  │ ACM generates   │ │ User attaches  │  │
│  │ aws acm     │  │ validation      │  │ CNAME to       │  │ certificate     │ │ cert to ALB/   │  │
│  │ request-    │  │ requirements    │  │ Route 53       │  │ & private key   │ │ CloudFront     │  │
│  │ certificate │  │                 │  │                │  │                 │ │                │  │
│  └─────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘ └────────────────┘  │
│                                                                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                                  AUTOMATIC RENEWAL FLOW                                      │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                                     │
│    DAY 0              DAY ~305            DAY ~335           DAY ~365         DAY 366+              │
│    Certificate        ACM Starts          New Cert           Old Cert        Seamless              │
│    Issued             Renewal             Issued             Expires         Transition            │
│       │                  │                   │                  │               │                   │
│       ▼                  ▼                   ▼                  ▼               ▼                   │
│   ┌───────┐          ┌───────┐          ┌───────┐          ┌───────┐       ┌───────┐               │
│   │ VALID │──────────│RENEWING│──────────│RENEWED │──────────│ OLD   │───────│ NEW   │              │
│   │ 365d  │  ~60d    │       │  ~30d    │        │  ~30d   │EXPIRES│       │ CERT  │               │
│   └───────┘  before  └───────┘  before  └───────┘  before  └───────┘       │ ACTIVE│               │
│              expiry            expiry             expiry                    └───────┘               │
│                                                                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  ✅ NO USER ACTION REQUIRED FOR RENEWAL (if validation records still exist)                 │   │
│  │  ✅ Zero downtime - old and new certificates overlap                                        │   │
│  │  ✅ Automatic deployment to ALB/CloudFront                                                  │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                              DNS VALIDATION PROCESS (Recommended)                           │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                                     │
│   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │                                                                                            │   │
│   │    1. Request Certificate           2. Get CNAME Records         3. Add to Route 53       │   │
│   │    ┌──────────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐   │   │
│   │    │ aws acm request-     │   ─▶    │ _abc123.anihpj.com   │ ─▶  │ Route 53 Record    │   │   │
│   │    │ certificate          │         │ CNAME                │     │ Type: CNAME        │   │   │
│   │    │ --domain-name        │         │ _xyz789.acm-valid... │     │ Name: _abc123...   │   │   │
│   │    │   anihpj.com         │         │                      │     │ Value: _xyz789...  │   │   │
│   │    │ --validation-method  │         └──────────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘   │   │
│   │    │   DNS                │                                                               │   │
│   │    └──────────────────────┘         4. Wait for Validation       5. Certificate Issued   │   │
│   │                                     ┌──────────────────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐   │   │
│   │                                     │ AWS validates CNAME  │ ─▶  │ Status: ISSUED ✅  │   │   │
│   │                                     │ (~2-30 minutes)      │     │ Valid: 13 months   │   │   │
│   │                                     │ Route 53: Instant!   │     │ Auto-Renew: YES    │   │   │
│   │                                     └──────────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘   │   │
│   │                                                                                            │   │
│   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                                     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🏗️ ACM in anihpj AWS Ecosystem - Architecture Overview
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       AWS CERTIFICATE MANAGER ARCHITECTURE FOR ANIHPJ                   │
│                                                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                              AWS ACCOUNT (anihpj-production)                       │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │ │
│  │    │                    AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)                         │   │ │
│  │    │                                                                          │   │ │
│  │    │  📜 anihpj.com Certificate          📜 *.anihpj.com Wildcard            │   │ │
│  │    │  ├── Status: ISSUED                  ├── Status: ISSUED                  │   │ │
│  │    │  ├── Validation: DNS                 ├── Validation: DNS                 │   │ │
│  │    │  ├── Auto-Renewal: ✅                ├── Auto-Renewal: ✅                │   │ │
│  │    │  └── Cost: $0                        └── Cost: $0                        │   │ │
│  │    │                                                                          │   │ │
│  │    │  📜 api.anihpj.com (covered by wildcard)                                 │   │ │
│  │    │  📜 www.anihpj.com (covered by wildcard)                                 │   │ │
│  │    │  📜 admin.anihpj.com (covered by wildcard)                               │   │ │
│  │    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │ │
│  │                                         │                                          │ │
│  │                      Certificates deployed to:                                     │ │
│  │                                         │                                          │ │
│  │    ┌────────────────────┬───────────────┼───────────────┬────────────────────┐   │ │
│  │    ▼                    ▼               ▼               ▼                    ▼   │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │  ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐   │ │
│  │  │   ALB    │    │CloudFront│    │API Gateway│   │ Elastic  │    │   NLB    │   │ │
│  │  │  HTTPS   │    │   CDN    │    │   REST   │    │Beanstalk │    │   TLS    │   │ │
│  │  │ Listener │    │  SSL/TLS │    │  Custom  │    │  HTTPS   │    │ Listener │   │ │
│  │  │          │    │          │    │  Domain  │    │          │    │          │   │ │
│  │  └────┬─────┘    └────┬─────┘    └────┬─────┘    └────┬─────┘    └────┬─────┘   │ │
│  │       │               │               │               │               │          │ │
│  │       │               │               │               │               │          │ │
│  │       ▼               ▼               ▼               ▼               ▼          │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │ │
│  │  │                        Backend Services (HTTP Internal)                   │   │ │
│  │  │                                                                          │   │ │
│  │  │    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐                │   │ │
│  │  │    │   Django    │    │    S3       │    │   Lambda    │                │   │ │
│  │  │    │   EC2/ECS   │    │   Static    │    │  Functions  │                │   │ │
│  │  │    │  Port 8000  │    │   Assets    │    │             │                │   │ │
│  │  │    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘                │   │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │ │
│  │    │                         Route 53 DNS                                     │   │ │
│  │    │   _abc123.anihpj.com → _xyz.acm-validations.aws (DNS Validation CNAME)  │   │ │
│  │    │   anihpj.com → ALB DNS Name                                             │   │ │
│  │    │   api.anihpj.com → API Gateway Domain                                   │   │ │
│  │    │   static.anihpj.com → CloudFront Distribution                           │   │ │
│  │    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                        │
│  ⚡ Key Points:                                                                        │
│  • SSL termination happens at edge (ALB/CloudFront) - not at EC2                      │
│  • Internal traffic between ALB→EC2 can be HTTP (encrypted via VPC)                   │
│  • Certificates auto-renew 60 days before expiry                                       │
│  • No certificate files to manage - ACM handles everything                             │
│  • CloudFront requires certificates in us-east-1 region                                │
│                                                                                        │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 Core Concepts

Understanding the fundamental concepts of SSL/TLS certificates and how ACM simplifies certificate management for your anihpj Django job portal.

🔄 How ACM Works (anihpj SSL/TLS Flow)

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        ACM CERTIFICATE LIFECYCLE FOR ANIHPJ                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                          │
│  CERTIFICATE REQUEST FLOW:                                                               │
│  ─────────────────────────                                                               │
│                                                                                          │
│  ┌──────────┐    ┌───────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────────┐   │
│  │   You    │───►│   Request     │───►│   Domain     │───►│   Certificate          │   │
│  │ (Admin)  │    │  Certificate  │    │  Validation  │    │   Issued!              │   │
│  └──────────┘    └───────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └─────────────────────────┘   │
│       │                  │                   │                       │                   │
│       │ 1. Request for   │ 2. ACM creates    │ 3. Prove domain       │ 4. Certificate   │
│       │ anihpj.com &     │ CNAME record      │ ownership via DNS     │ ready to use     │
│       │ *.anihpj.com     │ for validation    │ or Email              │                  │
│       └──────────────────┴───────────────────┴───────────────────────┘                   │
│                                                                                          │
│  HTTPS REQUEST FLOW (After Certificate Deployment):                                      │
│  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────                                      │
│                                                                                          │
│  ┌──────────┐    ┌───────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────────┐   │
│  │   User   │───►│   Browser     │───►│   ALB/       │───►│   EC2 / Django         │   │
│  │ (Browser)│    │  HTTPS Req    │    │  CloudFront  │    │   (HTTP internally)    │   │
│  └──────────┘    └───────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └─────────────────────────┘   │
│       │                  │                   │                       │                   │
│       │                  │                   │                       │                   │
│       │ 1. User visits   │ 2. TLS Handshake  │ 3. SSL Termination   │ 4. Unencrypted   │
│       │ https://anihpj   │ ACM certificate   │ at edge (ALB)        │ to Django app    │
│       │ .com             │ presented         │                      │ (port 8000)      │
│       └──────────────────┴───────────────────┴──────────────────────┴───────────────────┘
│                                                                                          │
│  AUTO-RENEWAL FLOW (60 Days Before Expiry):                                              │
│  ──────────────────────────────────────────                                              │
│                                                                                          │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌───────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐   │
│  │  ACM Checks  │───►│  DNS Record   │───►│  New Cert    │───►│  Auto-Deployed     │   │
│  │  Expiry Date │    │  Still Valid  │    │  Generated   │    │  Zero Downtime     │   │
│  └──────────────┘    └───────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └─────────────────────┘   │
│       │                                                                                  │
│       └─── No action required! ACM handles everything automatically                     │
│                                                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔐 Public Certificates

Public certificates are issued by Amazon Trust Services CA and trusted by all major browsers. They're FREE and perfect for public-facing websites like your anihpj job portal.

📊 Public Certificate Structure
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              PUBLIC CERTIFICATE FOR ANIHPJ                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                           │
│  Certificate Details:                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Subject: CN=anihpj.com                             │ │
│  │  Issuer:  Amazon Root CA 1                          │ │
│  │                                                     │ │
│  │  Subject Alternative Names (SANs):                  │ │
│  │  ├── anihpj.com                                     │ │
│  │  ├── www.anihpj.com                                │ │
│  │  ├── api.anihpj.com                                │ │
│  │  ├── admin.anihpj.com                              │ │
│  │  └── *.anihpj.com (wildcard)                       │ │
│  │                                                     │ │
│  │  Validity:                                          │ │
│  │  ├── Not Before: 2025-01-15 00:00:00 UTC          │ │
│  │  └── Not After:  2026-02-14 23:59:59 UTC (13 mo)  │ │
│  │                                                     │ │
│  │  Key Algorithm: RSA 2048-bit                       │ │
│  │  Signature:     SHA-256 with RSA                   │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                           │
│  Trust Chain:                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  1. Root CA:     Amazon Root CA 1 (Self-signed)    │ │
│  │       ↓                                             │ │
│  │  2. Intermediate: Amazon RSA 2048 M01              │ │
│  │       ↓                                             │ │
│  │  3. End Entity:   anihpj.com (Your certificate)    │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🏢 Private Certificates (ACM Private CA)

Private certificates are for internal resources not exposed to the internet. Useful for internal microservices, private APIs, and IoT devices. Note: Private CA has costs ($400/month).

✅ Public Certificates (FREE)
  • Public-facing websites
  • ALB, CloudFront, API Gateway
  • Trusted by all browsers
  • Auto-renewal included
  • Perfect for anihpj!
⚠️ Private Certificates ($400/mo)
  • Internal microservices
  • Private APIs
  • IoT device authentication
  • Custom trust hierarchies
  • Enterprise use cases
🔄 Certificate Lifecycle

ACM manages the entire certificate lifecycle automatically, from issuance to renewal.

📊 Certificate States
State Description Action Required
⏳ Pending Validation Waiting for domain ownership proof Add CNAME record to DNS
✅ Issued Certificate ready to use Deploy to ALB/CloudFront
🔄 Pending Auto-Renewal ACM attempting renewal (60 days before) None - automatic
❌ Validation Timed Out 72 hours passed without validation Request new certificate
⚠️ Revoked Certificate manually revoked Request new certificate
⏰ Expired Certificate past validity date Request new certificate
✅ Domain Validation Methods

ACM offers two validation methods to prove you own the domain. DNS validation is recommended for automatic renewal.

🌐 DNS Validation (Recommended)

Add a CNAME record to your DNS configuration. ACM uses this record to verify ownership and for automatic renewal.

Pros:
  • ✅ Enables automatic renewal
  • ✅ No email required
  • ✅ Works with wildcard certificates
  • ✅ One-time setup
📧 Email Validation

ACM sends email to domain admin addresses (admin@, webmaster@, etc.) for manual approval.

Cons:
  • ❌ Manual renewal required
  • ❌ Requires working email
  • ❌ Can miss renewal emails
  • ❌ Higher maintenance
🔒 SSL/TLS Basics

Understanding the fundamentals of SSL/TLS encryption for securing web traffic.

🔐 What is SSL/TLS?
  • SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) - Deprecated protocol
  • TLS (Transport Layer Security) - Modern standard (1.2, 1.3)
  • Encrypts data between browser and server
  • Enables HTTPS (HTTP over TLS)
📋 TLS Handshake Process
  1. Client sends "Hello" with supported ciphers
  2. Server responds with certificate
  3. Client verifies certificate with CA
  4. Session keys exchanged
  5. Encrypted communication begins
🎯 Why HTTPS Matters
  • 🔒 Encrypts sensitive data (passwords, forms)
  • 📈 SEO ranking boost from Google
  • 🛡️ Prevents man-in-the-middle attacks
  • ✅ Required for modern browser features

🚀 Getting Started

Follow these step-by-step guides to set up SSL/TLS certificates for your anihpj Django job portal.

📝 Step 1: Request Certificate (AWS Console)
# Request certificate via AWS CLI:

# 1. Request public certificate for anihpj.com with wildcard
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" "www.anihpj.com" \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1 \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

# Response:
# {
#     "CertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-def456-..."
# }

# 2. Describe certificate to get validation records
aws acm describe-certificate \
    --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-... \
    --query 'Certificate.DomainValidationOptions'

# 3. List all certificates
aws acm list-certificates \
    --region us-east-1 \
    --query 'CertificateSummaryList[*].[DomainName,CertificateArn,Status]' \
    --output table
🌐 Step 2: DNS Validation (Route 53)
📋 DNS Validation Record Format:

ACM provides a CNAME record you must add to Route 53 (or your DNS provider):

# ACM provides this CNAME record:
# Name:  _abc123.anihpj.com
# Value: _xyz789.acm-validations.aws.

# Add to Route 53 via CLI:
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [{
            "Action": "CREATE",
            "ResourceRecordSet": {
                "Name": "_abc123.anihpj.com",
                "Type": "CNAME",
                "TTL": 300,
                "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "_xyz789.acm-validations.aws."}]
            }
        }]
    }'

# Or use ACM's Route 53 integration (one-click in console):
# Console → ACM → Certificate → "Create record in Route 53"

# Verify DNS propagation:
dig _abc123.anihpj.com CNAME
nslookup _abc123.anihpj.com
⏱️ Validation Time:

DNS validation typically completes in 5-30 minutes. ACM checks periodically until the CNAME is found.

📧 Step 2 (Alternative): Email Validation
# Request with email validation:
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" \
    --validation-method EMAIL \
    --region us-east-1

# ACM sends approval emails to:
# - admin@anihpj.com
# - administrator@anihpj.com
# - webmaster@anihpj.com
# - postmaster@anihpj.com
# - hostmaster@anihpj.com

# Resend validation email if needed:
aws acm resend-validation-email \
    --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-... \
    --domain anihpj.com \
    --validation-domain anihpj.com
⚖️ Step 3: Attach to Application Load Balancer
# 1. Create HTTPS listener on ALB with ACM certificate:
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123 \
    --protocol HTTPS \
    --port 443 \
    --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-def456 \
    --default-actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:targetgroup/anihpj-tg/xyz789

# 2. Add HTTP to HTTPS redirect:
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123 \
    --protocol HTTP \
    --port 80 \
    --default-actions Type=redirect,RedirectConfig='{Protocol=HTTPS,Port=443,StatusCode=HTTP_301}'

# 3. Add additional certificates (for multiple domains):
aws elbv2 add-listener-certificates \
    --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc123/xyz789 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/another-cert-arn
🏗️ ALB + ACM Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ANIHPJ ALB + ACM HTTPS ARCHITECTURE                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│   Internet Users                                                             │
│        │                                                                     │
│        │  HTTPS (Port 443)                                                   │
│        ▼                                                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │                    Application Load Balancer                         │   │
│   │                                                                      │   │
│   │  ┌────────────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────────────────┐   │   │
│   │  │  HTTPS Listener    │    │  ACM Certificate                   │   │   │
│   │  │  Port 443          │◄───│  anihpj.com + *.anihpj.com         │   │   │
│   │  │                    │    │  Auto-renewal enabled              │   │   │
│   │  └────────────────────┘    └────────────────────────────────────┘   │   │
│   │                                                                      │   │
│   │  ┌────────────────────┐                                             │   │
│   │  │  HTTP Listener     │  ────► Redirect to HTTPS (301)             │   │
│   │  │  Port 80           │                                             │   │
│   │  └────────────────────┘                                             │   │
│   │                                                                      │   │
│   │  SSL Termination Here ──────────────────────────────────────────►   │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│        │                                                                     │
│        │  HTTP (Port 8000) - Unencrypted internal traffic                   │
│        ▼                                                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │  Target Group: anihpj-django-servers                                 │   │
│   │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐                  │   │
│   │  │ EC2 Django  │  │ EC2 Django  │  │ EC2 Django  │                  │   │
│   │  │ Port 8000   │  │ Port 8000   │  │ Port 8000   │                  │   │
│   │  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘                  │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
☁️ Step 4: CloudFront Integration
⚠️ Important:

CloudFront requires certificates in us-east-1 (N. Virginia) region only!

# 1. Request certificate in us-east-1 for CloudFront:
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name static.anihpj.com \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1  # MUST be us-east-1!

# 2. Update CloudFront distribution with ACM certificate:
aws cloudfront update-distribution \
    --id E1234567890ABC \
    --distribution-config '{
        "ViewerCertificate": {
            "ACMCertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123...",
            "SSLSupportMethod": "sni-only",
            "MinimumProtocolVersion": "TLSv1.2_2021"
        },
        "Aliases": {
            "Quantity": 2,
            "Items": ["static.anihpj.com", "cdn.anihpj.com"]
        }
    }'
🔌 Step 5: API Gateway Integration

Configure custom domain names for your API Gateway endpoints with ACM certificates.

# 1. Create custom domain name for API Gateway:
aws apigateway create-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --regional-certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123... \
    --endpoint-configuration types=REGIONAL \
    --security-policy TLS_1_2

# 2. Create base path mapping to connect API to custom domain:
aws apigateway create-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --stage prod \
    --base-path v1

# 3. Get target domain name for DNS:
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'regionalDomainName'
# Returns: d-abc123.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

# 4. Create Route 53 alias record:
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [{
            "Action": "CREATE",
            "ResourceRecordSet": {
                "Name": "api.anihpj.com",
                "Type": "A",
                "AliasTarget": {
                    "DNSName": "d-abc123.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com",
                    "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
                    "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
                }
            }
        }]
    }'

# Your API is now available at: https://api.anihpj.com/v1/
✅ Benefits of Custom Domain:
  • Professional API URLs: api.anihpj.com/v1/jobs
  • Easy API versioning with base paths
  • Single certificate for multiple API stages
  • Consistent branding across services

⭐ Key Features

Explore the powerful features that make ACM the preferred certificate management solution for AWS.

🔄 Automatic Certificate Renewal
🔄
Zero Downtime Renewal

ACM automatically renews DNS-validated certificates 60 days before expiration

✅ What ACM Does Automatically:
  • Monitors certificate expiration
  • Renews 60 days before expiry
  • Updates certificate on ALB/CloudFront
  • Validates domain ownership via DNS
⚠️ Renewal Requirements:
  • DNS validation CNAME must exist
  • Certificate must be in use
  • Domain must still be registered
  • Email validation = manual renewal
🌟 Wildcard Certificates

Secure unlimited subdomains with a single wildcard certificate:

# Request wildcard certificate for all anihpj.com subdomains:
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" \
    --validation-method DNS

# This single certificate covers:
# ✅ anihpj.com           (apex domain)
# ✅ www.anihpj.com       (www subdomain)
# ✅ api.anihpj.com       (API subdomain)
# ✅ admin.anihpj.com     (admin panel)
# ✅ staging.anihpj.com   (staging environment)
# ✅ [any].anihpj.com     (any first-level subdomain)
⚠️ Wildcard Limitation:

*.anihpj.com covers api.anihpj.com but NOT v2.api.anihpj.com (nested subdomains). Request separate wildcards: *.api.anihpj.com

📋 Subject Alternative Names (SAN)

Combine multiple domains in a single certificate (up to 10 SANs):

# Request certificate with multiple SANs:
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names \
        "www.anihpj.com" \
        "*.anihpj.com" \
        "anihpj.net" \
        "www.anihpj.net" \
        "anihpj.io" \
        "api.anihpj.io" \
    --validation-method DNS

# Single certificate protects all listed domains!
SAN Type Example Use Case
Apex Domain anihpj.com Main website
WWW Subdomain www.anihpj.com WWW redirect
Wildcard *.anihpj.com All subdomains
Alternative TLD anihpj.net Brand protection
API Domain api.anihpj.io API endpoint
🔍 Certificate Transparency

ACM automatically logs certificates to public CT logs for security and compliance:

✅ Benefits
  • Detect unauthorized certificates
  • Required by browsers (Chrome, Safari)
  • Audit trail for compliance
  • Industry best practice
⚠️ Considerations
  • Domain names become public
  • Cannot opt-out for public certs
  • Private CA certs not logged
  • Monitor CT logs for your domains
# Check CT logs for your domain:
# Visit: https://crt.sh/?q=anihpj.com

# Or use Certificate Transparency API:
curl "https://crt.sh/?q=anihpj.com&output=json" | jq '.[0:5]'
📥 Import Existing Certificates

Import third-party certificates (DigiCert, Let's Encrypt, etc.) to use with AWS services:

# Import certificate from files:
aws acm import-certificate \
    --certificate fileb://certificate.pem \
    --private-key fileb://private-key.pem \
    --certificate-chain fileb://certificate-chain.pem \
    --region us-east-1 \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Source,Value=external

# Import from Let's Encrypt:
aws acm import-certificate \
    --certificate fileb:///etc/letsencrypt/live/anihpj.com/cert.pem \
    --private-key fileb:///etc/letsencrypt/live/anihpj.com/privkey.pem \
    --certificate-chain fileb:///etc/letsencrypt/live/anihpj.com/chain.pem

# Re-import to update expiring certificate:
aws acm import-certificate \
    --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123... \
    --certificate fileb://new-certificate.pem \
    --private-key fileb://new-private-key.pem \
    --certificate-chain fileb://new-chain.pem
⚠️ Imported Certificate Limitations:
  • No automatic renewal - you must re-import before expiration
  • Private key required (ACM stores securely, but you provide it)
  • Set CloudWatch alarms for expiration monitoring
📤 Export Certificates (Private CA Only)
ℹ️ Note:

Only certificates issued by ACM Private CA can be exported. Public ACM certificates cannot be exported.

# Export private CA certificate (requires passphrase):
aws acm export-certificate \
    --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123... \
    --passphrase $(echo -n "YourSecurePassphrase" | base64) \
    --output json > exported-cert.json

# Parse exported certificate:
cat exported-cert.json | jq -r '.Certificate' > cert.pem
cat exported-cert.json | jq -r '.PrivateKey' > key.pem
cat exported-cert.json | jq -r '.CertificateChain' > chain.pem

# Use exported cert on non-AWS resources (on-premises, etc.)

🔐 Security & Best Practices

Implement enterprise-grade security for your anihpj certificate infrastructure.

✅ Security Checklist
🛡️ ACM Security Best Practices for anihpj
🔑 Certificate Security
  • ☑️ Use DNS validation (auto-renewal)
  • ☑️ Enable Certificate Transparency
  • ☑️ Use 2048-bit RSA or ECDSA keys
  • ☑️ Monitor CT logs for domain
  • ☑️ Set expiration CloudWatch alarms
🔒 TLS Configuration
  • ☑️ Use TLS 1.2+ (disable TLS 1.0/1.1)
  • ☑️ Use strong cipher suites
  • ☑️ Enable HSTS headers
  • ☑️ Implement HTTP→HTTPS redirect
  • ☑️ Use SNI for multiple domains
📊 Monitoring & Compliance
  • ☑️ CloudTrail logging enabled
  • ☑️ Config rules for compliance
  • ☑️ Regular certificate inventory
  • ☑️ Expiration alerts (30/14/7 days)
  • ☑️ Audit imported certificates
🔑 Key Management
🏗️ ACM Key Security Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         ACM KEY MANAGEMENT                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                      AWS Certificate Manager                         │    │
│  │  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │    │
│  │  │  Certificate    │  │  Private Key    │  │  Certificate Chain  │  │    │
│  │  │  (Public)       │  │  (Encrypted)    │  │  (Public)           │  │    │
│  │  └─────────────────┘  └────────┬────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │    │
│  └────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                   │                                          │
│                                   ▼                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                       AWS Key Management Service                     │    │
│  │                                                                      │    │
│  │  • Private keys encrypted with AWS-managed CMK                      │    │
│  │  • Keys never leave ACM (hardware security modules)                 │    │
│  │  • Cannot export public ACM certificate private keys                │    │
│  │  • Automatic key rotation with certificate renewal                   │    │
│  │                                                                      │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                   │                                          │
│                                   ▼                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                    Integrated AWS Services                           │    │
│  │                                                                      │    │
│  │  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌──────────┐  │    │
│  │  │   ALB   │  │CloudFront│ │API GW   │  │Elastic  │  │App Runner│  │    │
│  │  │         │  │         │  │         │  │Beanstalk│  │          │  │    │
│  │  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └──────────┘  │    │
│  │                                                                      │    │
│  │  Keys deployed securely - never exposed to users                    │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📋 Compliance & Standards
Compliance Standard ACM Coverage anihpj Relevance
PCI DSS ✅ TLS 1.2+, strong ciphers, audit logs Payment processing security
HIPAA ✅ Encryption in transit, key management Healthcare job postings
SOC 2 ✅ Security controls, monitoring, logging Enterprise customer requirements
GDPR ✅ Data encryption, privacy protection EU user data protection
ISO 27001 ✅ Information security management Security certification
📊 Monitoring & Alerts
# CloudWatch alarm for certificate expiration (30 days warning):
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "ACM-Certificate-Expiring-anihpj" \
    --alarm-description "ACM certificate expiring within 30 days" \
    --namespace "AWS/CertificateManager" \
    --metric-name "DaysToExpiry" \
    --dimensions Name=CertificateArn,Value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123 \
    --statistic Minimum \
    --period 86400 \
    --threshold 30 \
    --comparison-operator LessThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

# List certificates expiring within 90 days:
aws acm list-certificates \
    --query 'CertificateSummaryList[?NotAfter<=`2024-06-01`]' \
    --output table

# EventBridge rule for ACM events:
aws events put-rule \
    --name "ACM-Certificate-Events" \
    --event-pattern '{
        "source": ["aws.acm"],
        "detail-type": ["ACM Certificate Approaching Expiration"]
    }' \
    --state ENABLED
📧
SNS Alerts

Email/SMS notifications

🔔
EventBridge

Automated workflows

📊
CloudWatch

Metrics & dashboards

CloudTrail

Audit logging

🔄 Certificate Rotation Strategies

Best practices for managing certificate lifecycle and rotation in production.

✅ ACM Auto-Rotation (Recommended)
  • Use DNS validation exclusively
  • Keep CNAME records permanent
  • Monitor CloudWatch DaysToExpiry metric
  • Set up SNS alerts for failures
🔄 Imported Certificate Rotation
  • Track expiration dates manually
  • Request new cert 30+ days early
  • Re-import to same ARN (zero downtime)
  • Automate with Lambda + EventBridge
⚠️ Private CA Rotation
  • Plan CA hierarchy carefully
  • Use subordinate CAs for flexibility
  • Implement CRL/OCSP for revocation
  • Test rotation in staging first
# Automated rotation check script:
# anihpj/management/commands/check_cert_rotation.py

import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def check_certificates():
    acm = boto3.client('acm', region_name='us-east-1')
    certs = acm.list_certificates()['CertificateSummaryList']
    
    for cert in certs:
        details = acm.describe_certificate(
            CertificateArn=cert['CertificateArn']
        )['Certificate']
        
        if details.get('NotAfter'):
            days_left = (details['NotAfter'].replace(tzinfo=None) - 
                        datetime.utcnow()).days
            
            if days_left < 30:
                print(f"⚠️ {details['DomainName']} expires in {days_left} days!")
            elif days_left < 60:
                print(f"🔔 {details['DomainName']} renewal expected soon ({days_left} days)")

Component Price Notes
Private CA Operation $400/month Per CA, prorated daily
Certificates (General Use) $0.75/cert First 1,000 certs/month
Certificates (High Volume) $0.35/cert 1,001 - 10,000 certs
Certificates (Bulk) $0.001/cert 10,001+ certs
Short-Lived Certs (7 days) $0.058/cert IoT/ephemeral use cases
💡 Recommendation for anihpj:

Use FREE public certificates unless you need internal PKI, IoT device certs, or compliance requiring private CA.

📉 Cost Optimization
✅ Free Strategies
  • Use public ACM certs (always free)
  • Consolidate with wildcard certs
  • Use SAN certs for multiple domains
  • Leverage DNS validation (auto-renew)
  • Clean up unused certificates
⚠️ Cost Traps to Avoid
  • Private CA when not needed
  • Separate certs per subdomain
  • Forgetting to delete test CAs
  • Over-provisioning short-lived certs
  • Running unused Private CAs

🐍 Django Integration

Configure your anihpj Django application for HTTPS behind ALB:

# anihpj/settings.py - HTTPS Configuration

# 1. Trust ALB forwarded headers
USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST = True
SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO', 'https')

# 2. Security settings for production
SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True  # Redirect HTTP to HTTPS
SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000  # 1 year HSTS
SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True
SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = True

# 3. Cookie security
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True
SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True

# 4. Content security
SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF = True
SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER = True
X_FRAME_OPTIONS = 'DENY'

# 5. Allowed hosts
ALLOWED_HOSTS = [
    'anihpj.com',
    'www.anihpj.com',
    '*.anihpj.com',
    # Include ALB DNS for health checks
    '.elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com',
]

# 6. CSRF trusted origins (Django 4.0+)
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = [
    'https://anihpj.com',
    'https://www.anihpj.com',
    'https://*.anihpj.com',
]
📋 Integration Best Practices
✅ DO
  • Use wildcard for all subdomains
  • Request in us-east-1 for CloudFront
  • Enable DNS validation
  • Configure HSTS headers
  • Set up monitoring alerts
  • Use SNI for multiple domains
❌ DON'T
  • Use email validation (no auto-renew)
  • Forget CloudFront us-east-1 rule
  • Skip HTTPS redirect
  • Use weak TLS protocols
  • Ignore certificate expiration
  • Mix HTTP and HTTPS content

💼 Use Cases

Real-world implementation scenarios for anihpj job portal.

🌐 Web Portal HTTPS
Scenario: Secure Job Portal Website

Enable HTTPS for anihpj.com main website with wildcard coverage.

# Complete setup for anihpj web portal:

# 1. Request wildcard certificate
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" "www.anihpj.com" \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1

# 2. Create DNS validation record in Route 53
# (Get CNAME from describe-certificate output)

# 3. Attach to ALB HTTPS listener
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN \
    --protocol HTTPS --port 443 \
    --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=$CERT_ARN \
    --default-actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$TG_ARN

# 4. Add HTTP to HTTPS redirect
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN \
    --protocol HTTP --port 80 \
    --default-actions Type=redirect,RedirectConfig='{Protocol=HTTPS,Port=443,StatusCode=HTTP_301}'
🔒
HTTPS Enabled
🔄
Auto-Renewal
🆓
$0 Cost
🔐 API Security
Scenario: Secure REST API with Custom Domain

Set up api.anihpj.com with HTTPS for job posting API endpoints.

# API Gateway with ACM certificate:

# 1. Certificate already covers api.anihpj.com via wildcard
# Or request specific certificate:
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1

# 2. Create custom domain in API Gateway
aws apigateway create-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --regional-certificate-arn $CERT_ARN \
    --endpoint-configuration types=REGIONAL \
    --security-policy TLS_1_2

# 3. Create base path mapping
aws apigateway create-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --rest-api-id $API_ID \
    --stage production \
    --base-path v1

# 4. Add Route 53 record pointing to API Gateway
# Use the target domain name from create-domain-name response
🌍 Multi-Domain Setup
Scenario: Multiple Domains on Single Certificate

Protect anihpj.com, anihpj.net, anihpj.io with one SAN certificate.

# Multi-domain SAN certificate:
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names \
        "www.anihpj.com" \
        "*.anihpj.com" \
        "anihpj.net" \
        "www.anihpj.net" \
        "*.anihpj.net" \
        "anihpj.io" \
        "www.anihpj.io" \
        "*.anihpj.io" \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1

# Note: You'll need DNS validation records for EACH domain
# ACM will provide a CNAME for each domain that needs validation
Domain Coverage Purpose
anihpj.com ✅ Apex + wildcard Primary domain
anihpj.net ✅ Apex + wildcard Alternative TLD
anihpj.io ✅ Apex + wildcard Developer/API domain

☁️ Hybrid Cloud SSL Strategy
Scenario: SSL for On-Premises + AWS Hybrid Environment

Implement consistent SSL/TLS security across hybrid cloud infrastructure.

☁️ AWS Resources (ACM)
  • ALB/NLB - ACM public certificates
  • CloudFront - ACM (us-east-1)
  • API Gateway - ACM custom domains
  • Internal services - ACM Private CA
🏢 On-Premises Resources
  • Import ACM Private CA certs
  • Use Let's Encrypt for web servers
  • Enterprise CA integration
  • Hardware Security Modules (HSM)

💡 Hybrid SSL Best Practices:
  • Use ACM Private CA for consistent internal trust across environments
  • Implement certificate lifecycle automation on both sides
  • Centralize certificate monitoring with CloudWatch + on-prem tools
  • Use AWS Systems Manager to push certs to hybrid instances

🔬 Advanced Topics

Deep dive into advanced ACM features and configurations.

🏢 Private Certificate Authority
⚠️
Private CA Costs $400/month

Only use for internal PKI, IoT devices, or compliance requirements

# Create Private CA (Enterprise use only):
aws acm-pca create-certificate-authority \
    --certificate-authority-configuration '{
        "KeyAlgorithm": "RSA_2048",
        "SigningAlgorithm": "SHA256WITHRSA",
        "Subject": {
            "Country": "US",
            "Organization": "AnihPJ Inc",
            "OrganizationalUnit": "Engineering",
            "CommonName": "AnihPJ Internal CA"
        }
    }' \
    --certificate-authority-type ROOT \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj

# Issue private certificate:
aws acm-pca issue-certificate \
    --certificate-authority-arn $CA_ARN \
    --csr fileb://server.csr \
    --signing-algorithm SHA256WITHRSA \
    --validity Value=365,Type=DAYS
📌 Certificate Pinning
⚠️ Warning:

Certificate pinning is generally NOT recommended with ACM due to automatic rotation. Use alternative approaches.

✅ Better Alternatives
  • Use Certificate Transparency monitoring
  • Trust Amazon root CAs only
  • Implement proper TLS verification
  • Use CAA DNS records
⚠️ If You Must Pin
  • Pin to Amazon root CA (not leaf)
  • Include backup pins
  • Plan for rotation failures
  • Use short HPKP max-age
🤖 Automation with boto3
# anihpj/utils/acm_manager.py
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class ACMManager:
    """ACM certificate management for anihpj"""
    
    def __init__(self, region='us-east-1'):
        self.client = boto3.client('acm', region_name=region)
    
    def request_certificate(self, domain, sans=None, validation='DNS'):
        """Request new ACM certificate"""
        params = {
            'DomainName': domain,
            'ValidationMethod': validation,
            'Tags': [
                {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                {'Key': 'ManagedBy', 'Value': 'boto3'}
            ]
        }
        if sans:
            params['SubjectAlternativeNames'] = sans
        
        response = self.client.request_certificate(**params)
        return response['CertificateArn']
    
    def get_expiring_certificates(self, days=30):
        """Find certificates expiring within N days"""
        expiring = []
        paginator = self.client.get_paginator('list_certificates')
        
        for page in paginator.paginate():
            for cert_summary in page['CertificateSummaryList']:
                cert = self.client.describe_certificate(
                    CertificateArn=cert_summary['CertificateArn']
                )['Certificate']
                
                if cert.get('NotAfter'):
                    days_until_expiry = (cert['NotAfter'].replace(tzinfo=None) - 
                                        datetime.utcnow()).days
                    if days_until_expiry <= days:
                        expiring.append({
                            'arn': cert['CertificateArn'],
                            'domain': cert['DomainName'],
                            'expires_in_days': days_until_expiry,
                            'status': cert['Status']
                        })
        return expiring
    
    def get_validation_records(self, certificate_arn):
        """Get DNS validation records for certificate"""
        cert = self.client.describe_certificate(
            CertificateArn=certificate_arn
        )['Certificate']
        
        records = []
        for dvo in cert.get('DomainValidationOptions', []):
            if 'ResourceRecord' in dvo:
                records.append({
                    'domain': dvo['DomainName'],
                    'name': dvo['ResourceRecord']['Name'],
                    'type': dvo['ResourceRecord']['Type'],
                    'value': dvo['ResourceRecord']['Value']
                })
        return records

# Usage example:
acm = ACMManager()
cert_arn = acm.request_certificate(
    domain='anihpj.com',
    sans=['*.anihpj.com', 'www.anihpj.com']
)
print(f"Certificate ARN: {cert_arn}")
🔧 Troubleshooting Guide
Issue Cause Solution
Certificate stuck in PENDING DNS validation record not found Verify CNAME record exists, check DNS propagation with dig
Validation timeout 72 hours passed without validation Request new certificate, add DNS record immediately
Certificate not renewing DNS validation CNAME removed Keep CNAME records permanent, don't delete after issuance
CloudFront cert error Certificate not in us-east-1 Request certificate in us-east-1 (N. Virginia)
ALB HTTPS not working Security group blocking 443 Add inbound rule for port 443 from 0.0.0.0/0
ERR_CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID Domain not in certificate SANs Add domain to certificate SANs or use wildcard
Mixed content warnings HTTP resources on HTTPS page Update all URLs to HTTPS or use protocol-relative
Certificate expired Email validation used (no auto-renew) Switch to DNS validation, request new cert
📦 Migration from Other Providers
# Migration from Let's Encrypt to ACM:

# Option 1: Import existing Let's Encrypt cert (temporary)
aws acm import-certificate \
    --certificate fileb:///etc/letsencrypt/live/anihpj.com/cert.pem \
    --private-key fileb:///etc/letsencrypt/live/anihpj.com/privkey.pem \
    --certificate-chain fileb:///etc/letsencrypt/live/anihpj.com/chain.pem

# Option 2: Request new ACM certificate (recommended)
# 1. Request ACM cert
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" \
    --validation-method DNS

# 2. Add DNS validation record

# 3. Wait for ISSUED status

# 4. Update ALB/CloudFront to use new ACM cert
aws elbv2 modify-listener \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --certificates CertificateArn=$NEW_ACM_CERT_ARN

# 5. Remove Let's Encrypt renewal cron jobs
# 6. Delete imported certificate after confirming ACM works

⚡ Quick Reference: AWS CLI Commands for ACM

📝 Certificate Management
# Request certificate
aws acm request-certificate --domain-name anihpj.com \
  --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" \
  --validation-method DNS

# List certificates
aws acm list-certificates --output table

# Describe certificate
aws acm describe-certificate --certificate-arn $ARN

# Delete certificate
aws acm delete-certificate --certificate-arn $ARN

# Get certificate (public info)
aws acm get-certificate --certificate-arn $ARN
🔄 Import & Export
# Import certificate
aws acm import-certificate \
  --certificate fileb://cert.pem \
  --private-key fileb://key.pem \
  --certificate-chain fileb://chain.pem

# Re-import (update existing)
aws acm import-certificate \
  --certificate-arn $ARN \
  --certificate fileb://new-cert.pem \
  --private-key fileb://new-key.pem

# Export (Private CA only)
aws acm export-certificate --certificate-arn $ARN \
  --passphrase $(echo -n "pass" | base64)
📧 Validation
# Resend validation email
aws acm resend-validation-email \
  --certificate-arn $ARN \
  --domain anihpj.com \
  --validation-domain anihpj.com

# Get validation DNS records
aws acm describe-certificate --certificate-arn $ARN \
  --query 'Certificate.DomainValidationOptions'
🏷️ Tags & Monitoring
# Add tags
aws acm add-tags-to-certificate \
  --certificate-arn $ARN \
  --tags Key=Env,Value=prod

# List tags
aws acm list-tags-for-certificate \
  --certificate-arn $ARN

# Remove tags
aws acm remove-tags-from-certificate \
  --certificate-arn $ARN \
  --tags Key=Env

✅ ACM Production Checklist for anihpj

📜 Certificate Setup
  • ☐ Request wildcard certificate (*.anihpj.com)
  • ☐ Use DNS validation method
  • ☐ Add CNAME validation record to Route 53
  • ☐ Verify certificate status = ISSUED
  • ☐ Request in us-east-1 if using CloudFront
⚖️ Load Balancer
  • ☐ Create HTTPS listener (port 443)
  • ☐ Attach ACM certificate to listener
  • ☐ Set TLS policy (TLSv1.2 minimum)
  • ☐ Add HTTP→HTTPS redirect
  • ☐ Open port 443 in security group
🐍 Django Configuration
  • ☐ Set SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER
  • ☐ Enable SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT
  • ☐ Configure HSTS settings
  • ☐ Set SESSION/CSRF cookies secure
  • ☐ Add CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS
📊 Monitoring
  • ☐ CloudWatch expiration alarm (30 days)
  • ☐ SNS alert for certificate events
  • ☐ CloudTrail logging enabled
  • ☐ Monitor CT logs for domain
  • ☐ Keep DNS validation CNAME permanent

🔧 Troubleshooting

Comprehensive guide to diagnosing and resolving ACM certificate issues.

⚠️ Common Issues
Issue Cause Solution
Certificate stuck in PENDING DNS validation record not found Verify CNAME record exists, check DNS propagation with dig
Validation timeout (72 hours) Validation record not added in time Request new certificate, add DNS record immediately
Certificate not renewing DNS validation CNAME removed Keep CNAME records permanent, never delete after issuance
CloudFront cert error Certificate not in us-east-1 Request certificate in us-east-1 (N. Virginia) region
ALB HTTPS not working Security group blocking 443 Add inbound rule for port 443 from 0.0.0.0/0
ERR_CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID Domain not in certificate SANs Add domain to certificate SANs or use wildcard
Mixed content warnings HTTP resources on HTTPS page Update all URLs to HTTPS or use protocol-relative URLs
Certificate expired despite ACM Email validation was used (no auto-renew) Switch to DNS validation, request new certificate
❌ Validation Errors
DNS Validation Failures
  • CNAME not found: Check exact record name and value
  • Propagation delay: Wait 10-30 minutes, use dig to verify
  • Wrong record type: Must be CNAME, not TXT or A
  • Apex domain issue: Some DNS providers don't support CNAME at apex
Email Validation Failures
  • Email not received: Check spam, verify WHOIS email
  • Link expired: Request resend within 72 hours
  • Domain not owned: Email goes to admin@, webmaster@, etc.
  • MX records missing: Ensure domain has valid MX records
# Debug DNS validation:
# 1. Get validation record details
aws acm describe-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN \
    --query 'Certificate.DomainValidationOptions[*].ResourceRecord'

# 2. Verify CNAME record exists
dig CNAME _abc123.anihpj.com +short

# 3. Check authoritative nameservers
dig NS anihpj.com +short
dig @ns1.example.com CNAME _abc123.anihpj.com

# 4. Force DNS refresh
dig CNAME _abc123.anihpj.com @8.8.8.8 +trace
🐛 Debugging Strategies
# anihpj/management/commands/debug_acm.py
import boto3
import ssl
import socket
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Debug ACM certificate issues'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        # Check certificate status
        acm = boto3.client('acm', region_name='us-east-1')
        certs = acm.list_certificates()['CertificateSummaryList']
        
        for cert in certs:
            details = acm.describe_certificate(
                CertificateArn=cert['CertificateArn']
            )['Certificate']
            
            self.stdout.write(f"\n{'='*60}")
            self.stdout.write(f"Domain: {details['DomainName']}")
            self.stdout.write(f"Status: {details['Status']}")
            self.stdout.write(f"Type: {details.get('Type', 'N/A')}")
            
            if details['Status'] == 'PENDING_VALIDATION':
                self.stdout.write(self.style.WARNING('⚠️ Pending Validation'))
                for dvo in details.get('DomainValidationOptions', []):
                    self.stdout.write(f"  Domain: {dvo['DomainName']}")
                    self.stdout.write(f"  Status: {dvo['ValidationStatus']}")
                    if 'ResourceRecord' in dvo:
                        rr = dvo['ResourceRecord']
                        self.stdout.write(f"  CNAME: {rr['Name']} -> {rr['Value']}")
            
            elif details['Status'] == 'ISSUED':
                self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS('✅ Issued'))
                not_after = details.get('NotAfter')
                if not_after:
                    self.stdout.write(f"  Expires: {not_after}")
        
        # Test HTTPS connection
        self.stdout.write(f"\n{'='*60}")
        self.stdout.write("Testing HTTPS connection to anihpj.com...")
        try:
            ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
            with socket.create_connection(('anihpj.com', 443)) as sock:
                with ctx.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname='anihpj.com') as ssock:
                    cert = ssock.getpeercert()
                    self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS('✅ HTTPS working'))
                    self.stdout.write(f"  Subject: {cert['subject']}")
                    self.stdout.write(f"  Issuer: {cert['issuer']}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR(f'❌ HTTPS failed: {e}'))

# Run with: python manage.py debug_acm

⌨️ API & CLI Reference

Complete reference for ACM command-line interface and SDK operations.

🖥️ AWS CLI Commands
# Certificate Request Operations
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names "*.anihpj.com" "www.anihpj.com" \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --idempotency-token unique-token-123 \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

# List & Describe Certificates
aws acm list-certificates --certificate-statuses ISSUED PENDING_VALIDATION
aws acm list-certificates --includes keyTypes=RSA_2048,EC_prime256v1
aws acm describe-certificate --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789:certificate/abc123
aws acm get-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN

# Certificate Management
aws acm delete-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN
aws acm renew-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN
aws acm update-certificate-options --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN \
    --options CertificateTransparencyLoggingPreference=ENABLED

# Import External Certificate
aws acm import-certificate \
    --certificate fileb://certificate.pem \
    --private-key fileb://private-key.pem \
    --certificate-chain fileb://certificate-chain.pem \
    --tags Key=Source,Value=External

# Validation Operations
aws acm resend-validation-email \
    --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN \
    --domain anihpj.com \
    --validation-domain anihpj.com

# Tag Management
aws acm add-tags-to-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN \
    --tags Key=CostCenter,Value=Engineering
aws acm list-tags-for-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN
aws acm remove-tags-from-certificate --certificate-arn $CERT_ARN \
    --tags Key=CostCenter
🐍 Boto3 SDK
# anihpj/utils/acm_sdk.py
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

class ACMClient:
    """Complete ACM SDK wrapper for anihpj"""
    
    def __init__(self, region='us-east-1'):
        self.client = boto3.client('acm', region_name=region)
    
    def request_certificate(self, domain, sans=None, validation='DNS'):
        """Request new ACM certificate"""
        try:
            params = {
                'DomainName': domain,
                'ValidationMethod': validation,
                'Options': {
                    'CertificateTransparencyLoggingPreference': 'ENABLED'
                },
                'Tags': [
                    {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                    {'Key': 'ManagedBy', 'Value': 'boto3'}
                ]
            }
            if sans:
                params['SubjectAlternativeNames'] = [domain] + sans
            
            response = self.client.request_certificate(**params)
            return {'success': True, 'arn': response['CertificateArn']}
        except ClientError as e:
            return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)}
    
    def list_certificates(self, status_filter=None):
        """List all certificates with optional status filter"""
        params = {}
        if status_filter:
            params['CertificateStatuses'] = status_filter
        
        paginator = self.client.get_paginator('list_certificates')
        certificates = []
        for page in paginator.paginate(**params):
            certificates.extend(page['CertificateSummaryList'])
        return certificates
    
    def describe_certificate(self, arn):
        """Get detailed certificate information"""
        try:
            response = self.client.describe_certificate(CertificateArn=arn)
            return response['Certificate']
        except ClientError as e:
            return {'error': str(e)}
    
    def import_certificate(self, cert_path, key_path, chain_path=None):
        """Import external certificate"""
        try:
            with open(cert_path, 'rb') as f:
                cert = f.read()
            with open(key_path, 'rb') as f:
                key = f.read()
            
            params = {
                'Certificate': cert,
                'PrivateKey': key,
                'Tags': [{'Key': 'Source', 'Value': 'Imported'}]
            }
            if chain_path:
                with open(chain_path, 'rb') as f:
                    params['CertificateChain'] = f.read()
            
            response = self.client.import_certificate(**params)
            return {'success': True, 'arn': response['CertificateArn']}
        except (ClientError, IOError) as e:
            return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)}
    
    def delete_certificate(self, arn):
        """Delete a certificate"""
        try:
            self.client.delete_certificate(CertificateArn=arn)
            return {'success': True}
        except ClientError as e:
            return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)}

# Usage
acm = ACMClient()
result = acm.request_certificate('anihpj.com', sans=['*.anihpj.com'])
if result['success']:
    print(f"Certificate ARN: {result['arn']}")
📦 CloudFormation Templates
# cloudformation/acm-certificate.yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: ACM Certificate for anihpj

Parameters:
  DomainName:
    Type: String
    Default: anihpj.com
    Description: Primary domain name
  
  HostedZoneId:
    Type: AWS::Route53::HostedZone::Id
    Description: Route 53 hosted zone ID

Resources:
  Certificate:
    Type: AWS::CertificateManager::Certificate
    Properties:
      DomainName: !Ref DomainName
      SubjectAlternativeNames:
        - !Sub '*.${DomainName}'
        - !Sub 'www.${DomainName}'
      ValidationMethod: DNS
      DomainValidationOptions:
        - DomainName: !Ref DomainName
          HostedZoneId: !Ref HostedZoneId
      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          Value: anihpj
        - Key: Environment
          Value: production
  
  # CloudFront Certificate (must be us-east-1)
  CloudFrontCertificate:
    Type: AWS::CertificateManager::Certificate
    Condition: CreateCloudFrontCert
    Properties:
      DomainName: !Ref DomainName
      SubjectAlternativeNames:
        - !Sub '*.${DomainName}'
      ValidationMethod: DNS

Outputs:
  CertificateArn:
    Description: ACM Certificate ARN
    Value: !Ref Certificate
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-CertificateArn'
🏗️ Terraform Configuration
# terraform/acm.tf
# ACM Certificate for anihpj

variable "domain_name" {
  default = "anihpj.com"
}

variable "zone_id" {
  description = "Route 53 Hosted Zone ID"
}

# Request ACM Certificate
resource "aws_acm_certificate" "main" {
  domain_name               = var.domain_name
  subject_alternative_names = ["*.${var.domain_name}", "www.${var.domain_name}"]
  validation_method         = "DNS"
  
  options {
    certificate_transparency_logging_preference = "ENABLED"
  }
  
  tags = {
    Project     = "anihpj"
    Environment = "production"
    ManagedBy   = "terraform"
  }
  
  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }
}

# DNS Validation Records
resource "aws_route53_record" "cert_validation" {
  for_each = {
    for dvo in aws_acm_certificate.main.domain_validation_options : dvo.domain_name => {
      name   = dvo.resource_record_name
      record = dvo.resource_record_value
      type   = dvo.resource_record_type
    }
  }
  
  zone_id         = var.zone_id
  name            = each.value.name
  type            = each.value.type
  records         = [each.value.record]
  ttl             = 60
  allow_overwrite = true
}

# Certificate Validation
resource "aws_acm_certificate_validation" "main" {
  certificate_arn         = aws_acm_certificate.main.arn
  validation_record_fqdns = [for record in aws_route53_record.cert_validation : record.fqdn]
}

# Output
output "certificate_arn" {
  value       = aws_acm_certificate.main.arn
  description = "ACM Certificate ARN"
}

output "certificate_status" {
  value       = aws_acm_certificate.main.status
  description = "Certificate validation status"
}

📚 Resources & Glossary

Essential documentation links, terminology, and frequently asked questions.

📝 ACM Glossary
SSL (Secure Sockets Layer)

Deprecated protocol for encrypted communication. Replaced by TLS.

TLS (Transport Layer Security)

Current standard for encrypted communication (TLS 1.2, 1.3).

CA (Certificate Authority)

Trusted entity that issues digital certificates (Amazon, DigiCert, Let's Encrypt).

CSR (Certificate Signing Request)

Encoded file with public key and domain info sent to CA for certificate issuance.

SAN (Subject Alternative Name)

Extension allowing multiple domains on single certificate (up to 10 in ACM).

Wildcard Certificate

Certificate covering all subdomains (*.anihpj.com) but not the apex.

CT (Certificate Transparency)

Public log of all issued certificates for monitoring and security.

HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security)

HTTP header forcing browsers to use HTTPS for all future requests.

Private CA

ACM feature to create internal certificate authority ($400/month).

Domain Validation (DV)

Basic validation proving domain ownership via DNS or email.

❓ Frequently Asked Questions
Is ACM really free?

Yes! Public certificates for use with AWS services (ALB, CloudFront, API Gateway) are completely free. You only pay for the AWS resources using them. Private CA costs $400/month.

Can I use ACM certificates on EC2 instances?

Not directly. ACM certificates cannot be exported to EC2. Use ACM with ALB in front of EC2, or use Let's Encrypt/Certbot for direct EC2 HTTPS.

Why is my certificate stuck in PENDING_VALIDATION?

The DNS CNAME validation record hasn't been detected. Verify the exact record name and value, allow 10-30 minutes for propagation, and use dig to confirm the record exists.

Do I need separate certificates for each AWS region?

Yes, for regional services like ALB. CloudFront is special—it requires certificates in us-east-1 only, regardless of where your origins are.

How do I renew an ACM certificate?

ACM auto-renews certificates ~60 days before expiration if DNS validation records exist. Keep CNAME records permanent. Email-validated certificates require manual action.

Can I use one certificate for multiple domains?

Yes! ACM supports up to 10 SANs (Subject Alternative Names) per certificate. Add multiple domains during request or use wildcard (*.domain.com).

Should I use DNS or email validation?

Always use DNS validation when possible. It enables automatic renewal and is more reliable. Email validation requires manual action for every renewal.

Your anihpj job portal now has enterprise-grade SSL/TLS security

HTTPS Enabled
